 * Develop the go-to-market strategy: driving customer acquisition, managing the entire sales process, building a strong partner network.
   
   

Face to Market & Strategic Relationship Building

Market Analysis & Performance Reporting

Your profile


 * You have a degree in Business Administration, Management, Entrepreneurship or comparable
 * You see your strengths in the area of Business Development, Sales and Networking
 * You bring at least 10 years of Sales experience and ideally have already built and scaled digital B2B business models or successfully acquired customers in the SaaS sector. A strong network in the automotive industry is a plus.
 * Ideally, you have already successfully built and scaled digital B2B Business Models and acquired customers in the SaaS sector and/or bring a proven track record in Sales
 * You are flexible, able to adapt quickly and have a high degree of independence
 * You are business fluent in Spanish and English and keen to work in a fast-paced environment
 * You are willing to travel frequently for business across Spain and you hold a valid driver’s license
   
   

Why us?


 * The opportunity to be part of one of the most exciting FinTech Scale-Up stories in Europe

 * An open and transparent corporate culture where your ideas and suggestions are always heard

About Us

We, Aufinity, are a Cologne-based FinTech Scaleup and pioneer for digital payment solutions in the automotive sector. In 2018, we created bezahl.de, the first all-in-one platform for digital payment management in the automotive trade. With our product, we optimize internal payment processes and help our customers save valuable time and costs in the back office. At the same time, we enable car dealership groups to offer a fully digital payment process with various payment methods - including specialized payment methods for large sums - for the first time.

title: Director of Strategy & Planning
description:
BrainRocket is a global company creating end-to-end tech products for clients across Fintech, iGaming, and Marketing. Young, ambitious, and unstoppable, we've already taken Cyprus, Malta, Portugal, Poland, and Serbia by storm. Our BRO team consists of 1,300 bright minds creating innovative ideas and products. We don’t follow formats. We shape them. We build what works, launch it fast, and make sure it hits.

The Director of Strategy and Planning will be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining a robust OKR framework that aligns all departments with the company’s strategic goals. This role will work closely with C-level executives and department heads to ensure that goals are effectively set, tracked, and adjusted, creating a unified direction for the organization. Director of Strategy & Planning will serve as the "architect of efficiency" for our global operations. This person will be the primary navigator for the company’s strategic roadmap, ensuring that our vision is translated into actionable, measurable, and highly structured plans.

We are looking for a "Human-Algorithm": someone who thinks in logic, structures, and processes, but possesses the high emotional intelligence (EQ) required to influence the Board and senior leadership. You will bridge the gap between high-level ambition and operational execution within the iGaming ecosystem.




Responsibilities

 * Collaborate with C-level executives and department heads to define and refine the company’s strategic objectives
 * Ensure alignment with the overall vision across all departments
 * Provide direction across departments to cascade high-level goals effectively
 * Offer guidance and support to maintain cohesive, aligned objectives throughout the organization
 * Design a clear, standardized goal-setting framework across all departments. Implement templates, guidelines, and review timelines for consistency
 * Build and oversee a reporting system to track progress on key goals
 * Deliver regular updates, insights, and recommendations to executives based on tracked performance. Lead change management initiatives to support framework adoption across the organization
 * Conduct workshops to reinforce continuous alignment and effectiveness in goal-setting and performance tracking




Requirements

title: District Operations Manager - Madrid
companyName: Centauro Rent a Car - Alquiler de coches
description:
Centauro Rent a Car is a company dedicated to the vehicle rental sector with more than 50 years of experience in the industry. Our headquarters are located in Alicante (Spain), and we operate in the most popular tourist destinations across Southern Europe and the Mediterranean coast: Spain, the Balearic Islands, Portugal, Madeira, Italy, Sardinia, Sicily, mainland Greece, and the Greek islands.




From our Operations Department, we are seeking a proactive, dynamic, and highly results-driven District Operations Manager to lead our business in Madrid. This role is key to ensuring operational excellence across our branches, driving commercial performance, strengthening team leadership, and delivering a best-in-class customer experience. The selected candidate will oversee day-to-day operations, ensure compliance with company standards, optimise resources, and collaborate closely with Headquarters and cross-functional teams to support sustainable growth in one of our most strategic regions.




The main responsibilities are:

 * Coordinate and supervise all the operational actions of the offices of its area.
 * Supervise the staff of its area, in direct contact with Branch Managers.
 * Achieve the annual goals set by the company. Including customer service and sales targets.
 * Promote and achieve the correct performance of the operational quality system of its area, as well as its distribution among all staff.
 * Monitor the condition, appearance and maintenance of the facilities.
 * Supervise any kind of building work associated with the remodeling of the office or any repairs that need to be made to the offices or garages, as well as obtaining corresponding estimates.
 * Identify and notify any suggestions for the improvement of the day-to-day operation of its area and for the Company in general.
 * Coordinate the work with suppliers and providers of each branch.
 * Maintain good relationship and attend meetings set out by the airports authorities of its area.




Minimum requirements:

 * At least 5 years of experience in managerial positions within the Rent a Car business.
 * Experience managing and coordinating different work teams.
 * Very good knowledge of the RAC business and its operations.
 * Commercial vocation and customer orientation.
 * Advanced level of Office Automation.
 * Fluent English.
 * Time availability.
 * Availability to travel.




What do we offer?

 * Full time position.
 * Fixed salary plus variable salary based on incentives and commissions.
 * Company car and phone.
 * Be part of a stable and growing company.




Are you looking for a new challenge? If you think your profile is suitable and you would like to be part of Centauro team, sign up for this offer!

title: Head of Key Accounts - Spain
description:
At Too Good To Go, we dream of a world where all produced food is consumed.

We dream of a world where we all - producers, retailers, workers in the hospitality industry, consumers, and policymakers- join forces to create a common solution to tackle food waste-head on..

In a very short time, we’ve become one of Europe’s most promising impact businesses. We’re now active in 20 countries and have built a community of over 133 million people.

We still have big plans to make the world a better place—one saved meal at a time. And, specifically, in Spain we want to drive our mission forward - which is why we’re now looking for a highly motivated Head of Key Accounts to help us in the fight against food waste!

If you want to be part of our fantastic team and are looking for a meaningful role, read on!

What The Role Is About

As Head of Key Accounts , you will contribute to Too Good To Go’s growth by defining and driving the national Key Account strategy for Spain. You’ll directly lead and grow the Key Account team - managing both acquisition and the development of existing partnerships across all of our Key Account Segments such as supermarkets, bakeries, hotel chains and more.

You’ll be part of the management team and report directly to the Country Director. You’ll be responsible for setting the strategy, prioritising efforts, building and sustaining a high-performing team, driving cross-functional collaboration, and achieving our joint goals for Key Accounts. Additionally, you’ll be personally involved in acquiring and developing our most important key partners.

Team Leadership


 * Lead the Key Account team with the goal of involving all major partners in Spain in our mission to fight food waste
 * Build and develop a productive, efficient, and independent Key Account team: using your deep knowledge of sales and negotiation techniques, you’ll continuously coach and support your team so that each member can reach their full potential and deliver top results
 * Play an active role in the overall development of the business and be a key leader beyond just Key Accounts - Leading by examples and making necessary business-decisions in order to keep us moving forward.
 * Manage the Key Account team, including 3 direct reports across three verticals.
   
   

Strategy, Partner Relationships & Performance


 * Build and grow partnerships with the largest national food retailers and HORECA operators
 * Structure and coordinate the rollout of new key accounts and drive Spain’s key account acquisition strategy
 * Conduct benchmarking and market analysis to identify key account strategies
 * Launch new initiatives to expand our Key Account business—focus especially on finding new ways to rescue more food with our partners and scale global key account chains
 * Strategically develop existing accounts
 * Own the revenue, budget, and operating results for the Key Account department
   
   

Who We’re Looking For


 * You bring extensive Key Account experience and have a strong background having worked and negotiated with national accounts — you’ll use this to lead your team by example
 * You are an experienced, motivating leader with a deep understanding of both scale-up dynamics, affinity in coaching and managing a Key Account team that navigates through shifting priorities and complex partners negotiations.
 * You know how to thrive in a matrix organization with direct and dotted-line reporting, and you’re excellent at managing both internal and external stakeholders
 * You have a proven track record of acquiring major key accounts, especially in grocery retail, but also HORECA
 * Being data driven is a natural strength of yours, you work with KPIs to track results, manage your team and refine Key Account strategy. Proven experience with common sales tools (ideally Salesforce) is a bonus!
 * You’re a bold, big-picture thinker with a passion for clear communication. You don’t just spot opportunities — you chase them down and thrive on turning ideas into impactful action.
 * Your team would describe you as strategic, results-oriented, hands-on, and resilient
 * You have strong negotiation skills at C-level
 * You’re fluent in both Spanish and English
 * You live in Madrid and are available to join us in the office a few days a week
 * You believe in our mission, live and love sustainability, and want a job where you can have a positive impact
   
   

Work Flexibily


 * Enjoy hybrid working from our great offices, at home or abroad
 * Extra parental leave and days off beyond local legislation and the option to take an extra week of unpaid leave
 * Health insurance
 * Additional days off for significant life events
   
   

Celebrate & Socialise


 * Regular social events like summer and winter parties
 * Coffee, snacks and fully-equipped kitchens
 * Get to know our community with an annual €50 to use on the app.
 * Paid volunteer time through our Shareback volunteering programme
 * Women in the Workplace, P.R.I.D.E., People of Colour and Functionally Diverse Employee Resource Groups
   
   

Local Benefits


 * Flexible working hours during summer months.
 * Technical equipment
 * Internet contribution for at home days.
   
   

Our Values


 * We win together
 * We raise the bar
 * We keep it simple
 * We build a legacy
 * We care
   
   

How To Apply


 * We take recruitment very seriously, so please carefully read everything we have written above. Please also check our website and international media in order to get a good overview of Too Good To Go
 * Submit your CV and Cover letter in English.
   
   

A Movement for Everyone

We want to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together. With that mission, it’s only natural that we want to build a diverse and inclusive team of highly capable individuals who are passionate about doing things in a better way. We strongly believe we all excel and are more creative when we’re allowed to be ourselves, and we’re committed to a culture where all of us belong.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need. If you need reasonable accommodation at any point in the application or interview process, please let us know.

description:
De cara a la próxima apertura del nuevo establecimiento Hotel Elba Corralejo Resort & Spa ubicado en el corazón de Corralejo (Fuerteventura), buscamos RESPONSABLE DE MANTENIMIENTO.




Se trata de un novedoso y fantástico resort diseñado para desconectar que proporciona formas diferentes de vivir las vacaciones tanto para familias como para adultos que cuenta con 478habitaciones, 52 de ellas swim-up(acceso directo a piscina tipo río) y 15 Junior Suite (piscina y solarium privado),Centro Spa "Elba Corralejo Spa & Wellness, cuatro Restaurantes, cuatro Bares, Miniclub, etc.




El Hotel apuesta por la integración tecnológica moderna y funcional, una tecnología discreta, eficiente y sostenible combinada con el lujo. Buscamos a una persona que entienda el mantenimiento y la reparación no sólo como una función técnica, sino como un oficio clave y esencial para cuidar el lugar y a quienes lo habitan.




Y para hacerlo realidad precisamos incorporar una/un RESPONSABLE DE MANTENIMIENTO cuyo papel será fundamental para garantizar el correcto funcionamiento de todas las instalaciones del hotel, la conservación y la excelencia operativa, asegurando que cada detalle esté alineado con las experiencias y los altos estándares de calidad y servicio que ofrecemos a nuestros clientes. Todo ello desde una mirada eficiente, segura y sostenible.




La persona ideal deberá contar con experiencia previa demostrable en el puesto y conocimientos para supervisar todos los aspectos técnicos de cada una de las áreas del complejo, garantizando un correcto funcionamiento de las instalaciones en coordinación con los distintos departamentos operativos del hotel. Para ello liderará un equipo de técnicos/as de mantenimiento de 17 personas, gestionará las incidencias técnicas del día a día, mantenimientos preventivos y correctivos, elaborar y controlar el presupuesto anual del departamento, gestión de inventarios y proyectos de mejora, etc.




Valoraremos muy positivamente experiencia en gestión digital y eficiencia automatizada, gestión de Sistemas Inteligentes y Domótica.




¡¡¡¡BUSCAMOS LAS MEJORES PERSONAS CANDIDATAS!!!!, si crees que tu perfil encaja en el puesto ofertado, ¡nos encantaría contar contigo! Te ofrecemos contrato indefinido, jornada completa, excelente ambiente laboral y atractivo paquete retributivo en función de valía de la persona seleccionada.




En Elba valoramos la diversidad, la inclusión y la igualdad. No discriminamos por razón de edad, raza, identidad de género, orientación sexual, color, religión género, nacionalidad ni discapacidad, por lo que todas las personas candidatas serán consideras en igualdad de condiciones en base a sus habilidades, conocimientos y experiencia.




¡Te esperamos!
sector: Hospitality, Accommodation and Food Services, and Hotels and Motels

title: Director-a de Negocio Zona Norte
description:
Grupo empresarial de referencia en el ámbito de los servicios, desea incorporar en su sede en Bizkaia




Director-a de Negocio Zona Norte




Serán sus principales funciones, en reporte directo al Consejo de Administración:




 * Dirección de los diferentes negocios del Grupo en la Zona Norte, reportando al Consejo de Administración de las diferentes mercantiles, con participación activa.
 * Relación directa con Accionistas y Consejeros.
 * Elaboración de presupuestos y seguimiento de los mismos.
 * Relación con entidades financieras. Búsqueda de financiación, y optimización de la financiación del Circulante, de las Inversiones Capex.
 * Desarrollo de las relaciones comerciales con clientes (interlocución con Administración Pública y clientes privados)
 * Definir y gestionar el marco de relaciones laborales de la empresa, y participar y liderar los procesos de negociación.




El perfil del puesto corresponde a una persona con titulación superior en ADE, Económicas o similar, con experiencia desarrollando funciones de similar contenido y responsabilidad, valorándose especialmente la experiencia desarrollada en empresas del sector servicios. Deberá poseer sólidos conocimientos económico/financieros y conocimientos de Euskera.




El puesto ofertado es especialmente atractivo tanto por la solidez del Grupo como por el contenido y desarrollo profesional previsto para esta posición. La retribución económica será competitiva y negociable.

title: Controller (Consumer Industy)
description:
About Howard Financial

Howard Financial is a full-service back-office solution for companies and entities with bookkeeping, payroll, and bespoke reporting needs that require accuracy, timeliness, and institutional-quality reporting without the burden of full-time staff.

We are an experienced team of bookkeepers and administrators with multiple decades of combined experience across industries allowing companies to focus on their core business while we handle the rest.

Founded in 2023, our team of 90 professionals supports ~40 clients across a diverse range of industries. We recently reached $5M of contracted ARR and are growing rapidly (over 150% MRR growth YTD).

Position Overview

We are seeking a highly experienced, hands-on Controller to lead and scale the accounting and finance operations for our growing consumer-focused business. This leader will be both strategic and operational—owning financial integrity while actively partnering with executive leadership and external clients.

The ideal candidate thrives in fast-paced consumer environments, excels in client communication and relationship management, and has a proven track record of building and leading high-performing teams.

Key Responsibilities

Financial Leadership & Strategy


 * Oversee all accounting operations including GL, AP/AR, payroll, revenue recognition, inventory, and cost accounting.
 * Lead monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes with accuracy and timeliness.
 * Ensure compliance with GAAP and all regulatory requirements.
 * Develop, implement, and optimize internal controls and financial policies.
 * Partner with executive leadership on financial planning, forecasting, and strategic initiatives.
   
   

Consumer Business Operations


 * Manage financial reporting related to consumer revenue streams, margins, pricing models, and promotional impacts.
 * Oversee inventory accounting and cost management in a consumer-driven environment.
 * Analyze sales performance, channel profitability, and customer trends.
 * Support operational scaling initiatives with strong financial discipline.
   
   

Client & Stakeholder Relations


 * Serve as a financial liaison for key clients and partners.
 * Communicate financial insights clearly to non-financial stakeholders.
 * Build trusted relationships across internal teams, external partners, and executive leadership.
 * Support audits, investor inquiries, and board reporting as needed.
   
   

Team Leadership


 * Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing accounting team.
 * Foster accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
 * Establish scalable processes to support company growth.
 * Recruit and retain top-tier accounting talent.
   
   

Qualifications


 * Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance (CPA strongly preferred).
 * 8–12+ years of progressive accounting/finance experience.
 * 4+ years in a Controller or senior accounting leadership role.
 * Experience in a consumer-focused, retail, e-commerce, or CPG environment.
 * Strong knowledge of GAAP, internal controls, and audit processes.
 * Proven experience leading teams and scaling finance functions.
 * Exceptional communication and client-facing skills.
 * Hands-on leader willing to dive into details when necessary.
 * ERP system experience (NetSuite preferred).
   
   

Core Competencies


 * Strategic thinker with operational execution strength
 * Client-centric mindset
 * High integrity and accountability
 * Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
 * Excellent interpersonal and leadership abilities
 * Ability to thrive in dynamic, growth-oriented environments
   
   

Why Join Us


 * Opportunity to shape and scale a growing consumer business
 * Direct exposure to executive leadership
 * High-impact, visible role
 * Collaborative and entrepreneurial culture

title: Europe & Latam Tax Director
description:
About Horse Powertrain

Horse Powertrain Limited is an independent new powertrain solutions business, with over 125 years of heritage from Renault and Geely. We develop and produce world-class hybrid and combustion engines and transmissions to move our industry forward, offering many different paths to net zero.

Horse Powertrain has 19,000 dedicated and determined employees, working in 17 manufacturing plants, and 5 R&D centers on three continents. This role is bases in Madrid, with occasional travel possible to Horse Powertrain’s other sites.

Why join us?

At Horse Powertrain, you’ll have opportunities to grow your career while making a difference. This is a dynamic, international environment, staffed by caring and collaborative people. If you’re ready to create excellence with us, you’ll be appreciated and rewarded.

What We’re Looking For


 * A proven track record of leading tax teams across multiple countries (across Latam and Europe) and managing complex tax audits and negotiating settlements
 * University degree in Economics/Law/ Finance/ Accounting.
 * Outstanding technical expertise in CIT, VAT, TP and tax compliance and leadership of multicultural tax teams.
 * Strong analytical and risk management skills, coupled with strong communication and stakeholder- management skills.
 * Knowledge of the automotive industry is a plus.
 * Excellent written and spoken English; good working knowledge of Spanish is an advantage.
   
   

Equal opportunities employer

As a company, we are committed to equal opportunities, and recruit people based on their competencies and qualifications. We encourage candidates from underrepresented groups to apply for our job vacancies. If you believe you have the skills and passion to excel in this role, we would love to hear from you. Let’s explore the possibilities together.

How To Apply

Deadline to submit your application is 22/03/2025 but the selection process will be running continuously. Please note that due to data regulations, we do not accept applications via email.

Questions

If you have any additional questions regarding the position, you are welcome to contact recruiting manager.

For questions regarding the recruitment process please contact Recruiter Natalie Freundt.

Horse Powertrain Limited is a global leader in powertrain solutions. Learn more at www.horse-powertrain.com
sector: Engines and Power Transmission Equipment Manufacturing

title: Director of Capital Management
description:
Country: Spain

IT STARTS HERE

Santander (www.santander.com) is evolving from a global, high-impact brand into a technology-driven organization, and our people are at the heart of this journey. Together, we are driving a customer-centric transformation that values bold thinking, innovation, and the courage to challenge what’s possible.

This is more than a strategic shift. It’s a chance for driven professionals to grow, learn, and make a real difference.

Our mission is to contribute to help more people and businesses prosper. We embrace a strong risk culture and all our professionals at all levels are expected to take a proactive and responsible approach toward risk management.

The Wealth Management & Insurance division covers three businesses in all Santander Group countries: Insurance, Asset Management and Private Banking. We work to provide the best solutions to our clients in these three businesses in the 10 countries where we are present.

THE DIFFERENCE YOU MAKE

Santander Wealth Management & Insurance is looking for a Director of Capital Management based out of Madrid

We’re shaping the way we work through innovation, cutting-edge technology, collaboration and the freedom to explore new ideas.

We are seeking an experienced Director of Capital Management to lead the capital management strategy of Santander’s insurance businesses globally, across Europe and the Americas, to ensure:


 * The capital adequacy and efficiency of the insurance businesses of Santander Group
 * Robust compliance with Solvency II and other relevant international prudential requirements
   
   

This role is pivotal in optimizing capital efficiency, managing risk, and supporting strategic decision-making at the highest level.

Key Responsibilities


 * Work closely with the reporting teams, both global and local, to ensure the capital treatment of Santander’s insurance businesses is appropriate and consistent
 * Coordinate with the M&A team to determine the appropriate capital treatment for potential future acquisitions
 * Work closely with the executive team to develop and deliver group holding and risk management structures that ensure capital efficiency for the local and global insurance businesses, and Santander Group overall
 * Develop and maintain capital management plans, collaborating with senior management to ensure they are aligned with business objectives
 * Monitor capital and accounting developments closely to help Santander Group prepare in a timely manner for future changes
   
   

What You’ll Bring

Our people are our greatest strength. Every individual contributes unique perspectives that make us stronger as a team and as an organization. We’re enabling teams to go beyond by valuing who they are and empowering what they bring.

The following requirements represent the knowledge, skills, and abilities essential for success in this role. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Professional Experience


 * Minimum 10 years’ experience in insurance, preferably in capital management, valuation or reporting in an international insurance group and/or consulting firm (Required)
   
   

Education


 * Degree in Actuarial Science, Finance, Mathematics, Law or related field; (Required)
 * Actuarial or CFA qualification (Preferred)
   
   

Languages


 * English language proficiency (Required)
 * Spanish desirable (Preferred)
   
   

Hard Skills


 * Deep knowledge of Solvency II regulations and their application to insurance groups, preferably across different jurisdictions, ownership levels and holding structures (Required)
 * Understanding of IFRS accounting frameworks, including IFRS17 (Required)
 * Strong analytical, problem-solving and communication skills, with the ability to influence at senior levels (Required)
 * Experience leading and delivering multi-stakeholder projects (Required)
 * Understanding of the interaction between insurance and bank capital regimes (Preferred)
 * Experience across both life and non-life sectors at the insurance company level (Preferred)
 * Understanding of capital and accounting frameworks in the Americas (Preferred)
   
   

Soft Skills


 * Strategic thinking with a commercial mindset
 * High attention to detail and accuracy
 * Proactive working style and ability to manage multiple priorities
 * Collaborative style with cross-functional influence
   
   

WE VALUE YOUR IMPACT

Your contribution matters, and it’s recognized. You can expect a fair, competitive reward package that reflects the impact you create and the value you deliver. But we know rewards go beyond numbers.


 * We’re enable our teams to go beyond through global opportunities and broad career paths.
 * Flexibility that works. Enjoy a hybrid working model—some days remote, some days onsite with your team—along with flexible hours.
 * Learning for life. Access hundreds of courses on our platforms, including exclusive access to our global learning space: Santander Open Academy (www.santanderopenacademy.com)
 * Competitive rewards. Receive a highly competitive salary with performance-based bonuses, motivating you to keep growing with us.
 * Financial advantages. Benefit from preferential banking terms, special interest rates on loans, life insurance, and more.
 * Your health is our priority. Through BeHealthy, our global wellness programme, we promote Holistic wellbeing.
 * We know family is everything. That’s why we offer childcare support and family-friendly programmes tailored to each life stage.
 * Always by your side. Get access to Santander Contigo, our program for employees and their families offering legal, emotional, and administrative advisory services.
 * Extra benefits. Gym/WellHub membership, medical centers in some of our facilities, meal subsidy, parking, shuttle service from various points in Madrid, as well as exclusive discounts and offers for Santander employees. And that’s only the beginning—we’ll tell you more when you join!
   
   

We’re here to keep you motivated, help you reach your goals, and celebrate your progress, every step of the way.

LOCAL COMPLIANCE

Santander is proud of being an organization where there are equal opportunities regardless of age, gender, disability, civil status, race, religion or sexual orientation. We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible application process for all candidates.

What To Do Next

If this sounds like a role you are interested in, then please apply.

READY TO TAKE THE NEXT STEP IN YOUR JOURNEY?

title: Dirección Financiera - Asturias
description:
Basado en las oficinas centrales en las inmediaciones de Oviedo, y con reporte directo a la propiedad, buscamos un/a Director/a Financiero/a que será responsable de liderar la estrategia financiera y de control de gestión del grupo. El objetivo central es profesionalizar la toma de decisiones mediante el análisis de datos, asegurando la rentabilidad operativa y la solidez financiera.




Buscamos un profesional con alma operativa, procedente del sector hostelería y/o alimentación, con una trayectoria contrastada en el control de gestión y liderazgo de equipos. Se valorará especialmente su capacidad para actuar como socio estratégico de la propiedad, traduciendo los datos financieros en acciones de negocio tangibles y preparando el terreno para el futuro relevo generacional.




Reportando al CEO, el/la candidato/a asumirá la dirección del departamento financiero. Entre sus principales funciones se incluyen:

 * Liderar la estrategia financiera y el plan de sucesión: Definir y ejecutar la política financiera en coordinación con la propiedad. Garantizar la estabilidad de la tesorería y la planificación de liquidez necesaria para el crecimiento sostenible del negocio.
 * Transformación digital y procesos: Impulsar la modernización del área a través de la implantación y optimización de herramientas tecnológicas (ERP, BI), automatizando el reporting financiero y mejorando la eficiencia administrativa.
 * Gestión del dato y control operativo: Transformar la contabilidad tradicional en una contabilidad analítica y de gestión avanzada. Diseñar y monitorizar KPIs críticos del sector hostelero (costes de materia prima, personal, mermas, escandallos), proporcionando información veraz y ágil para la toma de decisiones en tiempo real en las diferentes unidades de negocio.
 * Liderazgo de equipo y relación con terceros: Dirigir, motivar y desarrollar al equipo actual (tesorería, administración, controller), fomentando la colaboración transversal con RRHH y Marketing. Gestionar la relación con entidades financieras, auditores y asesores fiscales, negociando líneas de financiación y asegurando el cumplimiento normativo.




Requisitos:

 * Licenciatura en ADE, Económicas o similar, valorándose MBA o posgrado en Dirección Financiera.
 * Experiencia sólida en dirección financiera o control de gestión, imprescindible procedencia del sector Hostelería y/o Restauración.
 * Dominio de contabilidad analítica, control de gestión y herramientas de Business Intelligence y ERPs (Navision, SAP u otros).
 * Vinculación o disponibilidad real para establecer su residencia y proyecto de vida en Asturias a largo plazo.




Skills:

 * Integridad, ética profesional y lealtad, valores fundamentales para esta empresa familiar.
 * Visión estratégica con capacidad operativa, entendiendo la realidad del negocio desde la base.
 * Liderazgo cercano y habilidades interpersonales para comunicar información financiera compleja a interlocutores no financieros.
 * Alta tolerancia a la presión y flexibilidad para adaptarse a un entorno dinámico y en constante evolución.
 * Identificación plena con la cultura del esfuerzo y el compromiso de mejora continua que define al grupo.




Si eres un directivo/a financiero/a con vocación de gestión general y buscas un proyecto vital en Asturias, no dudes en enviar tu candidatura.

title: Mánager de Operaciones
description:
En Improven tenemos un propósito: ser referentes en el mundo de la gestión para ayudar a afrontar los retos actuales y futuros de los empresarios. Es por eso que somos personas dinámicas, exigentes, cazadores de tendencias que autoaprenden del entorno constantemente y que poseen grandes habilidades para impulsar el cambio. Buscamos a gente muy comprometida con su desarrollo de carrera, que aporte soluciones, activa y comprometida.




Nuestros principales servicios se centran en las siguientes áreas clave: Crecimiento empresarial, mejoras de procesos y operaciones, desarrollo de modelos culturales y del Talento y Transformación digital.




Contamos con clientes de sectores muy diversos y presencia a nivel nacional, con oficinas en Valencia, Vigo, Barcelona y Madrid.




Como Mánager de Operaciones de Improven tendrás como misión principal garantizar la rentabilidad, calidad y satisfacción del proyecto siendo un referente para el/la cliente/a, el talento interno y los foros en los que participe.




📋¿Cuáles serían tus principales responsabilidades?

 * DIRECCIÓN DE PROYECTOS: Liderar proyectos de principio a fin, representando a la compañía ante el/la cliente/a, planificando recursos y prioridades, supervisando entregables y garantizando el cumplimiento de objetivos, calidad y rentabilidad. Anticipar riesgos, gestionar desviaciones y asegurar una comunicación fluida con cliente y equipo hasta el cierre final del proyecto.
 * CONSTRUCCIÓN DE OFICINA Y EQUIPO: Liderar el desarrollo y consolidación de la oficina mediante la captación y fidelización de talento, la creación de una cultura sólida y alineada con la estrategia corporativa, y el fortalecimiento de la cohesión interna. Impulsar el desarrollo profesional a través de la formación, mentorización y evaluación continua del desempeño.




👀Requisitos:

📚FORMACIÓN: Graduado/a en Ingeniería industrial, Ingeniería en Tecnologías Industriales, Ingeniería Organizacional Industrial o grados similares + Máster o posgrado que otorgue una visión holística de la empresa (MBA) o que esté relacionado con las operaciones industriales (logística, supply chain, producción...).

📈EXPERIENCIA: + de 10 años ocupando algún puesto de responsabilidad de algún área de Operaciones. Es imprescindible contar con experiencia en consultoría.

💪🏻SKILLS: persona comprometida con su desarrollo profesional, autoexigentes y con un firme propósito de mejorar el mundo que les rodea. Pasión, Honestidad, Compañerismo y Esfuerzo son valores imprescindibles en Improven.

⚠️IMPRESCINDIBLE: disponer de vehículo propio, disponibilidad para viajar a nivel nacional con frecuencia y residencia en Cataluña (se valorará el Catalán). Y, contar con experiencia en consultoría.




💪🏻Nuestra propuesta de valor:

 * Incorporación inmediata a sólido proyecto empresarial.
 * Plan de carrera estructurado donde desarrollar tus competencias profesionales y crecer en un ambiente dinámico y enriquecedor. Dicho plan está enfocado a que te desarrolles como Directivo/a.
 * Oportunidad de trabajar en proyectos de distintos sectores, distintas áreas (logística, producción, cadena de suministro…) y distintos tipos de empresa.
 * Contrato indefinido y 24 días de vacaciones.
 * Salario fijo + variable por objetivos. Según experiencia demostrada – entre 53 – 69 k (bruto/anual fijo) + entre 8 – 17k (bruto/anual variable).
 * Abono de dietas diarias (máximo 15 € al día), gastos de desplazamiento y pernoctas.
 * Seguro médico y póliza dental.
 * Plan de Formación anual adaptado.
 * Excelente clima laboral y entorno multidisciplinar.
 * Flexibilidad horaria donde nos adaptamos al cliente y modalidad híbrida.

title: Head of Learning Development (SENCO) & / or Designated Safeguarding Lead (DSL)
description:
Inspired Education is the leading global group of premium schools, with a portfolio of 121 premium private schools spanning 6 continents which utilise proven educational practices from every corner of the globe, ensuring over 95,000 students receive a world-class learning experience from Kindergarten to Year 13.

In 28 countries, Inspired schools are individually developed and anchored in the vibrant cultures of their respective locations. They collectively form an international family of schools that nurtures each student's academic and personal development in a progressive, dynamic, and innovative learning environment.

By joining our award-winning team of over 13,000 employees, you can work in state-of-the-art facilities alongside industry-renowned educators and leaders in some of the world's most desirable locations. We invest heavily in our team members who enjoy industry-leading professional development, exceptional career opportunities, and mobility across our group.

ROLE SUMMARY:

King’s College School Soto de Viñuelas, is seeking to appoint an outstanding and experienced professional to support and lead Learning Development and Safeguarding across the school from September 2026.

Flexible Appointment Structure

Depending on the profile and experience of candidates, the school may appoint:


 * A full-time Head of Learning Development (SENCO) & DSL (combined leadership role)
 * A full-time Head of Learning Development (SENCO) only
 * A teacher (Primary or Secondary) with SENCO or DSL as an additional responsibility
   
   

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:


 * Leading Learning Development provision across the whole school
 * Acting as the school SENCO
 * Leading the development and monitoring of IEPs
 * Supporting pupils with IEPs across the curriculum
 * Ensuring appropriate access arrangements for public examinations
 * Delivering and leading INSET related to SEND
 * Managing Learning Development staff and resources
   
   

Additional DSL Responsibilities (where Applicable)


 * Acting as Designated Safeguarding Lead in line with UK best practice
 * Leading safeguarding training and compliance
 * Liaising with external agencies
 * Promoting a strong safeguarding culture across the school
   
   

THE IDEAL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE:


 * Holds QTS or equivalent
 * Has significant SEND experience in a British curriculum setting
 * Demonstrates strong leadership and organisational skills
 * Is committed to inclusive education and pupil wellbeing
 * For DSL: has substantial safeguarding leadership experience
   
   

SAFEGUARDING STATEMENT

Inspired Education Group is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people and expects all staff, volunteers and other third parties to share this commitment. Safer recruitment practices and pre-employment background checks will be undertaken before any appointment is confirmed.

Visit our careers site for more information; https://jobs.inspirededu.com

title: Supervisor/a operaciones
description:
Director/a de operaciones

1 vacante

Requisitos


 * Estudios mínimos: Estudios universitarios
 * Experiencia mínima: requerida de dos años mínimo en el sector de turismo.
 * Carnet de conducir
 * Trabajo en equipo y de espíritu cooperativo
 * Visión global y de futuro.
 * Saber delegar y repartir tareas.
 * Digital, líder, innovador, dinámico, empatía, con vocación de servicio
 * Experiencia en gestión de equipos
 * Nivel avanzado de inglés. Valorable otros idiomas extranjeros.
 * Alta capacidad de organización, planificación y pensamiento estratégico
 * Comunicación efectiva y enfocado a los resultados
 * Flexibilidad y gestión del cambio.
   
   

Trabajar en Luderna es mucho más que tener un empleo.

Es formar parte de un proyecto con raíces profundas en el corazón del Valle de Arán, donde cada detalle cuenta y cada experiencia deja huella.

Gestionamos más de 200 apartamentos entre el Valle de Arán y Andorra, y nos hemos convertido en la referencia del alquiler vacacional en la región. Ahora, como parte del grupo Emerald Stay, estamos conectados con una red internacional de alojamientos únicos en más de 20 destinos europeos. Pero nuestra esencia sigue aquí, en la montaña, donde el confort y la hospitalidad tienen su propio ritmo.

Nos mueve la voluntad sincera de hacer las cosas bien. Queremos que nuestros huéspedes se sientan especiales sin saber muy bien por qué. Y que los propietarios confíen en nosotros, no solo por lo que decimos, sino sobre todo por lo que hacemos.

Nuestro equipo de operaciones trabaja desde Vielha, coordinando cada engranaje para que la experiencia sea impecable. Y ahora buscamos a alguien como tú: con ganas, con curiosidad, con iniciativa y con ilusión por crecer dentro del proyecto LUDERNA.

Aquí el trabajo se vive con responsabilidad, pero también con empatía, respeto y cercanía. Te escuchamos, te motivamos a mejorar y te acompañamos en el camino. Porque crecer en Luderna es también construir algo propio.

Descripción

Buscamos a nuestra/o director/a de operaciones: persona organizada y dinamica, con buena resolución de incidencias. Con experiencia en el sector y en la gestión de equipos. Las aptidudes necesarias para desarrollar el trabajos son:


 * Capacidad de trabajo en equipo y liderazgo
 * Visión global y de futuro
 * Organización y reparto de tareas. Con capacidad para la toma de decisiones
   
   

Funciones


 * Planificar, organizar, y gestionar las distintas unidades de negocio, para favorecer un buen posicionamiento en el mercado con el fin de obtener los objetivos fijados
 * Formación y motivación del equipo de operaciones (alojamiento y housekeeping) y reciclaje según cambien procesos o sea necesario.
 * Auditar el seguimiento de la comunicación interna, horarios y guardias de personal.
 * Desarrollo de protocolos y procedimientos de trabajo en el área de operaciones, y seguimiento de los principales KPIs
 * Resolución de incidencias y quejas.
 * Gestión de acceso a los apartamentos.
 * Movilizar a los trabajadores a impulsar la calidad e involucrarnos en el perfeccionamiento de los procesos con una orientación “Guest centricity”
 * Desarrollar la cultura de atención al cliente basada en criterios de calidad y gestión de los puntos de dolor y de valor basadas en encuestas internas.
 * Reportará directamente a la Dirección General
   
   

Condiciones

Horario: lunes a domingo (dos días de descanso semanales).

Jornada completa: 8 horas diarias.

Tipo de contrato: indefinido

Salario: a determinar

Incorporación: inmediata

title: Director de centro Vitoria
description:
¡Tu liderazgo puede marcar la diferencia! Únete a nuestro equipo.




¿Quiénes somos?

Somos Norauto, multinacional líder en equipamiento y mantenimiento multimarca del automóvil. Contamos con más de 2.600 especialistas en 93 centros distribuidos en España, comprometidos en ofrecer la mejor experiencia a nuestros clientes.




¿Qué buscamos?

Líderes apasionados por el retail, la gestión de equipos y la atención al cliente, con fuerte orientación a resultados y visión de negocio.

title: Director, ELA OMS QEHS
location: San Sebastián de los Reyes, Community of Madrid, Spain
description:
Requisition Number: 25820

Company: Tecnatom S.A.U.

Location:

SAN SEBASTIAN DE LOS REYES, M, ES, 28703 Västerås, SE Nivelles, BE Orsay, FR Mannheim, DE Salwick Preston, GB

Welcome to the future of nuclear energy, where Westinghouse Electric Company is leading the field with expertise and progress to shape the power of tomorrow.

At Westinghouse, innovation is in our DNA. We are creative. We think differently. We reimagine the possible across the nuclear industry every day.

As the ELA (Europe, Latin America and Asia) OMS QEHS Director you will have full responsibility and organizational leadership of the Environmental, Health and Safety (EHS) and Quality functions for the ELA OMS organization.

The QEHS Director will collaborate with the Americas OMS EHS director, the global OMS Quality team and Global Operations Services leading a multi-country team in the range of 50 employees.

The role will report to the Senior Vice President, OMS QEHS and can be based in any European location where we have operations (Spain, France, Belgium, UK, Germany and Sweden).

Hybrid work schedule.

Ehs

Main Responsibilities:

Implement EHS Programs for ELA OMS.


 * Establish a governance structure to ensure the inclusion of all important partners implementation of programs/changes considering product differences, geographical differences, and performance needs/expectations.
 * Oversee ELA OMS EHS systems, programs, and policies that are compliant with applicable local, regional, national EHS regulations and standards.
 * Align with Westinghouse's EHS Management Systems.
 * Promote excellence in the organizations' performance in Environmental, and Health and Safety.
 * Provide leadership for EHS change plans aimed at driving a safety culture which enables business growth.
 * Develop measurement and reporting of EHS performance metrics to deliver tangible results for ELA OMS.
 * Evaluate trends for continuous improvement and corrective measures and measuring process effectiveness with both leading and lagging indicators.
 * Ensure resources for Environmental, Health, and Safety compliance along with Sustainability expectations globally.
 * Develop and support OMS EHS strategic vision, partner with Corporate and Business Unit EHS leaders to develop Westinghouse's EHS strategic vision and perform OMS EHS management reviews.
 * Maintain overall budget responsibility for all responsibilities ensuring process effectiveness and adherence to all applicable laws and regulations.
 * Coordinate needs, such as customer requests, supplier EHS, contractor EHS, mergers and acquisitions, LOA's.
 * Lead development of EHS integration plans for BU acquisitions and monitor execution of the plan to ensure integration that minimizes risk to the BU and Westinghouse.
 * Communicate and facilitate finding ELA OMS support for Westinghouse EHS internal audits.
   
   

Quality


 * Manage implementation of Westinghouse quality policies in compliance with local customer requirements, codes and standards.
 * Manage team of QA staff to support Westinghouse's Quality Assurance Program, manage Qualified Supplier List (QSL), and lead continuous improvement plans.
 * Ensure understanding of all European, Latin America and Asia quality requirements, as applicable, and understanding of Westinghouse's procedures.
 * Monitor and implement changes to Westinghouse global QMS.
 * Promote a culture of compliance and customer satisfaction.
 * Ensure quality program changes are communicated and implemented throughout Business Unit and Global Operations Services.
 * Interface on quality matters with the customers and the regulatory authority having jurisdication.
 * Communicate with all disciplines on quality matters (Engineering, Procurement, Production, Sales, EHS/HU).
 * Oversee internal and external audits, project audits, management assessments.
 * Lead corrective action program for ELA OMS.
 * Resolve solutions to quality problems and implement quality assurance best practices through continuous improvement.
 * Develop quality indoctrination training and other education programs.
 * Develop department budget and control expenditures to remain within budget.
 * Ensure staff are trained and manage employee performance to achieve department goals.
 * Compile quality performance data for annual management review.
   
   

Qualifications


 * Minimum Bachelor's degree (technical discipline).
 * MBA or Master's degree desirable.
 * 15+ years experience , including EH&S programs and quality program administration.
 * 5 + years experience in nuclear quality management.
 * ISO 14001 and/or 45001 knowledge and working experience.
 * English and any other European language required.
 * Demonstrated strong communication skills.
 * Travel required: Minimum 25%.
 * Global Travel required.
   
   

Why Westinghouse?

We know that to put forth your best effort, you need to be challenged and enjoy what you do in a supportive and respectful environment. We aim to maintain this balance by offering our employees the amenities, benefits and training they need to reach personal and professional goals.

Below is an example of what employees in Spain can expect:


 * Attractive compensation
 * Hybrid model of work.
 * High flexibility and autonomy.
 * Great benefits for your convenience and safety:
    * Peer-to-peer recognition program.
    * Lunch allowance.
    * Private health insurance.
    * Life insurance.
    * Employee Assistance Program: confidential counseling and resources for employees, eligible dependents, and household members

 * Learning and development opportunities.
 * We encourage our employees to participate in community service events and other team-building activities to strengthen their bonds and inspire each other.
   

You can learn more about Westinghouse by visiting http://www.westinghousenuclear.com.

Westinghouse is an Equal Opportunity Employer including Veterans and Individuals with Disabilities.

Get connected with Westinghouse on social media: Twitter | Facebook | LinkedIn| YouTube

title: Global Human Resources Director

title: Country Financial Controller – Spain
description:
About MoOngy

Founded in 2005, MoOngy is an international group specialized in Engineering and IT, operating across Europe and North Africa.

With a presence in 14 countries, more than 40 entities, and 9,500 talents, we continue to grow every day thanks to one simple thing: people who are genuinely passionate about technology and innovation.




The Role: Country Financial Controller




📍 Location: Spain, Madrid is preferable

🗣️ Languages: Spanish: fluent; English C1; French B2 is a plus

✅ Eligibility: Currently living in Spain be legally authorized to work there







🧑 Your Profile

Education & Certifications

 * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or Business Administration.
 * Professional certification (CPA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) is a strong plus.




Professional Experience

 * 8 – 15 years of experience in finance, controlling, or financial management roles.
 * Proven experience as Financial Controller, Senior Controller, or Finance Manager.
 * Background in project-based, people-driven environments (engineering, consulting, professional services, construction, EPC, or similar).
 * Hands-on experience managing multi-project P&L, cost control, and long-term contracts.
 * Experience working with €30M+ entities or complex multi-entity structures.




Technical & Financial Skills

 * Strong knowledge of Spanish GAAP and IFRS.
 * Solid understanding of revenue recognition models (percentage of completion, milestones, T&M).
 * Advanced expertise in budgeting, forecasting, and scenario modeling.
 * Strong command of working capital management, cash flow forecasting, and DSO optimization.
 * Good knowledge of Spanish tax regulations (Corporate Tax, VAT, withholding taxes).
 * Experience with internal controls, audits, and external advisors.
 * High level of proficiency in ERP systems and financial reporting tools (SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, or similar).
 * Advanced Excel / financial modeling skills (yes, still unavoidable 😅).




Leadership & Business Partnering

 * Proven experience managing and developing finance teams (6–10 people).
 * Strong business partnering skills with Project Managers, Technical Leads, and Senior Management.
 * Ability to translate financial data into clear, actionable insights for non-finance stakeholders.
 * Comfortable supporting commercial decisions, pricing strategies, and contract negotiations.
 * Experience driving process improvement and finance digitalization initiatives.




Language Requirements

 * English: Fluent / Professional working proficiency (mandatory) – daily interaction with group finance and international stakeholders.
 * French: Professional proficiency (preferred) – strong asset in group-level communication and cross-country collaboration.
 * Spanish: Native or fluent







🛠️ What You’ll Be Doing:

 * You´ll lead all financial operations for the Spanish entity of a multinational engineering consulting firm, managing a business of approximately €40M turnover.
 * This role sits right at the crossroads of finance, projects, and strategy. You’ll ensure rock-solid financial governance, deep project-level profitability control, and full compliance with Spanish and group regulations—while acting as a trusted business partner to technical leaders and senior management.
 * In a people-driven, project-based environment with long delivery cycles, your insights will directly influence margins, investment decisions, and sustainable growth.
 * In short: numbers matter, but context matters more.




Why Join Us?

 * 🌍 Innovation & Impact: Play a central role in driving the future through creative solutions
 * 🚀 Challenging projects: Modern environments, real impact, zero boredom.
 * 📚 Continuous learning: Trainings, certifications, and structured career growth.
 * 🤝 Team spirit: An international community where collaboration means something.
 * 🌈 Diversity & inclusion: At MoOngy, everyone belongs — no fine print.




Diversity & Inclusion

At MoOngy Group, we are committed to equality and diversity. We foster a safe, inclusive, and respectful environment where everyone has equal opportunities.

We do not discriminate based on age, origin, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or any criterion unrelated to skills and merit.

title: Store Manager -  Alicante
description:
Desde su creación en París en 1998, Maje ha sabido construir su propio estilo y ha trazado una sutil línea entre la modernidad y la extravagancia, centrándose en piezas clave y detalles nítidos. El espíritu Maje es una silueta muy femenina, a la vez sobria, glamurosa y audaz. Afiliada al Grupo SMCP, la marca prosigue con éxito su desarrollo internacional y cuenta con 627 puntos de venta y cerca de 1.700 empleados con talento en 44 países.


En 2022, Maje alcanzó una facturación de más de 467 millones de euros. En los últimos años, la casa ha abordado la transición digital con gran ambición y ha acelerado su compromiso hacia la sostenibilidad. La inclusión y la diversidad también están en el centro de nuestros intereses porque estamos convencidos de que la innovación y la creatividad nacen de la diversidad de opiniones y perfiles. Porque queremos ofrecer a nuestros empleados y candidatos un entorno inclusivo donde cada quien tiene su lugar, independientemente de su origen, procedencia y preferencias...


¡Te toca a ti enriquecer nuestra historia!

¡Atrévete a ser aventurero y a asumir un nuevo reto, ven y expresa tu talento dentro de la familia Maje!



Descripción del empleo


Lo que ofrecemos:

Una posición indefinida de Store Manager. En Maje, en el día a día, serás responsable de la gestión comercial y operativa de la tienda, la gestión de un equipo, el coaching y la formación de los miembros del equipo, la animación en tienda y el cumplimiento de nuestras guías de VM.





Trabajar para Maje es

Empezar tu aventura con un onboarding personalizado la primera semana,

Contribuir al mayor desarrollo de nuestra marca con un servicio único y cortés,

Trabajar junto a equipos apasionados, solidarios y dinámicos,

Participación en proyectos ambiciosos de una marca orientada al futuro, la innovación y la estabilidad.

Tener oportunidades de desarrollo profesional dentro del Grupo Maje o SMCP.



Requisitos


¿Eres un líder fuerte y puedes unir tu equipo?

¿Eres un gerente empático, enfocado en entrenar y desarrollar a tu equipo?

¿Es capaz de analizar el business de negocio y poner en marcha los planes de acción adecuados?

¿Te apasiona la moda y el retail?



¿Te inspiran los siguientes valores: amabilidad, libertad, creatividad, generosidad y familia?

No esperes más, únete a Maje.





Proceso de selección para este puesto:

Un gerente o un miembro del equipo de recursos humanos se comunicará con usted por teléfono para una entrevista inicial.

Si esta primera llamada confirma que nuestras ambiciones coinciden, se le invitará a una entrevista con el Gerente de Área.



Información adicional


Las ventajas que Maje tiene para ofrecer son:

Tarjeta digital Restaurante Pass

Un programa de bonus mensual

Un programa de bonus de temporada

Descuentos en tus compras en las marcas SMCP

Ventas privadas constantes

Acceso ilimitado a nuestra plataforma de E-learning: Mylearning

title: Director de Diseño
description:
Job Description

Quiénes somos

Omnicom Production es la unidad global de producción creativa de Omnicom Group. Trabajamos como un hub internacional de delivery, ofreciendo soluciones de contenido escalables, consistentes y de alta calidad para marcas globales, en múltiples mercados y canales.

Operamos en un entorno internacional, colaborando con equipos creativos y de producción de distintos países, y trabajando con modelos de producción optimizados que priorizan la eficiencia, la calidad y la coherencia de marca.

Sobre El Rol

Buscamos un/a Director de Diseño para liderar equipos creativos multidisciplinares en OP España, elevando la excelencia en diseño y garantizando un output creativo excepcional en todos los proyectos.

Serás responsable de guiar el desarrollo de conceptos, supervisar la dirección visual, mentorizar a los equipos de diseño y colaborar estrechamente con los equipos de estrategia, producción y liderazgo creativo para dar vida a ideas de nivel internacional.

Este puesto es ideal para un/a líder visionario/a del diseño, con un fuerte instinto creativo, una sólida base técnica y pasión por crear experiencias de marca con impacto.

Responsabilidades


 * Liderar e inspirar equipos de diseño en múltiples disciplinas, incluyendo diseño gráfico, motion, digital y experiencias.
 * Desarrollar y supervisar conceptos creativos, asegurando una ejecución de diseño de alta calidad en todos los entregables.
 * Colaborar con los equipos de creatividad, estrategia y producción para definir enfoques de proyecto y soluciones creativas.
 * Velar por el cumplimiento y la evolución de las guías de marca, garantizando coherencia y excelencia en todos los puntos de contacto.
 * Ofrecer mentoría, feedback y liderazgo a diseñadores/as de todos los niveles, apoyando su crecimiento y desarrollo profesional.
 * Presentar conceptos creativos y recomendaciones de diseño a la alta dirección y a clientes con claridad y confianza.
 * Apoyar el desarrollo de propuestas, pitches y necesidades de diseño para nuevo negocio.
 * Supervisar el control de calidad de todos los outputs visuales, asegurando precisión, nivel de craft y alineación con los objetivos estratégicos.
 * Investigar y promover nuevas tendencias, herramientas y tecnologías de diseño para impulsar la innovación.
   
   

Requisitos


 * Amplia experiencia en roles de liderazgo en diseño dentro de agencias creativas, estudios de producción o entornos de marca.
 * Portfolio que demuestre un alto nivel de excelencia en diseño y liderazgo en múltiples formatos y medios.
 * Dominio de herramientas de diseño como Adobe Creative Suite y Figma, y familiaridad con flujos de trabajo de motion y 3D.
 * Gran capacidad de pensamiento conceptual y de traducir la estrategia en ideas visuales impactantes.
 * Excelentes habilidades de comunicación y presentación, con experiencia liderando conversaciones con clientes.
 * Capacidad para equilibrar la visión estratégica con un liderazgo práctico y hands-on en diseño cuando sea necesario.
 * Experiencia demostrada en mentorizar y desarrollar talento creativo.
 * Mentalidad colaborativa y orientada a soluciones, con fuertes habilidades organizativas y de liderazgo.

title: Production Director
description:
Operations Director – Pharma Manufacturing




Own end-to-end Production and Packaging at a fast-growing pharmaceutical site in expansion mode. This is a hands-on leadership role with real scale and complexity: new lines, automation, digitalisation, and preparation for increased FDA-regulated business.




You will lead multi-technology operations (sterile liquids, solids, high-containment), run a sizeable budget, and work shoulder-to-shoulder with Engineering, Supply Chain, Quality, and Projects to raise performance, modernise the site, and support long-term growth.

High visibility with senior leadership and international clients

title: Head of Supply
description:
The Story So Far: We’re Building a Global Brand in Real Estate

Huspy is one of the leading property technology companies in EMEA.

Launched in 2020, we now operate in multiple cities across the UAE and Spain, expanding into Saudi Arabia and 3 more European markets by 2026. Today, we own the largest portion of the UAE mortgage market and are one of the fastest-growing players in every European city we’ve entered.

We’ve raised over $140 million (Series A and Series B) from the world’s top investors, including Sequoia Capital and Balderton Capital, to reshape the homebuying journey through powerful technology and agent-first tools.

We’ve built a SuperApp that empowers real estate agents and mortgage brokers, bringing cutting‑edge technology to one of the world’s most traditional industries. We’re transforming how property transactions happen — faster, smarter, and better for everyone.

We’re not slowing down. The question is: will you be part of what’s next?

The Main Event: What You’ll Drive, Build, and Own

The Head of Supply will lead the properties division at Huspy, with the mission of consolidating, optimizing, and expanding the portfolio of properties available on our platform. This role is crucial in defining and executing property acquisition strategies, ensuring operational efficiency in documentation and listing processes, and enhancing the service provided to clients and agents.


 * Portfolio Management: Develop a solid strategy for property acquisition and management, ensuring a relevant and competitive market offering.
 * Acquisition Strategies: Design and implement innovative plans to attract high-value properties aligned with business needs.
 * Operational Optimization: Organize and oversee documentation processes, property listing uploads, and quality control of listings.
 * Team Leadership: Manage, motivate, and scale a high-performance team, ensuring goal achievement.
 * Analysis & Metrics: Define and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to evaluate the effectiveness of acquisition and portfolio management strategies.
 * Cross-functional Collaboration: Work closely with sales, technology, and marketing teams to optimize growth and efficiency in the supply area.
   
   

The Perfect Match: What It Takes to Succeed at Huspy


 * 4+ years of leadership experience in startups, proptechs, or real estate-related industries.
 * Strong analytical skills to interpret data and make strategic decisions based on metrics.
 * Proven experience managing teams, with leadership capabilities and talent development skills.
 * Knowledge of the real estate ecosystem, including property acquisition models and commercial strategies.
 * Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously in a dynamic and fast-paced environment.
 * Entrepreneurial mindset, results-driven, with exceptional communication and negotiation skills.
   
   

The Perks: Why You’ll Love Working With Us


 * Pluxee card of 240€ per month.
 * 26 days of holidays per year.
 * The opportunity to lead one of the most strategic areas in a fast-growing startup.
 * A dynamic and collaborative environment where innovation is key.
 * Professional development opportunities in a challenging and motivating setting.
 * Competitive compensation package, including fixed salary and performance-based variable pay.
   
   

If you want to be part of the real estate revolution and have the profile to lead our Supply division, we’re looking for you!

title: Export Global Manager
description:
Somos una empresa líder del sector del electrodoméstico. Buscamos un/a Export Global Manager para liderar el lanzamiento y desarrollo internacional de una de nuestras marcas, con un enorme potencial de crecimiento. La persona seleccionada tendrá un papel clave en la definición del modelo comercial global, en la apertura de nuevos mercados y en la construcción de todos los procesos, herramientas y estructuras necesarias para impulsar la marca a nivel internacional.

Este rol es ideal para alguien con mentalidad emprendedora, capacidad de crear desde cero y motivación por construir un proyecto global con impacto.




Responsabilidades principales

En una marca donde todo está por hacer, tus responsabilidades serán tan estratégicas como operativas:




Construcción estratégica

 * Definir desde cero la estrategia internacional de la marca, incluyendo mercados prioritarios, enfoque comercial y roadmap de expansión.
 * Diseñar el modelo de distribución internacional: selección de partners, agentes, distribuidores y canales adecuados para cada país.
 * Desarrollar la estrategia de pricing global, política comercial y condiciones para distribuidores.




Apertura y desarrollo de mercados

 * Identificar oportunidades en nuevos países y liderar la apertura de mercados desde la prospección inicial hasta el cierre de acuerdos.
 * Realizar estudios de mercado, análisis de competencia y evaluación de tendencias para orientar el posicionamiento de la marca.
 * Construir y escalar la red internacional de partners, asegurando relaciones de largo plazo.




Operaciones y estructura internacional

 * Crear y optimizar los procesos de exportación, desde logística hasta documentación y compliance.
 * Coordinar previsiones de ventas, necesidades logísticas y planificación de demanda para garantizar lanzamientos exitosos.
 * Trabajar en estrecha colaboración con producto, marketing y operaciones para asegurar la coherencia global de la marca.




Representación y crecimiento

 * Representar a la marca en ferias y eventos internacionales, contribuyendo al reconocimiento y reputación en mercados clave.
 * Impulsar el crecimiento internacional mediante análisis continuo de resultados, KPIs y márgenes por región.
 * Proponer, liderar y ejecutar planes de acción que aceleren la expansión y la visibilidad de la marca.

title: Responsable de Almacenes y Distribución
description:
Rol del puesto

Buscamos al Responsable Integral de la operativa de almacenamiento y expedición de empresa muy potente en sector de distribución.

Buscamos una persona que aporte un fuerte conocimiento en la gestión del stock, almacenamiento y distribución, así como un liderazgo de equipos, operativo y estratégico.

Puesto basado en Granada.




Descripción del rol

 * Liderar de forma directa al equipo de logística,(Almacenes y Expediciones), con una estructura aproximada de 70 personas, asegurando orden, disciplina operativa, productividad y cumplimiento de objetivos en un entorno físico y exigente.

 * Gestionar la relación diaria con operadores logísticos, agencias de transporte y autónomos, negociando condiciones, horarios, prioridades de carga y niveles de servicio.
 * Mantener interlocución continua con proveedores del área logística: mantenimiento de instalaciones, sistemas de almacenaje, maquinaria, limpieza y servicios auxiliares.
 * Definir y ordenar el sistema de almacenamiento por ubicaciones, optimizando layouts, flujos internos y movimientos de mercancía para mejorar productividad y reducir errores.
 * Analizar y hacer seguimiento de los principales indicadores del área (productividad, rotación, servicio, incidencias, costes, stock), tomando decisiones correctoras cuando sea necesario.
 * Elaborar y controlar el presupuesto del área logística, detectando desviaciones y aplicando medidas correctivas para asegurar el cumplimiento de objetivos económicos.
 * Establecer procedimientos claros de trabajo y sistemas de control interno que aporten orden, trazabilidad y estabilidad a la operativa diaria.
 * Trabajar de forma coordinada con compras para el aprovisionamiento y con ventas para la planificación del servicio, asegurando alineación entre stock, demanda y capacidad operativa.
 * Participar activamente en proyectos de mejora continua, orientados a ganar eficiencia, fiabilidad y calidad de servicio.
 * Velar por el cumplimiento de normativas de calidad, seguridad y prevención, asegurando un entorno de trabajo seguro y conforme a los stándares de la compañía.







Perfil buscado (H/M/D)

 * Experiencia mínima de 5 años en puestos similares de responsabilidad y dirección en distribución intensiva, retail, recambios, suministros, o gran consumo.
 * Experiencia en gestión de almacenes manueales, con elevado número de referencias (y con logística de entrega nacional. Gestión diaria superior a 4000 unidades y más de 800 expediciones.
 * Perfil firme, resolutivo y con autoridad natural.
 * Orientado a resultados, orden y disciplina operativa.
 * Capacidad de interlocución transversal con compras, ventas y dirección.




Qué ofrecemos

 * Incorporación a una empresa sólida del sector retail, con operaciones de gran volumen y alta complejidad logística.
 * Posición estratégica con alto impacto en el negocio y visibilidad ante la Dirección de Operaciones.

 * Paquete retributivo competitivo, alineado con la responsabilidad del puesto y la experiencia aportada.

title: Director, Global Digital Excellence
description:
Director, Global Digital Excellence, full-time, Global Hub Barcelona

Introduction

Join us at AstraZeneca’s Biopharmaceuticals Business Unit (BBU) where we turn ideas into life-changing medicines. We’re passionate about the potential of science to address the unmet needs of patients around the world. We commit to those areas where we believe we can really change the course of medicine and bring big new insights into life. Our Vision for Global Business Excellence is to support our BBU Ambition with a relentless focus on securing pre-launch / launch excellence, future proofing the BBU with elevated world class capabilities and enabling practice change at scale.

Team Overview

In our ever-changing environment the importance of finding a blend between innovation and new ways of working while keeping focus on the core principles for impactful interactions with our external customers is more important than ever. In short, strong marketing capabilities are at the core of this and will secure the long-term success for the BBU and AZ overall. 

The Global Marketing Excellence team plays a critical role in defining the roadmap for future success. Ensuring that the required marketing and launch capabilities are developed across the BBU. Our Marketing Excellence mission is to “Inspire and build world class marketers, equipped to deliver exceptional customer experiences, which change clinical practice, improve patient outcomes, and drive growth for our brands”.

Role Overview

Reporting to the Senior Director, Global Launch & Marketing Excellence, your main priority will be to drive implementation and adoption of Digital Twin by preparing our people, processes and mindset to embed new ways of working, creating greater customer value. The role sits within the Digital Twin workstream “Change Management, Communications & Capability Building” and provides change management expert support, stakeholder engagement, and communications for the Digital Twin Program—ensuring adoption, behavioral change, and measurable business impact.

Key Objectives In 2026 For The Role


 * Ensure continuation and refresh plans in pace for appropriate wave 1 markets, proactively addressing any adoption challenges with course correction
 * Continue to onboard additional region / markets by creating consistent systematic onboarding process that drives implementation with ease
 * Elevate existing assets and content, while creating new centrally curated content that will amplify the change narrative and drive capability builds with role-based learning pathways
   
   

Key Accountabilities


 * Change strategy and planning: Define the end-to-end change approach for Digital Twin use cases across EUCAN Customer Engagement (case for change, stakeholder analysis, change impacts, adoption metrics, roadmap). Align with CoE governance, Global Functions, and Market delivery plans.
 * Stakeholder engagement: Map and segment stakeholders across EUCAN markets, BBU Global Brand Teams and Global Functions (GIBEx, cDH, CIT, GBS). Build engagement plans, influence senior sponsors, and maintain feedback loops to surface risks and adoption barriers early.
 * WorkVivo Space ownership: Establish and govern the EUCAN Customer Engagement WorkVivo Space (information architecture, editorial standards, moderation, community management). Curate content that promotes consistent communications, best-practice sharing, showcases, and Q&A for Digital Twin and adjacent initiatives.
 * Communications leadership: Design and execute multi-channel communications (executive messaging, town halls, newsletters, demos, videos, FAQs, Nucleus/WorkVivo content). Maintain consistent storytelling on value, progress, and outcomes; manage an editorial calendar and tone of voice.
 * Readiness and adoption: Maintain and evolve role-based training and enablement with SMEs (playbooks, job aids, learning paths). Coordinate pilots and cutovers with Global and market teams. Measure adoption, sentiment, and proficiency; drive interventions to close gaps.
 * Use-case storytelling: Translate Digital Twin capabilities into customer engagement outcomes (e.g., call planning optimisation, next-best-action, resource allocation, omnichannel effectiveness) and coordinate with markets to publish success stories via WorkVivo.
 * Governance and risk management: Run change governance forums; manage issues, dependencies, and change risks. Ensure alignment with other CoE workstreams and particularly with Regulatory to meet data privacy, compliance, brand, and medical/legal standards across EUCAN.
 * Community of practice: Facilitate a Customer Engagement community across Markets/Global Functions to share methods, templates, and lessons learned; harmonise approaches with BBU/OBU partners.
 * Continuous improvement: Use analytics and feedback (WorkVivo engagement data, sentiment, surveys) to refine messaging, channels, and training. A/B test communications and iterate based on adoption data.
   
   

Your second priority will be collaborating with the global marketing excellence team on broader digital initiatives. This involves building a global marketing community, embedding our vision for Marketing 2030, evolving launch and strategic planning processes, and driving flagship marketing initiatives to support Ambition 2030.

Expertise Required


 * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Communication, or a related field.
 * Experience: 7+ years in change management and communications for digital/data transformation in commercial or customer engagement contexts; proven delivery in complex, multi-country environments —ideally in the pharmaceutical, healthcare, or related sectors
 * Commercial Digital Twin/AI experience: Experience with Commercial Digital Twins or comparable pharma commercial Artificial Intelligence solutions within a European data privacy context.
 * Transformation communications leadership: Demonstrated communications and change management leadership in large-scale business transformation programs.
 * Stakeholder management: Ability to influence without authority, manage complex stakeholder networks (Global Functions, EUCAN markets, BBU/OBU), and resolve resistance.
 * Strategic storytelling: Translate technical capabilities into customer engagement outcomes and market-ready narratives.
 * User-centricity: Build change and communications around personas and journeys (field teams, brand leads, analytics teams, medical, market leadership).
 * Collaboration: Create trusted partnerships across Global Functions, IBEX, EUCAN markets, BBU/OBU, and CoE teams.
 * Measurement and analytics: Skilled at defining adoption KPIs, establishing feedback mechanisms, and interpreting engagement and usage data to guide interventions.
 * Platform ownership: Experience standing up and governing collaboration platforms (e.g., WorkVivo, SharePoint) with clear information architecture and moderation practices.
 * Project delivery: Strong planning and orchestration across multiple workstreams; familiarity with agile ceremonies and PMO practices.
 * Operational rigour: Establish clear governance, standards, and cadences for WorkVivo and communications.
 * Resilience and adaptability: Navigate ambiguity and adapt to evolving priorities and discovery.
 * Results orientation: Drive adoption, behaviour change, and measurable impact, not just activity.
   
   

Business Area


 * GIBEX – Marketing Excellence
   
   

Organizational position


 * Global

12-feb-2026

Closing Date

11-mar-2026
