title: Lead Digital Strategist
description:
🚀 We're Growing our Operations Team!

Impact Studios is expanding its Transformation Practice, and we are looking for a high‑impact Lead Digital Strategist to help shape, build, and scale our consulting capability.

This role sits at the heart of business transformation, connecting strategy, operations, and technology to redesign how organizations work, grow, innovate, and execute. You will lead strategic engagements that modernize operating models, re-engineer processes, accelerate digital adoption, and introduce AI-enabled ways of working across enterprise functions.

You will work closely with our engineering organization (800+ AI, data, cloud, and software experts) to ensure strategies turn into measurable outcomes and real implementation.

This is a builder role: part strategy, part delivery, part leadership — ideal for someone who thrives in ambiguity, drives clarity, and has a strong point of view on how companies should transform in a world shaped by AI and rapid technological change.

Key Responsibilities2. Lead Strategic Transformation Projects3. Drive Commercial Growth & Client Success4. Build & Develop a High‑Performing Team5. Connect Strategy with Technology & Execution


 * Build and Evolve the Transformation Offering
 * Define the consulting portfolio for the Transformation Practice (Operating Model Evolution, Digital Roadmapping, Automation Strategy, AI‑enabled Process Redesign, Change Enablement)
 * Create methodologies, frameworks, playbooks, templates, and assessment tools
 * Develop repeatable transformation narratives, value cases, and delivery blueprints
 * Own end-to-end delivery of high‑impact transformation engagements
 * Conduct discovery, assess business processes, analyze maturity gaps, and design future‑state operating models
 * Build transformation roadmaps covering technology, people, governance, and execution
 * Structure MVPs and pilot programs that validate and scale change
 * Work cross‑functionally with AI, data, and engineering teams to bring strategies to life
 * Collaborate with Sales to identify opportunities and co-create proposals
 * Lead workshops, C‑level conversations, diagnostic sessions, and visioning exercises
 * Translate business priorities into actionable transformation initiatives that win projects and deepen client relationships
 * Support recruitment of strategists and consultants
 * Mentor and develop junior profiles
 * Create a culture of excellence, feedback, ownership, and client-first thinking
 * Ensure the team scales with strong methodological pillars and delivery consistency
 * Serve as the bridge between business transformation and Plain Concepts' technical capabilities
 * Integrate AI, data, cloud, and digital platforms into transformation initiatives
 * Ensure transformation roadmaps are actionable and implementation-ready
 * Collaborate closely with Engineering, AI, and Product leads to design executable solutions
   
   

Requirements


 * Experience:
 * 8+ years of experience in consulting, start- or grown-up, venture building with focus product development, management and strategy
 * Proven track record in delivering complex projects and leading client engagements
 * Generalist with a strong foundation in product management, go-to-market strategies
 * Skills & Expertise:
 * Excellent methodological knowledge; capable of creating and improving frameworks
 * Strong understanding of consulting environments and client dynamics
 * Technical literacy to engage in tech-driven projects (AI, automation, etc.)
 * Outstanding communication and stakeholder management skills
 * Proficient in business development and crafting tailored client solutions
 * Fluent in English, optimally strong/native in Spanish.
 * Characteristics:
 * Results-oriented and entrepreneurial mindset
 * Passion for innovation, growth, and driving tangible impact
 * Highly organized, with the ability to manage multiple priorities
 * Based in Spain is mandatory. Barcelona or Madrid would be perfect!
   
   

Benefits


 * Salary determined by the market and your experience 🤑
 * Flexible schedule 😎
 * Fully remote work (optional) 🌍
 * Flexible compensation (restaurant, transport, and childcare) ✌
 * Medical and dental insurance (completely free of charge for the employee) 🚑
 * Individual budget for training or equipment and free Microsoft certifications 📚
 * English lessons 🗽
 * Birthday day off 🌴🥳
 * Monthly bonus for electricity and Internet expenses at home 💻
 * Discount on gym plan and sports activities 🔝
 * Plain Camp (annual team-building event) 🎪
 * Extra perks: events attendance and speakers, welcome pack, baby basket, Christmas basket, discount portal for employees ➕ The pleasure of always working with the latest technological tools!
   
   

What is Plain Concepts?

Plain Concepts is a leading professional services company that provides IT consulting and technology services to global mid-size and large companies. Our enthusiasm for technology drives us toward finding the best solutions for our customers. Using the latest technology, we build comprehensive and innovative solutions with our customers in their transformation processes. We design data-driven, cloud-native applications enhanced with disruptive solutions, including AI and eXtended Reality.

Plain Concepts develops customized projects working side-by-side with its customers. The company is exceptionally committed to agile values and developing high-quality software for its customers. We are a multinational company with offices in Spain, USA, Germany and Romania. Currently, we employ c. 500 FTEs, 75%+ of them highly skilled developers. Plain Concepts has a diversified and blue-chip customer base of more than 150 clients, including big brand names such as Nestlé, Lidl, Microsoft, Repsol, and Telefonica.

The company's shareholding comprises existing employees and ABE Capital Partners, a Spanish Private Equity house that acts as a financial sponsor. ABE became a shareholder in December 2020. ABE is a Spanish Private Equity house established by three seasoned professionals with extensive experience in Private Equity. With more than 50 years of acumulative experience, 35 of them in private equity, the Investment Team has outstanding experience managing and advising portfolio companies in the most attractive sectors.

Having the backing of a financial sponsor like ABE allows Plain Concepts to have an ambitious growth plan to become a leading European player in IT professional services on the back of unparallel technical expertise.

As the next chapter of our growth story we have committed to building a next-generation business consulting practice that delivers measurable impact at the intersection of innovation, technology, and strategy. Our focus is on generating growth and efficiency for our clients through tailored solutions, new products and business models across core business optimization, new ventures, and cutting-edge technologies. For this the consulting unit will enhance Plain Concept's service portfolio with business, innovation and product practices to deliver projects end to end in close collaboration with our tech-teams.

What we do at Plain Concepts?

We pride ourselves on being a 100% technical team, dedicated to crafting custom projects from scratch, offering expert technical consultancy, and providing top-tier training.


 * Our approach goes beyond traditional outsourcing;
 * we focus on creating value together with our clients.
 * Our teams are diverse and multidisciplinary, operating in a flat, collaborative structure.
 * We live and breathe AGILE principles, ensuring flexibility and efficiency in everything we do.
 * Knowledge-sharing is at our core: from supporting each other internally to contributing to the broader tech community through conferences, events, and talks. Innovation drives us — even the boldest ideas are welcome here.
 * Transparency underpins all our relationships, fostering trust and long-term partnerships.
   
   

Want to learn more?

Check out our website! ➡ https://www.plainconcepts.com/

At Plain Concepts, we certainly seek to provide equal opportunities. We want diverse applicants regardless of race, colour, gender, religion, national origin, citizenship, disability, age, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Head of Data and AI
description:
Applus+ is one of the world's leading companies in testing, inspection, and certification, operating in over 70 countries across five continents with a team of more than 27,000 professionals. You can learn more about our projects on our website: http://www.applus.com/es.




We have experienced significant growth in recent years, thanks to our dedicated team, which we consider a key and strategic part of the company’s success.

Would you like to be part of this project?




Applus+ is looking for a Head of Data and AI to join the Corporate IT Department and lead the end-to-end data strategy, establishing a data-driven culture and ensuring data quality, governance, and accessibility to drive decision-making, competitive advantage, and innovation at scale through advanced analytics and Artificial Intelligence.




Main Responsibilities:

1. Data Strategy and Governance

 * Define the architecture, quality, and data governance standards to establish the data infrastructure as a trustworthy and compliant asset, ensuring data is accessible, secure, and useful across the enterprise.

2. Advanced Analytics and Insight Delivery:

 * Lead the Analytics and Data Science teams to convert raw data into strategic insights and predictive models that solve complex business problems and guide key leadership decisions.

3. AI Acceleration and Operationalization (MLOps)

 * Drive the strategy for Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), ensuring the scalable and secure implementation of models into production (MLOps) to automate processes and create new AI-driven products.

4. Technology and Platform Enablement

 * manage and evolve the Data Platform including warehouses, lakes, and visualization tools to ensure the underlying technology is robust, cost-efficient, and capable of supporting the organization's growing demands for Analytics and AI.




Requirements:

 * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Economics or related fields.
 * Minimum of 8 years of experience in similar roles, with at least 5 years in leadership positions.
 * In-depth knowledge of data governance, master data management, IA environments and organizational development
 * Technical understanding of data architecture, databases, interfaces, analytics, ML and gen AI.
 * Strong background in designing data pipelines (Azure environment, custom ETL, Fabric a plus) and reports (powerBI, qlikview)
 * Understanding of regulatory requirements (e.g., GDPR, ISO standards)
 * Expertise in elaborating data and IA business use cases and measuring RoI
 * Strong communication skills for both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
 * Confident in customer-facing discussions and technical solution design.
 * Proven experience in project management and agile methodologies.
 * Strong problem-solving skills and ability to make strategic decisions.
 * Excellent communication and leadership skills.
 * Team player with the ability to manage multiple priorities.
 * Fluent in English.




What We Offer:

 * The opportunity to lead a Corporate Department within the Data area, providing services to all divisions of the group at both national and international levels.
 * Hybrid work model.
 * Professional development opportunities and continuous training.
 * Flexible compensation system (health insurance, transportation, childcare, meal vouchers).
 * Employee parking.
 * Subsidized employee cafeteria.




Applus+ is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to evaluating all applications equally based on abilities, achievements, and experience, regardless of race, nationality, gender, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other classification protected by law.

title: Regional HR Manager - EMEA
description:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.

The company is founder led, profitable and growing.

We are hiring a Regional HR Manager for APAC to join our People team at Canonical.

With 1,200+ colleagues across 75 countries, we require a People function that thinks and acts globally. We're looking for a regional lead that will report into our Global Head of HR who can help build our company as we go through continued growth plans. It's an exciting time to join with the opportunity to help shape and create an HR function for the future. You will lead the APAC team of HR professionals to provide precise, compliant and scalable HR operations to the business, advise and execute on HR issues across the whole employee life-cycle, and partner with senior leadership in your region. You will have an analytical approach, a keen eye for detail and the ability to interpret data trends and themes.

The role entails the individual to:


 * Lead and scale Canonical's regional HR team
 * Deliver precise and compliant HR operations in a timely manner and with the highest degree of accuracy
 * Interact closely with the broader People team to create tight-knit processes across all regions
 * Be accountable for HR processes such as talent development, succession planning, performance assessments, onboarding, culture and engagement initiatives that drive high performing teams
 * Work with senior managers across the business on performance management, organizational design, employee engagement, rewards- and workforce planning
 * Own as well as coach and advise people managers on the full spectrum of employee relations issues, across multiple countries
 * Partner with people managers to support the delivery of appropriate training and development programs
 * Establish a trusted partnership with the business in your region
 * Drive diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives
 * Design new policies and deliver on business-critical HR related projects globally
 * Present at Canonical events to articulate Canonical's HR practices
   
   

What we are looking for in you


 * Exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
 * HR experience leading initiatives across regions within a technology business
 * People management experience
 * Experience in business partnering with senior stakeholders
 * A good balance between leading and executing, in this role you will need to be hands-on involved in the daily HR routines too
 * Experience in working in a remote first organization
 * Able to leverage data to make informed decisions
 * Knowledge and practical implementation of HR practices and employment law across APAC
 * Experience in handling and overseeing complex ER matters across multiple jurisdictions
 * Fluent in business English (written and spoken)
 * Self motivated, organized, accurate, confident, authentic, results-orientated, open-minded, enthusiastic and energetic
 * Willingness to travel up to 4 times a year for internal events
   
   

Nice-to-have skills


 * Experience with immigration policies and mobility processes
 * Professional HR qualification (CIPD/SHRM or other)
 * Facilitation skills
   
   

What we offer colleagues

We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.


 * Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
 * Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
 * Annual compensation review
 * Recognition rewards
 * Annual holiday leave
 * Maternity and paternity leave
 * Employee Assistance Programme
 * Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
 * Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events
   
   

About Canonical

Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.

Canonical is an equal opportunity employer

We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: Business Analyst
description:

title: Senior Product Manager
description:
Oscar Mobility is redefining car rental globally. With 600+ locations across Europe (Denmark, the Netherlands, Spain, Germany, and Belgium) and the US, we are scaling fast and rewriting the rules of mobility. Our asset light, data-driven platform enables dealerships to unlock new revenue streams, turning idle vehicles into profitable rental and subscription services.


We are looking for a Senior Product Manager with the ownership mindset of a mini product founder. You will be end to end responsible for individual products from both sides of our platform - professional car dealerships and workshops using our back office / partner tools, and customers booking rentals via web and app. Your mission is to deeply understand their problems and productize insights to ship experiences that move the needle on growth, conversion, and retention.


You are not a feature factory delivery manager. You are a builder who starts with customer problems, loves experiments and funnel optimization, and still enjoys getting your hands dirty and isn’t too proud to write tickets and dig into data.


Location: Hybrid in Spain (offices in Malaga)


Employment Type: Full time


Start: Asap


Tasks


What you will do


   
   
 * Support product strategy and roadmap: Help develop our marketplace product vision and strategy as well as translate company goals into a clear roadmap with clear priorities
   
   
 * Understand marketplace needs: Speak to our partners to understand their workflows, incentives, and friction points in our back office tooling, to our customers to understand different segments, journeys and pain points as well as to our internal teams.
   
   
 * Move the needle: Balance 0 to 1 bets with 1 to n optimization of existing flows and experiences through cross-functional experimentation across pricing presentation, packages, and messaging
   
   
 * Piece together the data points: Turn qualitative insight and quantitative data into crisp problem statements, hypotheses, and product decisions while defining and tracking KPIs
   
   
 * Optimize our funnel: Own key funnels across the business, from landing page to search, vehicle selection, checkout, and post booking experience
   
   
 * Act as sparring partner to tech: Work day to day with Engineering to turn problems into solutions, from low fidelity concepts to production ready features (incl. writing clear product specs, tickets, and acceptance criteria)
   
   
 * Own and evolve core workflows: Take on development of key products by defining simple, lightweight product practices that support discovery, prioritization, and learning without overloading the team with process.
   
   


Requirements


Who you are


   
   
 * You bring a builder mindset and, ideally, have founder or start-up experience
   
   
 * You have 5+ years of experience in Product Management in high growth tech, ideally in marketplaces, mobility, travel, or a similar space
   
   
 * You have owned core user journeys end to end from discovery through launch and iteration
   
   
 * You are comfortable both with the qualitative (running own discovery through interviews, shadowing, ticket analysis, and journey mapping) and quantitative (working with analytics tools and funnels, A/B testing, CVR optimization, defining metrics, and running experiments)
   
   
 * You have strong product sense and UX intuition for both B2C experiences and internal tools
   
   
 * You move fast, unblock other, as well as are hands on and pragmatic, equally happy writing tickets and acceptance criteria or discussing strategy with leadership
   
   
 * You are comfortable mocking up flows and wireframes, ideally using AI to push for simplicity and clarity
   
   
 * You are a strong communicator and can bring people along on decisions, incl. when you need to say no
   
   
 * You are fluent in English. German, Dutch, Danish, or Spanish are a plus
   
   


Benefits


Why Oscar Mobility


   
   
 * Market leader in flexible mobility: We have built a powerhouse dealership network that operates profitably across urban and rural markets
   
   
 * Scalable asset light model: Our rental and subscription business has a €50B+ market potential that no one else is catering to
   
   
 * Unique B2B2C marketplace challenge: Build product for both professional partners and end users, with real world operations attached
   
   
 * Vision to build a global mobility champion: We are rapidly expanding our dealership network and software driven platform across Europe and the US
   
   


What we offer


   
   
 * Competitive salary with performance based incentives aligned with the impact you create
   
   
 * High-impact, senior role where you own meaningful parts of the business rather than a narrow feature set
   
   
 * Direct exposure to founders and C-Level with a clear development path
   
   
 * A fast moving, execution first culture where outcomes matter more than process
   
   
 * Hybrid or remote friendly setup with HQ in Malaga
   
   
 * International environment across multiple European markets and the U.S., with the chance to visit our locations and partners
   
   


Ready to build the product core of a global mobility marketplace? Apply now. We encourage you to apply even if you do not believe you meet every single qualification.

title: DIRECTOR GENERAL (TECH)
description:
Empresa tecnológica consolidada, especializada en soluciones ERP y servicios digitales para pymes industriales, busca incorporar un/a Director/a General para liderar una nueva etapa de crecimiento y evolución estratégica.




Misión

Liderar la compañía con un fuerte enfoque comercial y de desarrollo de negocio, impulsando el crecimiento de la línea ERP y la apertura de nuevas líneas tecnológicas (ciberseguridad, inteligencia artificial y servicios digitales avanzados), asegurando una gestión rentable, cercana y alineada con el entorno empresarial local.




Responsabilidades

 * Definir y ejecutar la estrategia global de la compañía.
 * Liderar la estrategia comercial y participar activamente en la captación y cierre de operaciones estratégicas.
 * Desarrollar nuevas líneas de negocio tecnológicas.
 * Mantener relación directa con clientes clave.
 * Impulsar oportunidades de cross-selling y up-selling.
 * Traducir necesidades funcionales del cliente en propuestas de valor.
 * Liderar un equipo mayoritariamente técnico con orientación a negocio.
 * Supervisar resultados financieros, márgenes y rentabilidad.
 * Garantizar la correcta ejecución de proyectos y la satisfacción del cliente.




Perfil requerido

 * Experiencia sólida como Director/a General, Director/a Comercial o Director/a de Unidad de Negocio en empresa tecnológica.
 * Fuerte background en venta de soluciones tecnológicas B2B.
 * Experiencia en entornos ERP y análisis funcional de procesos empresariales.
 * Experiencia en creación y lanzamiento de nuevas líneas de negocio.
 * Capacidad para liderar equipos técnicos con visión estratégica.
 * Residencia en Osona o fuerte vinculación con la zona.
 * Mentalidad emprendedora y orientación clara a resultados.




Se ofrece

 * Rol clave con impacto directo en la estrategia y el crecimiento.
 * Alta autonomía y visibilidad.
 * Proyecto estable con ambición de expansión.
 * Retribución competitiva vinculada a resultados.

title: School Director Carabanchel
description:
En Kids&Us somos la chispa que enciende en las nuevas generaciones el deseo de descubrir el mundo a través de sus propios sentidos. Nos comprometemos diariamente con pasión a cambiar el panorama de la educación del idioma inglés, construyendo un mundo lleno de experiencias educativas de impacto.

Estamos buscando un/a Director/a de centro para una de nuestras escuelas en Carabanchel con experiencia comercial y conocimientos pedagógicos y con mucha experiencia en gestión de equipos.

Como Director/a de centro, te daremos todas las herramientas de formación necesarias para desarrollar aún más tus habilidades en ventas, gestión de la estructura y del personal, servicio al cliente y aspectos económicos.

Serás responsable de:


 * Supervisar todas las operaciones diarias y gestionar a 360° el centro Kids&Us, tanto desde un punto de vista comercial como académico.
 * Aumentar las ventas a través de la implementación de estrategias de venta específicas, monitoreando con frecuencia nuestros KPI de venta.
 * Dar la bienvenida a los clientes y construir relaciones con los padres y madres, así como realizar actividades de asesoramiento al cliente y actividades que involucren a las familias.
 * Colaborar con la oficina de marketing de la sede principal para aumentar la conciencia de marca, la credibilidad y la cuota de mercado de Kids&Us.
   
   

¿Qué aptitudes y actitudes buscamos?


 * Capacidad para planificar actividades.
 * Excelentes habilidades en el asesoramiento al cliente y en la construcción de relaciones comerciales.
 * Experiencia previa en el ámbito de la educación, especialmente en la gestión de equipos y la consolidación de los mismos, así como en la gestión o dirección de un centro académico.
 * Experiencia en ventas y comercial
 * Capacidad para gestionar y motivar a nuestros profesores.
 * Nivel C1/C2 de inglés.
 * Vehículo propio
 * Tener un Certificado de Delitos Sexuales sin antecedentes. OBTENCIÓN DEL CERTIFICADO DE DELITOS DE NATURALEZA SEXUAL EN ESPAÑA
   
   

¿Y qué te ofrece Kids&Us?


 * Contrato indefinido, a tiempo completo.
 * Remuneración variable por objetivos.
 * Semana laboral de lunes a viernes (algunos fines de semana cuando sea necesario).
 * Formación inicial y continua.
 * Ambiente joven y estimulante, con fuertes posibilidades de crecimiento.
   
   

Si crees que Kids&Us es la escuela de la que te gustaría formar parte, ¡nos encantaría recibir noticias tuyas!

Kids&Us apoya la diversidad y se compromete a atraer candidatos/as calificados que a su vez abrazan y aprecian la diversidad y la inclusión. Kids&Us es un empleador que respeta la igualdad de oportunidades. No aceptamos discriminación o persecución de ningún tipo. Apoyamos la singularidad en cada uno y animamos a cada individuo a ser él mismo.

Cláusula informativa: Responsable del tratamiento: Kids&Us English, S.L. con domicilio en avenida Tudela, 12, 08242 Manresa (Barcelona). Finalidad: gestión de CV, perfiles y candidaturas profesionales. Destinatarios: Kids&Us English, S.L. y toda su red de centros y franquicias adscritos. Derechos: tiene derecho de acceso, rectificación, supresión, oposición, limitación del tratamiento, portabilidad de los datos, a no estar sometido a decisiones individuales automatizadas y a la revocación. Para ejercer los anteriores derechos, así como para cualquier consulta relacionada con el tratamiento de sus datos, puede dirigirse al responsable del tratamiento al correo electrónico info@kidsandus.com.

Tiene más información en la página https://www.kidsandus.es/es/footer/politica-de-privacidad.

title: People & Culture Director - Hotel Nobu Ibiza Bay
description:
About The Property

Located on the shores of Talamanca Bay, just few minutes from Ibiza Town, Nobu Hotel Ibiza Bay is a luxury lifestyle destination where laid-back sophistication meets world-class service. With a focus on wellness, gastronomy, and personalized experiences, our hotel offers 152 rooms and suites, a Six Senses Spa, and exceptional culinary concepts including Nobu Restaurant, Chambao by the Beach, and Bay Café. As part of the global Nobu Hospitality brand, we are passionate about creating memorable moments for our guests and fostering a dynamic, collaborative environment for our team.

About The Role

Lead and manage the functions of the People & Culture department, ensuring the development and implementation of effective strategies to attract, retain, and develop talent. Promote a positive, equitable, and productive work environment by aligning human resources policies and practices with the company’s strategic objectives.

What You’ll Be Responsible For:

Operational Functions:


 * Develop action plans to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the People & Culture department specifically, and all other departments in general.
 * Participate and collaborate in decision-making processes during Executive Committee and Ownership meetings.
 * Develop and execute People & Culture (P&C) strategies that support the company’s short- and long-term business objectives.
 * Ensure compliance with labor laws and applicable regulations.
 * Oversee the timely and accurate completion of payroll processing and payments.
 * Manage employee and labor union relations.
 * Communicate and negotiate short-, medium-, and long-term strategies with the works council.
 * As a member of the Executive Committee, participate in the preparation of the Company’s Annual Investment Plan by identifying departmental investment needs (CAPEX).
 * Organize pre-opening activities.
 * Coordinate the onboarding of key team members and oversee the onboarding of the rest of the staff.
 * Lead team-building activities.
 * Deliver and coordinate training sessions for department heads before and during the opening phase.
 * Resolve labor conflicts and ensure disciplinary matters are handled fairly and in accordance with company policy. Apply disciplinary measures when necessary.
 * Supervise the development of the Annual Training Plan and ensure its proper implementation.
 * Supervise and manage the organization’s talent. Oversee recruitment, selection, hiring, and retention processes.
 * Lead and encourage the People & Culture team to create initiatives that promote employee well-being and satisfaction.
 * Provide support to managers across departments. Listen to and understand the challenges they face, offering solutions and guidance to improve team performance and satisfaction.
 * Facilitate the resolution of interdepartmental conflicts and ensure effective collaboration between departments.
 * Maintain a cordial and collaborative relationship with partner entities, payroll agencies, and public administrations, ensuring the creation of strategic synergies.
   
   

Leadership Functions


 * Identify and develop the talent of team members, providing them with the necessary support and tools to reach their full potential.
 * Participate in the recruitment and onboarding processes for staff in their area, as well as collaborate in their training and development.
 * Conduct performance evaluations for the team under their supervision, creating opportunities for growth and development.
 * Ensure effective relationships and communication with other departments, promoting collaboration to execute area activities efficiently.
 * Lead and engage team members, aligning them with objectives and motivating them to respond effectively to the hotel’s needs while developing their professional skills.
 * Evaluate productivity, performance, and achievement of objectives, recognizing successes and addressing attitudes or behaviors that require improvement.
 * Foster collaborative work by identifying and leveraging information, knowledge, and experience—both personal and from the team—to execute tasks more efficiently.
   
   

Management Functions


 * Implement and manage, within their area of responsibility, the company’s quality and environmental management system, and provide training to staff on appropriate practices to ensure compliance.
 * Supervise and participate in the implementation and tracking of quality system indicators for their department/area.
 * Communicate and oversee compliance with current Occupational Health and Safety regulations in the execution of tasks across different roles.
 * Communicate and oversee compliance with current Equality regulations.
   
   

Strategic Functions


 * Foster and maintain Organizational Culture and Climate, promoting an inclusive, diverse, and safe work environment.
 * Encourage and facilitate a positive work atmosphere that enables teams to work energetically and enthusiastically toward achieving objectives.
 * Lead organizational change initiatives, ensuring that employees are prepared for and committed to transitions.
 * Propose and implement new compensation policies that ensure fairness and equity.
 * Participate in the strategic planning of the hotel, aligning People & Culture department initiatives with overall business objectives.
 * Contribute to the hotel’s overall budgeting process.
 * Establish new strategic relationships and maintain existing partnerships with European schools for collaboration and the training of interns.
   
   

Required Education And Experience


 * Degree: University studies in Labor Relations, Business Administration, or Organizational Psychology, with specializations in Human Talent Management or HR.
 * Computer Skills: Advanced proficiency in office software.
 * Experience: Between 4 and 6 years in similar positions.
 * Languages: Ability to understand, read, and communicate effectively in both Spanish and English, orally and in writing.
   
   

Interdepartmental Relations

All Departments

Ready to Make Your Mark?

If you're passionate about luxury service and want to grow within an international brand, we’d love to meet you. Apply now and become part of our exceptional team in Ibiza.

Playa Real S.A. is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive environment and do not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, religion, disability, age, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: DIRECTOR/A CORPORATIVO - R2 HOTELES
description:
GRUPO R2 HOTELES




selecciona para Canarias y Baleares




DIRECTOR/A CORPORATIVO




Nexo selecciona para el Grupo Hotelero R2, Director/a Corporativo con base en Fuerteventura. Como representante de la propiedad en España, será el enlace estratégico entre R2 Group / Schauinsland Reisen y la gestora Summum Hotel Group. El objetivo es maximizar la rentabilidad, asegurar la gobernanza y potenciar el valor de la cartera de 12 hoteles. Ejercerá la máxima autoridad sobre los activos desde una perspectiva de Asset Management, sin intervenir en la operativa diaria.




Sus funciones principales serán:




 * Representación de la propiedad y gobernanza
 * Supervisión del rendimiento de los activos y la cartera.
 * Supervisar el rendimiento de todos los activos frente a los presupuestos aprobados, planes de negocio, previsiones (forecasts) y Kpis.
 * Revisar y auditar los informes financieros y operativos presentados por la gestora.
 * Identificar riesgos, bajo rendimiento y oportunidades de creación de valor.
 * Proponer medidas correctivas u optimizaciones al operador y reportar resultados a la propiedad.
 * Responsabilidad financieray capex.
 * Supervisión contractual y cumplimiento.
 * Monitorizar el cumplimiento total del Contrato de Gestión Hotelera (HMA).
 * Supervisar las obligaciones contractuales, estándares de rendimiento y requisitos de reporte.
 * Coordinar revisiones, enmiendas, renovaciones o renegociaciones de contratos según sea necesario.
 * Coordinarse con asesores legales, financieros, técnicos y fiscales en nombre del propietario.
 * Expansión, desarrollo y crecimiento estratégico en colaboración con Summun.
 * Asesorar a la propiedad en: oportunidades de expansión, nuevas adquisiciones, desinversiones, desarrollo o proyectos de reposicionamiento.
 * Participar en estudios de viabilidad, procesos de due diligence y análisis de inversión.
 * Contribuir e impulsar iniciativas relacionadas con proyectos y apoyar la estrategia de crecimiento a largo plazo en España
 * Representación Institucional
 * Actuar como la cara institucional de R2 Group en España.
 * Mantener relaciones con autoridades públicas, instituciones financieras, socios estratégicos y asesores.
 * Proteger y mejorar la reputación del propietario en el mercado hotelero español.




Formación: Titulación Universitaria, se valorará máster de posgrado en escuelas reconocidas.




Experiencia de, al menos, diez años, en:

 * Gestión de activos hoteleros (Asset Management), como Director General de Clúster o en estructuras hoteleras del lado de la propiedad.
 * Trayectoria demostrada trabajando con operadores hoteleros externos (terceros).
 * Sólido conocimiento de cuentas de resultados (P&L), balances, KPIs y métricas de inversión.
 * Conocimiento profundo del mercado hotelero español.




Habilidades y Competencias

 * Fuertes capacidades financieras, analíticas y estratégicas.
 * Alta capacidad de gestión de interlocutores (stakeholders) y de negociación.
 * Capacidad para representar los intereses de la propiedad con autoridad y diplomacia.
 * Mentalidad independiente, estructurada y orientada a resultados




Idiomas

 * Inglés: Fluido
 * Español: Fluido
 * Alemán: Se valorará positivamente




Buscamos a un profesional con una sólida trayectoria, con ganas de establecerse en un proyecto con continuidad y desarrollo.




La posición cuenta con una compensación económica altamente competitiva, fijo y variable en función de resultados, vivienda y transporte.




Nexo garantiza el trato individualizado y confidencial.
workType: Finance, Project Management, and Quality Assurance

title: Senior HR Manager
description:
BIC Graphic is a division of the BIC Group specializing in the customization of BIC® products. Based in Tarragona, Spain since 1995, we are recognized for our commitment to innovation and quality, proudly upholding the legacy of the iconic BIC® brand, known worldwide for over 75 years for offering essential, affordable, and high-quality products.

Our diverse portfolio includes BIC®’s iconic writing instruments and lighters, and strategic collaborations with other brands. At BIC Graphic, we live our core values of innovation, customer proximity, and social responsibility—sustainability is not just a goal, but a mindset embedded in everything we do.

As a leader in promotional marketing solutions, we serve clients across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa with a strong focus on delivering creative and sustainable branding solutions that elevate our customers’ brands.

As a Senior HR Manager for BIC Graphic, you are a key strategic partner to the leadership team, driving the people agenda across all functions including Operations, Commercial, Marketing and Corporate.

You oversee all HR activities for BIC Graphic team members based primarily in Tarragona, as well as those distributed across various locations in Europe. You provide direction and support to functional leaders, ensuring alignment of the organization’s structure, talent, and culture with strategic priorities.

Working closely with the European HR Leadership Team, you contribute to the pan-European HR roadmap, participating in cross-border initiatives such as leadership development, succession planning, organizational design, and employee engagement. You ensure operational excellence in all areas of HR, including compliance with local labor legislation, talent acquisition, performance management, training, people development and HSE.

BUSINESS PARTNERING


 * Provides guidance/counselling on the most efficient and adequate organization in line with the business goals and the capabilities needed
 * Contributes to the Operating Plans, the budget cycles and reviews of each of the countries/functions supported
 * Builds strong relationships with works councils and employee representatives, ensuring a climate of social dialogue, trust, and constructive engagement.
 * Leads negotiations and consultation processes as required, while ensuring compliance with legal frameworks.
 * Ensures full compliance with local labor laws, health & safety regulations, and internal policies.
 * Partners with Health, Safety & Security teams to maintain a safe and compliant working environment and proactively address any workforce-related risks.
 * Elaborates and drives a people plan (organization, team member development, succession) for his/her perimeter, in synch with the Europe HR roadmap
   
   

PEOPLE’S CHAMPION


 * Develops a good knowledge of the team members of his/her perimeter and acts as the HR key contact
 * Bring support and coaching to managers in their team management: to better deliver performance feedback, build development plans, create team dynamics…
 * Contribute to identifying talents within the organization and developmental opportunities
 * Drives the people/organization communication agenda throughout the year
   
   

CENTER OF EXPERTISE COUNTERPART

Leverage on the COEs HR to deliver the best experience to our candidates and team members and more specifically:


 * Engages with Talent Acquisition on each hiring process and provide feedback to the hiring managers
 * Manages our compensation /job classification system
 * Monitors our benefits offer across countries
 * Proactively shares his/her specific expectations for support/project for the Cluster and the functional/COE departments
   
   

HR PROCESSES AND COMMUNICATION


 * Designs the annual training plan based on the training needs detection and development plans of team members, in alignment with the business orientations
 * Runs the Total Rewards agenda with the objective of differentiating performance, recognizing talent, addressing internal and external consistency and be able to attract and retain talent
 * Completes the Talent Review and Succession plans
 * Deploys Group process of Engagement survey and supporting managers for action plans
 * Provides a constant view on headcount and HR costs in liaise with Finance
 * Supports the Management Team for underperformance management and disciplinary actions (or termination of contracts)
 * Leads the Communication strategy within BGE and externally, promoting BGE’s employer branding. Liaises with the Europe Communications team to ensure internal communication on team members and people initiatives.
   
   

DIVERSITY, EQUALITY & INCLUSION (DEI)


 * Implements DEI policies and designs actions to promote diversity and inclusion within the company
 * Ensures alignment with company´s values and current legislation
 * Promotes an inclusive leadership across the organization
   
   

EMPLOYEE ENGAGEMENT


 * Promotes the participation in the engagement survey and deploys results across the organization
 * Creates and fosters action plans to correct biases and improve engagement
 * Assesses risks and opportunities regarding results obtained in internal surveys
 * Runs employer branding activities: EVP, communication, candidate experience, ambassadors
 * Implements wellbeing and recognition activities
   
   

Qualifications:


 * Relevant studies in HR, law or BA.
 * Solid knowledge of social legislation and employee relations, with proven experience in an industrial or factory environment.
 * Minimum of 8 years of professional experience as an HR Business Partner or HR Manager in Spain, ideally within industrial or manufacturing environments.
 * Fluent in both English and Spanish. Catalan is a must.
 * Good interpersonal and influencing skills
 * Ability to challenge and have an impact on decisions
 * Strong business acumen
 * Able to work autonomously
 * Very good team player
 * A sense of service to internal clients
 * Pragmatic, structured and with excellent organizational skills
   
   

There’s never been a better time to join BIC Graphic. As a dynamic division of the BIC Group with a strong vision for the future, we offer exciting opportunities in a collaborative and forward-thinking work environment. At BIC Graphic, we’re passionate about our people—their well-being, growth, and development are at the heart of what we do, and our benefits reflect that commitment.

Join us and be part of a team that’s redefining promotional marketing through innovation and sustainability. Let’s shape the future—together.

BIC Corporation is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, protected Veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law.

BIC is not seeking assistance or accepting unsolicited resumes from search firms for this employment opportunity. Regardless of past practice, all resumes submitted by search firms to any team member at BIC via email, or directly to a BIC team member in any form without a valid written search agreement in place for that position will be deemed the sole property of BIC, and no fee will be paid in the event the candidate is hired by BIC as a result of the referral or through other means.

title: General Manager - Spain
description:
Job Title: General Manager – Southern Europe (Spain)

Location: Ceuta

Reports to: Senior Director – Southern Europe

PokerStars Southern Europe: A growth story you can be part of

PokerStars is part of Flutter Entertainment, the world’s largest sports betting and iGaming operator, with market leading positions in the US and across the world.

As part of our brand’s continual evolution, we’re dialing up the focus on PokerStars Southern Europe, covering France, Portugal and Spain. We combine the ambition of a fast-growing business with the stability and backing of Flutter’s global scale.

We’re building a new leadership team to shape this next chapter, and we’re looking for energised, collaborative, commercially minded leaders to help us set the vision, craft the strategy, and build on our winning culture.

You won’t just join – you’ll play a key role in shaping the next chapter for PokerStars SEU, with the freedom to make your mark.

Role Overview

Reporting to the Senior Director – Southern Europe, this position will be responsible for the P&L and performance of our Spanish market, overseeing a business that generates more than €100 m a year in revenues. Working primarily on the PokerStars brand, you will also oversee operations for our Betfair business in Spain, providing strategic guidance and day‑to‑day decision making as part of their leadership team too. Your day‑to‑day responsibilities will focus on driving commercial performance and improving the customer experience, leading a multi‑vertical team across Customer Value Management, Content and CX, Product and Proposition Development, Customer Care and Compliance. You will also work closely with the Marketing Director for Southern Europe to ensure we have an integrated and credible plan for customer acquisition growth. We’re looking for a dynamic leader with a strong digital background, capable of both thinking strategically and driving short‑term performance targets. You’ll need excellent communication skills, enjoy working in a diverse working environment and be a very effective stakeholder manager.

What You’ll Do

Strategic Leadership & Country Oversight


 * Full P&L ownership for PokerStars Spain, setting out clear goals and strategy, in line with our ambitions and targets for the market.
 * Develop and implement a credible plan for achieving those goals, covering key business levers around Promotions, Marketing, Customer Operations & Product Development.
 * Drive effective resource allocation, balancing adherence to strategy and plan with short-term performance trends and targets.
 * Develop a deep and coherent analysis and understanding of the local market and its consumers.
 * Foster collaboration between Product, Marketing, Acquisition, CVM, Customer Care, and Compliance teams within the country.
 * Oversee the day-to-day running of the Betfair Spain business.
   
   

Financial & Performance Management


 * Set and monitor financial KPIs, ensuring the country achieves revenue and profitability targets.
 * Identify and capitalize on new revenue streams while optimizing existing business lines.
 * Enhance efficiency in customer value management (CVM), acquisition, and retention strategies.
 * Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning for the country.
   
   

Commercial Planning


 * Lay down a commercial plan for all verticals within the market that reconciles to goals and objectives for the market.
 * Leverage consumer insight and analytics to build high impact plans to deliver the overall strategy.
 * Identify the key initiatives to drive commercial outcomes, create an outcome focused plan and working environment to deliver them.
   
   

Content Management


 * Oversee content localization and UX/UI efforts, ensuring alignment with business priorities and local market needs.
 * Coordinate cross-functional workstreams, enabling consistent, high-impact content and user experiences across regions.
   
   

Customer Value Management


 * Directly oversee promotional investment plans and execution.
 * Build a compelling customer contact strategy for players in the market.
 * Closely monitor retention and customer growth KPIs and optimize plans accordingly.
 * Create a collaborative and dynamic working culture with key delivery partners across Product, Marketing and Analytics.
   
   

Integrated Planning and Delivery with Key Partners


 * Work closely with Marketing, Customer Operations and Product teams to ensure we have an integrated plan for the market.
   
   

Leadership & Talent Development


 * Build and lead a high-performing country leadership team, ensuring strong execution and focus at the local level.
 * Develop talent engagement and succession plans to foster leadership growth.
 * Champion a culture of collaboration, innovation and accountability across all teams.
   
   

Regulatory & Stakeholder Management


 * Ensure full compliance with local iGaming regulations and corporate governance policies.
 * Build effective relationships with local regulatory bodies, operators, and key external stakeholders.
 * Manage relationships with local partners, affiliates, and governmental authorities to support business growth.
 * Champion player protection, promote responsible gaming practices, and embed customer wellbeing at the core of our operations.
   
   

Market Expansion & Brand Positioning


 * Identify new market opportunities within the country, overseeing local go-to-market strategies and customer-centric growth strategies.
   
   

What You’ll Bring

Industry Expertise & Market Knowledge


 * Extensive leadership experience, ideally as a Director, GM, or similar role within the iGaming industry.
 * Experience in managing country-specific operations with a focus on Poker, Sports, and Casino.
   
   

Financial & Strategic Acumen


 * Strong business acumen, with a proven track record of driving profitability and scaling operations.
 * Exceptional analytical and data-driven decision-making skills.
 * Ability to develop and execute a comprehensive country strategy that aligns with regional and corporate goals.
   
   

Leadership & Communication


 * Exceptional people management skills, with experience leading and mentoring senior teams.
 * Strong stakeholder management abilities, with the capacity to influence at all levels.
 * Excellent negotiation and communication skills in English and the local language.
   
   

Flexibility & Compliance


 * Experience navigating highly regulated markets and ensuring strict adherence to local laws.
 * Ability to travel within the region as required.
   
   

What You’ll Get


 * Discretionary annual performance bonus
 * 30 days paid leave
 * Health and dental insurance for you, your partner and your children (if you all live at the same address)
 * Personal life assurance
 * The option to join our company pension scheme
 * A personal interest allowance to let you learn something new or pursue a hobby
 * External learning support of up to £2,000 or equivalent in local currency, dedicated 4 learning “Power Hours” every month during office time, full access to the Udemy and Mindtools platforms, in-house leadership program and many other training opportunities for developing your skills and progressing your career
 * Looking to extend your family? You will receive a cash gift of 1,000 EUR for your new addition whilst working for us
 * 26 weeks primary carer leave at 100% pay & 4 weeks secondary carer leave pay at 100% pay

title: Head of Research
description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Growth at Revolut is where strategy, data, and creativity come together. The team drives expansion by launching in new markets, accelerating acquisition, and boosting engagement and retention. They turn insights into action, working across teams and channels to scale what works and experiment with what’s next.

We're looking for a Head of Research to play a pivotal role in shaping our understanding of consumer needs, market trends, and competitive dynamics. Your insights will directly influence product positioning, market strategy, and campaign development, ensuring that every move we make is backed by rigorous research and a deep understanding of our customers.

Up to turn data into impactful decisions and help scale a global fintech? Let's get in touch.

What You'll Be Doing


 * Supporting brand tracking and consumer research programmes across key markets
 * Designing and implementing high-quality surveys tailored to campaign and brand insight needs
 * Partnering with Marketing Managers to ensure research effectively supports campaign planning and measurement
 * Assisting in managing survey panels, ensuring data quality and timely delivery
 * Organising and executing ad hoc research requests and stakeholder briefs
 * Integrating research data into dashboards and analytic workflows
   
   

What You'll Need


 * Proven experience in market research, consumer insights, or a similar analytical role, preferably within a high-growth or tech-driven environment
 * Solid knowledge of both quantitative and qualitative research methods, with hands-on experience in survey design and data analysis
 * Familiarity with brand tracking tools, survey platforms, and panel management
 * The ability to translate research findings into actionable insights that support marketing strategies and campaign performance
 * Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with experience working cross-functionally with marketing, product, or data teams
 * Impeccable attention to detail and organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects and meet tight deadlines
 * Experience with data visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker) and a basic understanding of analytic workflows
 * A proactive mindset, curiosity, and a passion for understanding customer behaviour and brand performance
   
   

Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


 * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
 * Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.
   
   

We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: AI Director - Machine Learning | North America | Canada | Europe | Fully Remote
description:
What we are looking for:

We are looking for an experienced and passionate AI/Machine Learning Director to become a defining member of our Engineering team at Escape Velocity Entertainment. You will research, develop, and prototype AI/ML solutions to unlock new experiences and push the boundaries of our ambitious project.

Requirements

What we will do together:


 * Oversee ML bot research, development, and implementation
 * Identify and evaluate opportunities to use generative AI across the production pipeline
 * Gather AI-related ideas and needs from teams and convert them into actionable plans
 * Drive execution of approved AI initiatives, prototypes, and workflow improvements
 * Explore and prototype local models trained on internal data
 * Develop guidelines and best practices for agentic AI systems (e.g., Windwalk, Antigravity)
 * Educate directors and teams on AI capabilities, limitations, and practical usage
 * Up to date knowledge with emerging AI models, tools, and industry trends
 * Understand major AI models and clearly explain their strengths, weaknesses, and applicability
   
   

What you will bring:


 * Previously held an AI/ML Lead/Manager or equivalent position
 * Solid experience with Python and C++ with strong expertise with ML frameworks like PyTorch and TensorFlow
 * Strong communication skills for engaging both technical and non-technical audiences
 * Expertise in AI/ML theory, algorithms, and system design
 * A collaborative, mentoring-oriented mindset and passion for solving problems
 * Game development experience is a plus but is not required
   
   

Benefits

Interested... a bit about Escape Velocity:

Escape Velocity is a team of passionate, talented developers working at a studio that offers excellent benefits, on what we believe is a once-in-a-career project with a committed publisher. But that doesn't necessarily make us unique. What does make us special? We value the time, energy, and talent of our players and our colleagues. We want every hour spent developing and playing our games to be an incredibly rewarding and worthwhile use of your time. To that end, we are ‘Fully Remote' and support team members nearly anywhere in the world. We prioritize honesty, even when that means sharing bad news. We strive to give everyone autonomy so they can determine how best to work and play, and we care more about what you bring to the game and studio than how many hours you're clocking. We're dedicated to building a culture of play, which not only makes us better game makers, but also strengthens the bonds between us all. We believe in constant improvement across all areas - from the game itself to our production design - and are constantly re-evaluating how we spend our time to ensure that we're not wasting yours and we're all always working on the most important things.

A few benefits you can expect from us:


 * A generous PTO allowance
 * Summer and Winter holidays studio closure (separate to PTO allowance)
 * Private Medical & Dental Healthcare
 * Private Mental Health scheme
 * A generous annual Health & Wellbeing allowance
 * Discretionary Studio Bonus
 * Top range tech for your home set up
 * Other benefits based on your region
   
   

How to apply:

Click apply and complete an application along with a Resume / CV. If we would like to move forward with your application, a member of the Recruiting Team will reach out to you and guide you through our process.

Escape Velocity is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to hiring, promoting, and compensating employees based on their qualifications and demonstrated ability to perform job responsibilities.

Applications will be considered regardless of age, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, belief, race, or any other protected category.

title: Director of Sales, EU
description:
About the Company




Securitize, the leader in tokenizing real-world assets with $3.7B+ AUM (as of May 5, 2025), is bringing the world on-chain through tokenized funds in partnership with top-tier asset managers, such as Apollo, BlackRock, Hamilton Lane, KKR, and others. Securitize, through its subsidiaries, is a SEC-registered broker dealer, digital transfer agent, fund administrator, and operator of a SEC-regulated Alternative Trading System (ATS).




Securitize is a global, fully remote team consisting of top talent from the blockchain and financial services industries. Having raised $170M overall to date, we are backed by some of the largest names in finance and technology, including BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, Blockchain Capital, MUFG, Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank, Sony Finance, Banco Santander, Coinbase, among others. Securitize has also been recognized as a 2025 Forbes Top 50 Fintech company.




Before applying, we encourage you to visit us to learn more: Website | X/Twitter | LinkedIn




About the Role

Securitize is the global leader in compliant digital securities — bridging traditional finance with blockchain-based innovation. Following our expansion into Europe, we are seeking a Director of Sales, EU to build and lead our European go-to-market strategy, focusing on institutional issuers, asset managers, and digital-first investment firms. This individual will play a key role in scaling Securitize’s presence across the EU and UK, driving adoption of regulated digital securities infrastructure and establishing strategic relationships with leading financial institutions. The ideal candidate brings a deep understanding of EU financial markets, regulatory frameworks (MiFID II, AIFMD, DLT Pilot Regime), and a strong network across private markets and alternative investments.




Responsibilities

Market Leadership & Client Growth

 * Build and execute Securitize’s European sales and partnership strategy.
 * Drive new business with asset managers, private equity firms, fund administrators, and digital asset issuers.
 * Serve as the primary commercial liaison with European clients and strategic partners.
 * Identify and prioritize key markets across the EU and UK for growth and licensing alignment.




Revenue Generation

 * Develop and manage a robust sales pipeline to achieve quarterly and annual revenue targets.
 * Structure and negotiate commercial agreements with clients and intermediaries.
 * Collaborate with product and operations teams to deliver successful token issuances and trading integrations.
 * Establish partnerships with distributors (institutionals both from TradFi and DeFi).




Regulatory & Strategic Alignment

 * Partner with Legal and Compliance to ensure all European operations adhere to local regulatory requirements (MiFID II, AMLD5/6, DLT Pilot).
 * Stay informed on regulatory and market developments to influence Securitize’s European product roadmap.




Cross-Functional Collaboration

 * Work with Marketing to develop regional campaigns, events, and thought leadership initiatives.
 * Provide regular feedback to executive leadership on European market trends, client needs, and competitive positioning.



Qualifications

 * 8–12 years of sales, business development, or relationship management experience in financial services, fintech, or capital markets.
 * Deep familiarity with European regulatory frameworks (MiFID II, DLT Pilot, AIFMD, etc.).
 * 2 years in front office regulated roles providing information on investment products, investment services or ancillary services to customers according to MIFID II in an EU investment firm, bank, asset manager or similar entity.
 * Holder of a degree of the certificates included in CNMV’s technical guide on knowledge of the personnel informing regulated products under MIFID II or equivalent (https://www.cnmv.es/portal/Titulos-Acreditados-Listado.aspx).
 * Proven ability to originate, structure and close complex institutional deals.
 * Established network within private equity, asset management, or investment banking across Europe.
 * Exceptional communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills.
 * Fluency in English; additional European languages (French, German, Spanish) a strong plus.




Preferred Skills

 * Experience with tokenization, digital asset custody, or blockchain-based trading infrastructure.
 * Understanding of secondary market dynamics for private securities.
 * Experience scaling go-to-market efforts for a fintech or financial technology startup.
 * Advanced degree (MBA, MS Finance).



Why Join Us?

Become a part of our rapidly expanding organization and enjoy a supportive and rewarding work environment:

 * Unlimited Paid Time Off (PTO) – Promoting a healthy work-life balance.
 * Equity Grant Opportunities – Share in the success and future growth of the company.
 * Remote Work Flexibility – Work from anywhere while staying connected with a dynamic and collaborative team.

title: DIRECTOR/A DE PLANTA
location: Azagra, Chartered Community of Navarre, Spain
description:
Grupo Virto, empresa alimentaria líder y referente en el sector de las verduras ultracongeladas, busca para su sede central en Azagra un/a Director/a de Planta para dirigir y liderar el centro productivo de referencia dentro del Grupo, con un equipo de más de 400 personas.




¿Qué te aportará esta posición?

AUTONOMÍA Y ORGANIZACIÓN: Serás responsable de la gestión integral de la planta, con autonomía para organizar y optimizar los recursos técnicos y humanos disponibles, alineándolos con los objetivos estratégicos del Grupo.

LIDERAZGO Y EQUIPO DE TRABAJO: Gestionarás un equipo multidisciplinario y dinámico, formando parte de la dirección del Grupo y colaborando estrechamente con otros departamentos estratégicos en la toma de decisiones clave para las personas y su desarrollo.

MEJORA CONTINUA: Tendrás la oportunidad de implementar proyectos innovadores para la mejora de procesos y la optimización de la producción, impulsando la cultura de mejora continua y excelencia operacional.

PROYECTOS ESTRATÉGICOS: Colaborarás en el diseño y ejecución de proyectos estratégicos para el crecimiento y la expansión de la planta, con la posibilidad de dar tu visión en temas clave del Grupo.

SECTOR LÍDER Y TECNOLOGÍA DE VANGUARDIA: Trabajarás en el sector alimentario, utilizando la tecnología más avanzada y aplicando las mejores prácticas de producción y seguridad alimentaria, en un entorno que valora la innovación y el talento.

¿Qué tendrás que hacer?




En dependencia de la Dirección Industrial del Grupo, serás la persona responsable de la planta gestionando y optimizando los recursos técnicos y humanos. Tu misión será asegurar el cumplimiento de los objetivos de eficiencia, calidad y seguridad alimentaria y optimizar los procesos de producción, buscando siempre mejorar la eficiencia y reducir los costes operativos.

Gestionarás el equipo humano a tu cargo, desde operativos hasta mandos intermedios, motivándolos, desarrollándolos y asegurando que todos estén alineados con los objetivos de la empresa. Dimensionarás la carga de trabajo garantizando que la producción se realice de manera equilibrada y optimizando los tiempos de producción.

Definir y gestionar el presupuesto anual de la planta, asegurando la optimización de los recursos económicos. Deberás analizar las desviaciones y garantizar el control de los costes operativos, buscando siempre la rentabilidad y eficiencia en todas las áreas.

Coordinarás y asegurarás las áreas: calidad, mantenimiento, seguridad e higiene, y prevención de riesgos laborales cumplan con los estándares de calidad y seguridad establecidos, garantizando un entorno de trabajo seguro para todos los empleados y el cumplimiento de las normativas vigentes.

Por otro lado, serás el encargado de impulsar la mejora continua a través de proyectos de optimización de procesos. Implementarás metodologías como Lean Manufacturing, TPM y Six Sigma para mejorar la productividad, reducir desperdicios y garantizar la estandarización de los procesos. Todo ello sin dejar de lado la automatización y digitalización industrial, que serán fundamentales para mantener la competitividad de la planta.

¿Qué te hará triunfar en esta posición?

Se requiere formación universitaria en Ingeniería Industrial, Mecánica o disciplinas similares. Deberás contar con sólidos conocimientos sobre los procesos de producción, así como experiencia mínima de 5 años liderando una planta o en posiciones de responsabilidad en entornos industriales, preferiblemente en el sector alimentario, habiendo liderando equipos multidisciplinares.

Es imprescindible que tengas una mentalidad proactiva y orientada a resultados, y que sepas gestionar conflictos de forma constructiva. Tu experiencia en la gestión de cambios organizacionales será un valor clave, ya que serás la persona encargada de impulsar una cultura de mejora continua en la planta.

Marcarás la diferencia si además tienes pensamiento estratégico, capacidad de adaptarte a entornos de rápida evolución, toma decisiones y habilidades de comunicación.

La residencia en la Ribera (Navarra) o cercanías es necesaria para poder asumir la responsabilidad del puesto.




¡Si eres un líder con amplia experiencia en la gestión de plantas de producción, con un enfoque en la optimización de procesos, mejora continua y desarrollo de equipos, esta es tu oportunidad!

title: Gerente de Marca
description:
Nuestro cliente es uno de los principales grupos de distribución de automoción en España, con una amplia red de concesionarios y talleres, presencia en varias provincias y un porfolio multimarca de primer nivel. La compañía se encuentra en una fase de consolidación y crecimiento, reforzando su estructura de gestión por marcas para seguir impulsando resultados, eficiencia y profesionalización.

En este contexto, buscamos incorporar un/a Gerente de Marca para liderar el negocio de una de las marcas del grupo en Madrid.




Misión del puesto:




En dependencia directa del Director de División con sede en Madrid, la persona seleccionada asumirá la dirección integral del negocio de la marca, con responsabilidad sobre resultados, equipos y operaciones, asegurando el cumplimiento de los objetivos comerciales, económicos y de calidad, y el alineamiento con la estrategia del grupo y de la marca.




Responsabilidades:




 * Dirección integral del negocio de la marca en Madrid, con responsabilidad sobre ventas, postventa y resultados.
 * Definir e implementar el plan de negocio de la marca en coordinación con la Dirección de División.
 * Liderar y coordinar los equipos (ventas y postventa), impulsando una cultura de rendimiento, calidad y orientación a cliente.
 * Garantizar el cumplimiento de los objetivos de volumen, rentabilidad y calidad de servicio.
 * Analizar el desempeño del negocio mediante KPIs, márgenes y resultados, proponiendo y ejecutando planes de mejora.
 * Asegurar la correcta relación y coordinación con la marca y con las áreas transversales del grupo.
 * Impulsar la profesionalización de procesos, estándares de gestión y organización del negocio.




Perfil requerido:




 * Experiencia en posiciones de responsabilidad en entornos comerciales y/o de gestión de negocio en automoción o distribución (por ejemplo, Director/Jefe de Ventas, Gerente de concesión, Responsable de unidad de negocio).
 * Fuerte orientación a resultados, con capacidad para gestionar objetivos comerciales y económicos.
 * Experiencia en liderazgo de equipos comerciales y estructuras operativas.
 * Capacidad de análisis, toma de decisiones y ejecución en entornos exigentes.
 * Perfil con ambición de desarrollo: buscamos tanto gerentes consolidados como directores de ventas muy sólidos que quieran evolucionar hacia una gerencia completa de negocio.
 * Estilo de liderazgo cercano, exigente y orientado a la mejora continua.




Se ofrece:




 * Incorporación a un grupo líder del sector, con un proyecto sólido y en crecimiento.
 * Rol de alta responsabilidad e impacto directo en el negocio.
 * Proyecto estable, con recorrido y posibilidades reales de desarrollo profesional.
