title: Director/a de Ingeniería para Proyectos de Energía
description:
En Sener Energy deseamos incorporar en nuestro equipo a un/a Director/a de Ingeniería para Proyectos de Energía en nuestro centro de trabajo de Bilbao o Madrid.

Orientamos la ingeniería hacia la sostenibilidad de la industria y, de la sociedad. Por eso, desarrollamos proyectos orientados a lograr la máxima eficiencia en la transición hacia un modelo energético sostenible, mediante la innovación y la tecnología.

Nos encargamos de la concepción, desarrollo y construcción de plantas energéticas y componentes de las mismas, abarcando desde las tecnologías más convencionales como ciclos combinados, biomasas, etc. hasta las nuevas tecnologías renovables y de futuro como offshore wind, hidrogeno verde, gestionabilidad, economía circular y sostenibilidad.

Qué harás:


 * Liderar y gestionar el equipo de ingeniería en proyectos de energía, desde ingenierías conceptuales hasta ingenierías de detalle; y ofertas.
 * Gestionar el contrato, proveedores, subcontratistas y cliente.
 * Gestionar los costes y los recursos del proyecto de manera eficiente.
 * Asegurar el cumplimiento de requisitos.
 * Desarrollar y supervisar la planificación y plan de ejecución de proyecto.
 * Colaborar con otras áreas de la empresa para garantizar la integración y el éxito del proyecto.
   
   

Qué esperamos de ti:


 * Grado o Master en Ingeniería.
 * Experiencia mínima de 10 años en la gestión de grandes proyectos de ingeniería en el sector energía. Ciclos combinados, terminales de GNL, grandes plantas industriales o similares.
 * Conocimientos técnico-económicos de las tecnologías del sector
 * Ambición intelectual
 * Habilidades de liderazgo, trabajo en equipo, comunicación y gestión de equipos.
 * Disponibilidad para viajar.
 * Nivel de inglés C1 o superior
   
   

Qué te ofrecemos:

Estos son algunos de los beneficios de los que disfrutarás en Sener:


 * Tendrás la oportunidad de participar en proyectos innovadores y de alto nivel tecnológico a nivel internacional, colaborando con un equipo multidisciplinar y altamente cualificado.
 * Disfrutaras de una mejor gestión de tu tiempo a través de nuestras políticas de flexibilidad horaria y trabajo en remoto.
 * Podrás optimizar tu salario con nuestro plan retributivo flexible (tarjeta de comida, seguro médico privado, tickets guardería...) y dispondrás de un seguro de vida y accidentes.
 * Te cuidaremos con nuestro programa de bienestar físico y emocional, que incluye deportes, talleres, etc.
 * Impulsaremos tu desarrollo profesional, con formación técnica, en habilidades, idiomas, etc. para que continúes creciendo y alcanzando tus metas.
 * Promovemos la igualdad de oportunidades, diversidad e inclusión de todas las personas y estamos certificados como Entidad Familiarmente Responsable (efr).
   
   

Si te sientes identificado/a con nuestros valores y quieres formar parte de una ingeniería familiar internacional puntera, ¡te esperamos! Queremos llegar tan lejos como quieras llegar tú.

title: Plant Director ES_874498
description:
Reporting to the Group Operations Director, this role will take full responsibility for the day‑to‑day performance of a fully integrated production facility. The position provides leadership across all operational areas, including manufacturing, engineering, health & safety, logistics, and regulatory compliance. A central objective is to uphold a safe, efficient, and high‑quality production environment that meets both internal standards and industry regulations.




Main Responsibilities

 * Own the plant’s P&L, ensuring operational performance meets budget expectations and broader business goals.
 * Lead the development of budgets, forecasts, and production schedules, while coordinating closely with customer supply chain teams.
 * Guarantee that products are delivered on time and consistently meet defined quality requirements.
 * Champion initiatives focused on cost optimization, continuous improvement, and a strong culture of safety.
 * Oversee maintenance activities and material‑handling operations.
 * Manage capital expenditure plans and drive key operational projects.
 * Monitor performance metrics to support long‑term strategic objectives.
 * Cultivate a collaborative, transparent, and positive work environment across all teams.







Candidate Profile

 * Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering; postgraduate studies in operations or industrial fields will be considered a strong plus.
 * Minimum of 8 years’ experience leading operations in manufacturing settings, ideally within highly regulated industries such as food production.
 * Experience leading important transformations in manufacturing plants is a must.
 * Solid knowledge of production processes and a strong orientation toward continuous process improvement.
 * Proactive, solutions‑driven mindset with the ability to handle conflict constructively and encourage innovative problem-solving.
 * Strategic thinker with the agility to operate effectively in rapidly evolving environments.
 * Demonstrated leadership skills with the ability to guide and inspire teams.
 * Full professional proficiency in English.

title: Global Quality Manager
description:
At ALS, we encourage you to dream big.

When you join us, you’ll be part of a global team harnessing the power of scientific testing and data-driven insights to build a healthier future.

Reporting to: Executive General Manager Food

Location: Europe (remote / flexible)

ALS is seeking a Global Quality Manager (Food) to lead and strengthen quality strategy, governance and consistent execution across our global Food testing laboratories. Reporting to the Executive General Manager Food, this role ensures robust, compliant and reliable quality systems that support accurate results and outstanding customer confidence across regions.

You will design, implement and continuously improve a risk-based global Quality Management System (QMS) aligned with ISO/IEC 17025 and key regional regulatory requirements. You will partner closely with Operations and Technical leaders to harmonise standards, build quality capability and drive continuous improvement. You will also engage with accreditation bodies and customers to support audits, performance transparency and quality trust.

This is a key role in strengthening global consistency and client trust across our Food network.

What You’ll Do


 * Lead global quality strategy and governance: set global quality direction, policies and objectives aligned to business priorities and regulatory expectations.
 * Own and evolve the global QMS: drive a risk-based, integrated QMS aligned with ISO/IEC 17025 and ISO 9001 principles, ensuring consistency across sites.
 * Standardise and harmonise ways of working: define and deploy global procedures, methods, and controls where applicable; support comparability of results across laboratories.
 * Build capability and quality culture: establish global training and competency frameworks for senior quality leadership; promote a culture of scientific excellence, accountability and “right-first-time”.
 * Strengthen risk management and readiness: implement quality risk management frameworks, escalation criteria and crisis support processes; maintain global quality risk registers.
 * Drive performance and continuous improvement: define quality KPIs and dashboards, analyse trends and lead improvement programs (e.g. audit findings, CAPA cycle time, proficiency testing performance, TAT adherence, data integrity incidents, customer complaints).
 * Lead accreditation and customer engagement: support multi-site accreditation, manage external audits and communicate quality performance and improvement plans to stakeholders.
 * Partner across the business: collaborate with Operations, Scientific leaders, HR and Digital/IT teams to ensure quality processes are effective, scalable and well-supported through systems and data.
   
   

What You’ll Bring


 * Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Chemistry, Microbiology, Food Science, Analytical Science or a related field (advanced degree preferred).
 * Experience: strong experience in laboratory quality roles, ideally with multi-site or global responsibility in a testing environment (Food/Agri preferred or closely related).
 * Accreditation expertise: proven success with ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation (multi-site), strong knowledge of ISO 9001 principles and other relevant standards; familiarity with ILAC MRA requirements is a plus.
 * Continuous improvement: track record of delivering measurable results using structured improvement methodologies.
 * Leadership: strong stakeholder management, cross-cultural communication and change leadership; ability to influence across senior levels and drive consistency through collaboration.
 * Customer and regulatory focus: experience managing customer audits and navigating regional regulatory requirements.
   
   

Language & travel: fluent English; willingness to travel internationally (approx. 25–40%).

Working at ALS

The ALS team is a diverse and dedicated community united by our passion to make a difference in the world.

Our values are important to us, and shape how we work, how we treat each other and how we recognise excellence.

At ALS, you’ll be supported to develop new skills and reach your full potential. We invest in our people with programs and opportunities that help you build a diverse career with us.

We want everyone to have a safe, flexible and rewarding career that makes a positive impact on our people, the planet and our communities.

Everyone Matters

ALS is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive work environment where the strengths and perspectives of each employee are both recognised and valued.

ALS also welcomes applications from people with all levels of ability. Reasonable adjustments to support candidates throughout the recruitment process are available upon request. 

Eligibility

To be eligible to work at ALS you must be a Citizen or Permanent Resident of the country you are applying for, or either hold or be able to obtain, a valid working visa.

How To Apply

Please apply on-line and provide a resume & cover letter that best demonstrate your motivation and ability to meet the requirements of this role.

Our client is a Marine Survey and Environmental Consultancy company with its own accredited Laboratories. Our client specializes in the marine environment and offshore projects. Our client brings extensive experience in delivering geophysical, geotechnical, and environmental survey services to industries including oil & gas, renewables, submarine cables, and ports.

Key Responsibilities

The Head Of Geophysics Is The Point Of Contact For Any Technical Matter Related To Geophysics. With The Support Of The Offshore Operation Manager (to Whom She/he Reports) And The Survey Manager, She/he Creates And Continuously Improves The Marine Survey Geophysics Standards And Makes Sure That Project Operational Strategies Are Efficient.  * Retrieving data. Wait a few seconds and try to cut or copy again.

 * Strong ability to work in a cross-functional environment.
 * Problem-solving attitude.
 * Influential skills.
 * Hands-on / Willing to go offshore for troubleshooting if necessary.

 * Good work ethic, communication skills, and attitude.
 * Proficient with QINSy, Qimera, NaviSuite, CARIS, QPS, HYPACK, SonarWiz, AutoCAD/AutoCHART, or GIS applications.

title: Commercial Director (Madrid)
description:
En ALMAR WATER, valoramos y promovemos el talento de nuestro equipo de manera continua, apoyando el desarrollo personal y profesional de nuestra plantilla.

Somos una empresa internacional del sector del agua en pleno proceso de expansión con un gran equipo de profesionales y excelente ambiente de trabajo. Actualmente nos encontramos en la búsqueda de un Commercial Director / Director Comercial (Madrid).




¿En qué consistiría tu trabajo?




Responsabilidades:

 * Definir la estrategia comercial y marketing de la compañía en colaboración con el departamento de Marketing y Comunicación para lograr exitosamente los objetivos de la compañía.
 * Ampliación de la cartera comercial y captación de nuevos clientes y sectores productivos dentro del tratamiento de agua industrial, incluyendo la negociación y el cierre de contratos importantes.
 * Establecer e implementar los procedimientos de gestión del departamento comercial.
 * Gestionar la introducción de nuevos productos en el mercado relacionados con proyectos y servicios en el tratamiento de agua industrial.
 * Representar a la compañía en los diferentes eventos, ferias, congreso a nivel nacional e internacional.
 * Utilizar los datos disponibles para pronosticar con precisión las ventas y establecer objetivos de rendimiento adecuados.




¿Qué buscamos?




Formación:

 * Ingeniería Industrial/Medioambiental o similar.
 * Valorable: MBA o Máster en Dirección Comercial.

Experiencia:

 * Al menos 10 años en gestión comercial en el entorno del desarrollo de proyectos de tratamiento de agua de proceso y agua residual industrial en diferentes sectores.
 * Amplio conocimiento y cartera comercial en el sector del tratamiento de agua industrial y conexiones con los principales actores del sector (clientes, asociaciones, distribuidores…)
 * Experiencia en la elaboración y desarrollo del plan estratégico de ventas / marketing, y en implantación de procedimientos de gestión del departamento de ventas.
 * Conocimientos en procesos y tecnologías de tratamiento de agua en sectores industriales.
 * Experiencia en la introducción de nuevas soluciones y productos en el mercado relacionados con el tratamiento de agua industrial.
 * Valorable:
 * Experiencia en empresas internacionales de proyectos de tratamiento de agua en el sector industrial.
 * Experiencia en la generación de nuevos modelos de negocio no convencionales para clientes industriales.
 * Capacidad para generar contenidos sobre las tendencias del sector para publicar en red, web o congresos.

Idiomas:

Nivel alto de inglés y castellano




*Disponibilidad para viajar a nivel nacional e internacional




Otros:

 * Capacidad analítica.
 * Capacidad de comunicación y negociación.
 * Organización y planificación
 * Enfoque al cliente.
 * Orientación a resultados.




En Almar Water te ofrecemos:

·Paquete retributivo compuesto por: salario acorde al perfil + beneficios sociales.

·Retribución flexible: seguro de salud, tiques guardería y tarjeta transporte.

·Ubicación: Madrid, España.




Si quieres desarrollar tu carrera profesional y te identificas con estos intereses, nos va a encantar contar contigo para formar parte de nuestro equipo. ¡TE ESTAMOS ESPERANDO!

title: Director of Engineering- Ritz Carlton Abama, Tenerife
location: Greater Santa Cruz de Tenerife Metropolitan Area
description:
Additional Information

Job Number 25178535

Job Category Engineering & Facilities

Location The Ritz-Carlton Tenerife Abama, Calle Maria Zambrano 2, Tenerife, Tenerife, Spain, 38687VIEW ON MAP

Schedule Full Time

Located Remotely? N

Position Type Management

Lead engineering excellence at The Ritz-Carlton, Abama , a world-class resort on the breathtaking island of Tenerife. This is your opportunity to combine technical expertise with visionary leadership in one of Europe’s most iconic destinations.

Brand and Property Details

The Ritz-Carlton, Abama is synonymous with timeless luxury and exceptional service. Nestled on Tenerife’s stunning coastline, our resort offers unparalleled experiences for guests seeking elegance and sophistication. As part of our team, you’ll help maintain the beauty and functionality of this extraordinary property.

Company Overview

At Marriott International, we create unforgettable hospitality experiences across the globe. We value innovation, diversity, and growth—empowering our associates to thrive and make a difference. Join us and be part of a company that invests in your success.

Role Summary

As Director of Engineering , you will be the driving force behind the property’s engineering and maintenance operations. From safeguarding our physical assets to leading sustainability initiatives, you’ll ensure every detail reflects the Ritz-Carlton standard of excellence. Reporting to the General Manager, you’ll manage budgets, capital projects, and preventative maintenance programs while inspiring a high-performing team.

Key Responsibilities


 * Lead with Vision: Oversee all engineering operations, including HVAC, plumbing, electrical systems, and life safety systems.
 * Drive Performance: Develop and manage budgets, capital expenditure plans, and long-term asset protection strategies.
 * Ensure Compliance: Maintain adherence to local, state, and federal regulations, as well as brand standards.
 * Champion Sustainability: Monitor energy consumption and implement conservation initiatives.
 * Deliver Results: Review financial reports and identify opportunities to improve profitability.
 * Inspire Excellence: Coach and mentor the engineering team, fostering a culture of safety and collaboration.
 * Protect Our Legacy: Maintain current licenses, permits, and certifications for all building systems.
 * Respond with Care: Address guest and employee concerns promptly and professionally.
   
   

What We’re Looking For


 * High school diploma or GED with 6 years of engineering/maintenance experience OR
 * 2-year degree in Engineering, Building & Construction, or related field with 4 years of experience.
 * Technical training in HVAC-R, Electrical, and Plumbing.
 * Strong leadership and strategic planning skills.
 * Financial acumen and ability to manage complex projects.
 * Passion for innovation and operational excellence.
   
   

Location

On-site at The Ritz-Carlton, Abama, Tenerife —a destination as inspiring as the role itself.

Why Join Us?


 * Comprehensive healthcare and well-being programs
 * Parental leave and pension plans
 * Generous annual leave allowances
 * Career development through mentorship and world-class training
   
   

Ready to Make an Impact?

Apply today by submitting your CV and a cover letter detailing your experience and passion for this role.

At Marriott International, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, welcoming all and providing access to opportunity. We actively foster an environment where the unique backgrounds of our associates are valued and celebrated. Our greatest strength lies in the rich blend of culture, talent, and experiences of our associates. We are committed to non-discrimination on any protected basis, including disability, veteran status, or other basis protected by applicable law.

At more than 100 award-winning properties worldwide, The Ritz-Carlton Ladies and Gentlemen create experiences so exceptional that long after a guest stays with us, the experience stays with them. Attracting the world’s top hospitality professionals who curate lifelong memories, we believe that everyone succeeds when they are empowered to be creative, thoughtful and compassionate.

Every day, we set the standard for rare and special luxury service the world over and pride ourselves on delivering excellence in the care and comfort of our guests.

Your role will be to ensure that the “Gold Standards” of The Ritz-Carlton are delivered graciously and thoughtfully every day. The Gold Standards are the foundation of The Ritz-Carlton and are what guides us each day to be better than the next. It is this foundation and our belief that our culture drives success by which The Ritz Carlton has earned the reputation as a global brand leader in luxury hospitality. As part of our team, you will learn and exemplify the Gold Standards, such as our Employee Promise, Credo and our Service Values. And our promise to you is that we offer the chance to be proud of the work you do and who you work with.

In joining The Ritz-Carlton, you join a portfolio of brands with Marriott International. Be where you can do your best work, begin your purpose, belong to an amazing global team, and become the best version of you.

title: Head Finance Direct Procurement
description:
Sandoz continues to go through an exciting and transformative period as a global leader and pioneering provider of sustainable Biosimilar and Generic medicines. As we continue down this new and ambitious path, unique opportunities will present themselves, both professionally and personally.

As a newly independent company we are focused on growth. We see growth as the engine powering everything we want to achieve for patients. It will allow us to innovate & pioneer greater access to high-quality medicines, but we can only do this by bringing the best and the brightest minds together.

The Head Finance Direct Procurement is a global role partnering with Procurement and Finance function of production sites, to deliver 3rd party vendor savings. The role provides insightful and value-added analysis related to vendors and contracts and decision support to the STO Direct Procurement Leadership team and category heads in their organizations. Supports the development and execution of annual operating and long-term financial plans interim forecasts and “what if” scenarios, focusing on the underlying business drivers, key financial and operating metrics and relative sensitivity analysis with STO Finance leadership team and drives productivity and savings in the areas of STO Direct Spend. The role supports contract evaluation and financial analysis for vendor negotiations. Brings strategic financial outlook for vendor network development.

Direct Procurement Support

Responsibilities include but not limited to:


 * Ensures accuracy and reconciliation of planning and forecasting data between Procurement spend and savings vs. financial reporting for ACT and planning cycles (TGT/LFs)
 * Supports Direct Procurement projects and PPV/savings tracking and delivery
   
   

Productivity And Financial Reporting


 * Drives Projects and benchmarking to increase productivity on a short/long term basis for Sandoz Technical Operations with efficient make vs. buy evaluation.
 * Ensures efficient Procurement cost structure in line with business need and generics mindset
 * Provides input for TPC/Standard costs process. Manages Direct Material planning process and updates.
 * Manages proactively R&O and ensures delivery of financial commitments
 * Leads planning and forecasting for budgeting cycles and manages delivery against defined objectives for TGT/LFs etc.
   
   

Business Partnering


 * Support procurement savings calculation and tracking and ensure compliance according to savings guidelines. Play a key role in designing and developing reporting tools, interacting with internal functions to devise solutions that enhance reporting and data management.
 * Partners with Procurement category managers to deliver incremental Free Cash Flow through supplier payment term improvements.
 * Partner with manufacturing site teams, commercial organizations, beyond Procurement to maximize Enterprise value
 * Support and track financial impact of claims cases
 * Contribute to the pre‐contract negotiations with key suppliers and effectively deliver implementation of the Terms & Conditions in the contract.
   
   

Governance, Compliance, And Control

Ensure rigorous financial processes, strong internal controls, and full compliance with audit requirements, maintaining the highest standards of governance and transparency.

What You Need To Bring To The Role


 * University degree in Business, Finance or equivalent.
 * Desirable: CPA, MBA or a professional qualification
 * Fluent English
 * Extensive Finance and Strategy Experience
 * 5-10 years of progressive leadership experience, ideally within the pharmaceutical, biotech.
 * Experience in Finance of Operations, knowledge of product costing
 * Experience in Procurement finance is plus.
 * Ability to engage with business partners
 * Influence and reach alignment across the organization
   
   

Why Sandoz?

Generic and Biosimilar medicines are the backbone of the global medicines industry. Sandoz, a leader in this sector, touched the lives of 500 million patients last year and while we are proud of this achievement, we have an ambition to do more!

With investments in new development capabilities, state-of-the-art production sites, new acquisitions, and partnerships, we have the opportunity to shape the future of Sandoz and help more patients gain access to low-cost, high-quality medicines, sustainably.

Our momentum and entrepreneurial spirit is powered by an open, collaborative culture driven by our talented and ambitious colleagues, who, in return for applying their skills experience an agile and collegiate environment with impactful, flexible-hybrid careers, where diversity is welcomed and where personal growth is encouraged!

Join us!

Commitment to Diversity & Inclusion

We are committed to building an outstanding, inclusive work environment and diverse teams representative of the patients and communities we serve.

Join our Sandoz Network: If this role is not suitable to your experience or career goals but you wish to stay connected to hear more about Sandoz and our career opportunities, visit Sandoz.com/Careers

title: Experienced Innovation Consultant (EU Funding)
description:
Position for an innovation consultant with hands-on experience in EU proposal writing.


A hybrid position located in Madrid to become part of the team long term.


Competitive salary range depending on candidate experience and training (fixed wage + bonus).


   
   
 * EXPERIENCE LEVEL: intermediate (3+ years of experience, development of 4-5 EU proposals).
   
   
 * AREA THIS POSITION BELONGS TO: proposal writing and project management.
   
   
 * MANDATORY: C2 English & Spanish level. Scientific university degree at master’s level (agricultural engineering, environmental sciences, biology…etc). Experience in the writing of 2 full collaborative EU proposals (+10 partners) from beginning to end (H2020 / Horizon Europe / BBI JU / CBE JU…etc).
   
   
 * APPLICATION: Candidates will be reviewed on weekly basis, do not wait to apply!
   
   
 * KEYWORDS: #ProposalWriting #ProjectManagement #HorizonEurope #Agrifood #bioeconomy
   
   


Tasks


This position is ideal for a professional who can lead the preparation of EU proposals end-to-end with limited supervision, collaborating closely with senior colleagues and external partners across Europe. At Innovarum, innovation consultants contribute to both:


   
   
 * Proposal writing, including proposal conceptualisation, consortium building, benchmarking, work plan development, and proposal coordination.
   
   
 * Project management, supporting implementation, reporting, and coordination activities when proposals succeed.
   
   


You will progressively take more ownership over proposals, client relationships, and strategic positioning, depending on your strengths and development path.


Key responsibilities:


This position is meant for an experienced proposal writer able to carry out a full proposal writing on its own with limited supervision.


    
    
 1. Leading the drafting of EU proposals (Horizon Europe, H2020, BBI JU, CBE JU—especially Cluster 6 and related areas).
    
    
 2. Designing work plans (WPs, tasks, deliverables, milestones), budgets, and partner contributions.
    
    
 3. Preparing technical benchmarking and state-of-the-art analyses to support project ambition.
    
    
 4. Developing market studies, exploitation pathways, & innovation/business plans (as required by the call).
    
    
 5. Coordinating inputs from multiple partners and ensuring proposal consistency, quality, and compliance with call requirements.
    
    
 6. Supporting project implementation and reporting (technical and financial) depending on project needs.
    
    
 7. Contributing to proposal strategy: topic screening, consortium building, and positioning of innovation concepts.
    
    


Requirements


We are seeking a candidate who combines strong scientific foundations with the ability to translate research and innovation into compelling, fundable proposals. The ideal profile is proactive, structured and comfortable working in fast-paced environments, with strong attention to detail and a clear understanding of how EU evaluators assess excellence, impact and implementation. You enjoy working with international consortia, coordinating inputs from multiple partners and ensuring high-quality, coherent submissions that tell a convincing story from concept to impact.


MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS


    
    
 1. Minimum 3 years of relevant experience, with proven hands-on EU proposal writing experience. Overall track record of 4–5 EU proposals (H2020 / Horizon Europe / BBI JU / CBE JU…).
    
    
 2. Experience in the writing of 2 full collaborative EU proposals (+10 partners) from beginning to end: idea conceptualisation, consortium building, overall writing supervision and integration, budget and work packages development, and submission through the Funding & Tenders Portal.
    
    
 3. C2 level English and Spanish, written and oral.
    
    
 4. MSc (or equivalent) in Agricultural Engineering or related scientific/technical disciplines (environmental sciences, biology, etc.).
    
    
 5. Experience in technical roles requiring strong writing skills (reports, scientific documents, technical documentation).
    
    


NICE TO HAVE (BONUS POINTS)


    
    
 1. Experience in EU grant management, including technical and financial reporting.
    
    
 2. Some extra background in business/economics.
    
    
 3. Strong understanding of European R&I funding structures and evaluation logic.
    
    
 4. International experience (e.g., living abroad).Additional languages
    
    


Benefits


We offer a full-time hybrid position based in Madrid within a well-established European innovation consultancy, with long-term professional growth opportunities. The role includes a competitive salary package (€32,000–€36,000 gross/year, depending on experience) complemented by a performance-based bonus, as well as a clear development path towards greater autonomy, leadership in complex proposals, and strategic responsibilities. You will work in a collaborative and international team, enjoy remote work flexibility, and be involved in innovation projects aligned with ethical, climate-positive and environmentally sustainable objectives, combining scientific excellence with real-world impact.


Benefits & ways of working:


   
   
 * Hybrid (2 days/week office)
   
   
 * 38h/week (40h contract)
   
   
 * Structured recovery time after major submissions
   
   
 * Extra leave: birthday (when Mon-Fry) + Dec 24 & Dec 31
   
   
 * Fully remote periods (summer month + Christmas)
   
   
 * Team celebrations & 2 annual team-building events
   
   


Join us at Innovarum and be part of a dynamic team shaping the future of the agri-food sector and bioeconomy.

title: Director/a d’Operacions, Processos i Automatització (Operations, Process & Automation Director) - EL PERIÓDICO
description:
Liderar l’eficiència operativa de l’organització en el dia a dia i impulsar la millora contínua dels processos, incorporant automatització i simplificació com a palanques clau. El rol combina responsabilitat executiva sobre operacions amb una funció clara de transformació i optimització.

Responsabilitats principals:


 * Direcció d’Operacions
 * Assegurar el correcte funcionament operatiu de l’organització.
 * Detectar i gestionar colls d’ampolla, ineficiències i incidències recurrents.
 * Coordinar equips i responsables operatius, alineant-los amb els objectius de negoci.
 * Fer seguiment dels principals indicadors operatius (cost, temps, qualitat, servei).
 * Garantir l’execució eficient de les decisions estratègiques definides per direcció.
 * Processos i millora contínua
 * Analitzar, mapar i redissenyar processos clau end-to-end.
 * Simplificar fluxos de treball, eliminar redundàncies i estandarditzar pràctiques.
 * Definir criteris clars de “com treballem” orientats a eficiència i escalabilitat.
 * Introduir metodologies de millora contínua (Lean, BPM, Kaizen o similars) de manera pragmàtica.
 * Automatització i eficiència
 * Identificar tasques repetitives i processos susceptibles d’automatització.
 * Implementar solucions d’automatització low-code / no-code, RPA o integracions senzilles.
 * Col·laborar amb IT i equips de dades per connectar sistemes i optimitzar fluxos.
 * Incorporar tecnologies d’IA i automatització amb criteri de negoci i impacte real en coordinación amb els corresponents departaments.
 * Mesurar l’impacte de les millores (hores estalviades, errors reduïts, costos evitats).
 * Governança i seguiment
 * Definir i fer seguiment de KPIs d’eficiència operativa i de processos.
 * Prioritzar iniciatives segons retorn i impacte real per al negoci.
 * Comunicar de manera clara l’estat de les operacions i dels projectes de millora a direcció.
   
   

Perfil requerit:


 * Experiència contrastada en direcció d’operacions, processos o transformació operativa.
 * Haver liderat millores reals de processos i projectes d’automatització.
 * Experiència treballant amb equips multidisciplinaris i entorns complexos.
 * Es valorarà experiència en sectors de serveis, producción d’esdeveniments, mitjans, continguts, projectes o entorns de creixement.
 * Perfil híbrid, capaç de combinar execució operativa amb transformació.
 * Algú que entengui el dia a dia de l’empresa, però que també sàpiga qüestionar-lo i millorar-lo.
 * Orientació clara a eficiència, escalabilitat i millora contínua.
 * Capacitat de prioritzar i dir “no” quan una iniciativa no aporta valor real.
   
   

Competències clau:


 * Visió global de negoci i orientació a resultats.
 * Forta capacitat analítica i de simplificació.
 * Mentalitat pràctica, orientada a l’impacte i a l’execució.
 * Coneixement de processos i millora contínua sense enfocament excessivament teòric.
 * Experiència amb eines d’automatització, integració i digitalització de processos.
 * Capacitat d’influència transversal i lideratge sense necessitat de jerarquia directa constant.
 * Bones habilitats de comunicació amb equips operatius i direcció.
   
   

Indicadors d’èxit del rol:


 * Millora mesurable de l’eficiència operativa.
 * Reducció de temps, costos i errors en processos clau.
 * Increment de l’automatització de tasques repetitives.
 * Major claredat i estandardització en la manera de treballar.
   
   

Condicions:


 * Contracte indefinit.
 * 1 dia de teletreball.
 * Salari en funció de valia.
   
   

Com a empresa inclusiva, Prensa Ibérica està compromesa amb la diversitat, la igualtat d'oportunitats i valora totes les sol·licituds sense discriminació.

title: Director de cadena de suministro
description:
Desde Hays estamos colaborando con una sólida y reconocida compañía del sector alimentación, en pleno crecimiento y con fuerte presencia nacional e internacional, para incorporar un/a Director/a de Supply Chain que lidere la estrategia integral de la cadena de suministro.




🎯 Tu misión




Serás responsable de diseñar, coordinar y optimizar toda la cadena de suministro de la compañía: planificación, aprovisionamiento, compras, logística, almacenes y distribución. Tu objetivo será asegurar un flujo eficiente, sostenible y alineado con los estándares de calidad del sector alimentación.




🔧 Principales responsabilidades




 * Definir e implementar la estrategia global de Supply Chain de la compañía.
 * Garantizar la disponibilidad de materias primas y producto final bajo criterios de calidad, seguridad y coste.
 * Liderar los equipos de Compras, Planificación, Logística y Almacenes.
 * Optimizar procesos mediante metodologías como Lean, S&OP, mejora continua y digitalización.
 * Gestionar el presupuesto del área y proponer planes de eficiencia.
 * Coordinar la relación con proveedores estratégicos y partners logísticos.
 * Impulsar proyectos de sostenibilidad y trazabilidad, clave en el sector alimentación.
 * Participar en el comité de dirección como responsable de la cadena de suministro.




🧩 Qué buscamos




 * Experiencia consolidada (mín. 6–8 años) en posiciones de responsabilidad en Supply Chain, preferiblemente en alimentación, FMCG o industria con alta rotación.
 * Experiencia liderando equipos multidisciplinares.
 * Conocimiento profundo de planificación, compras, logística y mejora continua.
 * Nivel alto de herramientas de gestión (ERP, planificación avanzada, analítica).
 * Perfil estratégico, orientado a la mejora, con capacidad de análisis y visión de negocio.
 * Residencia en la zona o disponibilidad para trasladarse.




💼 Qué ofrece la compañía




 * Proyecto estable en una empresa referente en su sector.
 * Posición estratégica con impacto directo en el crecimiento del negocio.
 * Paquete retributivo competitivo y acorde con la responsabilidad del puesto.
 * Oportunidades de desarrollo y modernización de la cadena de suministro.

title: Global Head of Ethics and Compliance
description:
Global Governance & Engagement Officer

Location: Barcelona, Spain (Global HQ)

The Opportunity

A leading global healthcare company is seeking a Global Governance & Engagement Officer to shape and drive the future of its corporate compliance and ethics framework. This is a senior role, positioned at the heart of the organisation’s compliance strategy, with direct impact on governance, culture, and ethical decision-making worldwide.

This newly created role will report to the Chief Compliance Officer and partner with senior leadership across regions. The mandate is clear: strengthen governance, embed ethics into everyday business, and ensure alignment between culture, accountability, and performance across all functions and geographies.

Key Responsibilities

 * Leadership Messaging: Reinforce ethical tone at all levels of the business through leadership behaviours, communications, and governance practices.
 * Culture & Ethics: Assess organisational culture, psychological safety, and trust, and translate insights into action plans with global and regional leaders.
 * Governance & Oversight: Maintain and refine governance structures, escalation pathways, and global policies and procedures.
 * Board Engagement: Deliver clear, data-driven reporting to the Executive Team and Board Committees on ethics, culture, and compliance performance.
 * Risk & Metrics: Conduct enterprise risk assessments, define KPIs, and oversee digital tools to monitor governance effectiveness.
 * Training & Recognition: Develop global campaigns, training content, and recognition initiatives to strengthen compliance awareness and reward ethical leadership.
 * Certification & Accountability: Lead the annual compliance certification process across all business units, ensuring robust oversight and accountability.

Profile Sought

 * University degree in Law, Business, Compliance, or related field.
 * 10+ years’ experience in governance, ethics, or compliance, with a global scope.
 * Strong background in board/C-suite reporting, governance models, and organisational culture initiatives.
 * Familiarity with digital compliance tools, culture metrics, and enterprise risk assessment.
 * Exceptional communication and stakeholder-management skills, able to engage across functions and geographies.
 * Fluent English required; additional languages (Spanish, French, etc.) a plus.

Capabilities & Style

 * Strategic yet pragmatic, linking governance principles to practical business actions.
 * High integrity, sound judgement, and cultural sensitivity.
 * Experienced in managing change, leading cross-functional projects, and navigating complex organisations.
 * Strong analytical mindset to translate data and culture assessments into meaningful governance improvements.

Additional Information

 * Based in Barcelona, with up to 30% international travel.
 * Full executive exposure, with a direct reporting line to the CCO.
 * Opportunity to influence global compliance culture during a critical growth phase for the company.




The Leonid Group Corporate Governance is a talent acquisition consultancy that specialises in the placement of Legal, Compliance, Trade Compliance & Ethics professionals on an international scale across the Americas, EMEA and APAC. Legal and compliance are both global, with teams worldwide, and our network reflects this. We work with multinational corporations to help structure their departments with the best people, from the Global Chief Compliance Officer and General Counsel to the Compliance Analyst and Trade Compliance Specialist.

description:

title: Director of Commercial Excellence and Productivity
description:
K2 Partnering Solutions is a global provider of unique end-to-end consultative solutions in the enterprise applications, AI, and cloud space.




Director of Commercial Excellence and Productivity

Role Purpose

As the Director of Sales Operations & Enablement, you will be the architect and guardian of our European sales engine. You will bridge the gap between high-level strategy and daily execution, ensuring our sales managers operate with discipline, our data remains pristine, and our teams are equipped to over-perform. You are a "partner-coach" who isn't afraid to push back on leadership or hold Account Executives (AEs) accountable to rigorous standards.




Key Responsibilities 1. Commercial & Pipeline Governance

 * Operational Oversight: Own the health of the European sales pipeline, including stage definitions, conversion metrics, and data hygiene.
 * Pipeline Control: Manage and audit opportunities by client, technology, and service line to ensure a balanced and strategic portfolio.
 * Quota & Coverage: Lead quota setting and monitor pipeline velocity/coverage to ensure revenue targets are realistic and supported.
 * Commercial Management: Oversee account planning, segmentation models, and the commercial frameworks used by the sales team.




2. Sales Process & Performance Management

 * Process Rigor: Ensure all sales managers adhere to established structures; proactively "chase" updates and flag performance gaps to leadership.
 * Performance Analytics: Track team performance, advise on reward structures, and identify top/bottom performers.
 * Stakeholder Coaching: Act as a trusted advisor to Sales and People leaders, coaching managers on performance management best practices.




3. Enablement & Capability Building

 * Program Ownership: Partner with Revenue leaders to operationalize enablement programs that directly impact productivity.
 * Tool & Playbook Adoption: Track the effectiveness of sales tools and playbooks, ensuring they are utilized to their full potential.
 * Training Impact: Ensure all training efforts translate into measurable revenue growth and increased AE productivity.




Required Experience & Skills

 * Enterprise Sales Background: Proven experience within a Vendor environment, specifically managing teams of Account Executives or large client portfolios.
 * Strategic Operations: Deep expertise in Segmentation Models, quota setting, and pipeline management.
 * Leadership & Influence: Exceptional people management skills with a focus on coaching and development.
 * The "Enforcer" Mindset: A proven ability to push back when standards are diluted and to drive process adoption in a fast-paced environment.
 * Regional Expertise: Experience navigating the complexities of the European market.




Salary: 80-130K EUR/year + bonus

title: Director Comercial Interim – Soluciones OTT & Streaming - Freelance
description:

Trabajarás gestionando un equipo comercial con un portfolio de soluciones punteras en el ecosistema OTT y broadcasting: plataformas de streaming y junto a partners tecnológicos de primer nivel.

title: Dirección de Producto
description:
En Platanomelón creemos que el bienestar íntimo es salud, y queremos que formar parte de esta conversación sea algo natural, sencillo y humano. Trabajamos para acompañar a las personas a conocerse, cuidarse y disfrutar, construyendo una sociedad más libre, sana y feliz. Buscamos talento que quiera unirse a este movimiento y ayudarnos a impulsar un cambio real.




La salud sexual es bienestar. ¡Conócete!




La persona que ocupe el rol de Dirección de Producto liderará el desarrollo integral de productos bajo marca propia y de marca de terceros, desde la ideación estratégica hasta su ejecución en los mercados clave de España y México.

Trabajará en estrecha colaboración con equipos multidisciplinarios internos y externos, incluyendo diseño, proveedores, operaciones, marca y eCommerce, para coordinar estrategias de producto, go-to-market y campañas de lanzamiento. Su objetivo será asegurar que cada lanzamiento esté alineado con las necesidades del consumidor, los objetivos de negocio y los estándares de Platanomelón.







¿Cuáles serán tus responsabilidades?




 * Garantizar la entrega puntual de todos los proyectos de desarrollo de producto, dentro del alcance, timings y presupuesto definidos.
 * Analizar el comportamiento del consumidor, la competencia y las tendencias del mercado global para detectar oportunidades de innovación.
 * Coordinar con equipos multifuncionales (I+D, diseño, proveedores, operaciones y marca) para garantizar disponer de productos diferenciales y funcionales.
 * Colaborar con los equipos de marketing y eCommerce de España y México en el desarrollo de campañas de lanzamiento efectivas, que aseguren la consecución de los objetivos de ventas establecidos.
 * Diseñar estrategias de desarrollo de portafolio a corto (productos de marcas de terceros) y largo plazo (desarrollo de productos bajo marca Platanomelón White label o NPD), alineadas con los objetivos de crecimiento de la empresa.
 * Liderar la definición de estrategias de pricing y propuestas de valor por categoría o línea de producto.
 * Asumir la responsabilidad del forecast, P&L y rentabilidad de cada lanzamiento de nuevos productos en coordinación con los eCommerce manager de cada mercado.
 * Medir el desempeño de productos post-lanzamiento y proponer mejoras continuas con base en datos.
 * Coordinar, motivar y desarrollar al equipo de producto directo (3 personas), promoviendo la colaboración interdepartamental y la excelencia.
 * Mantenerse al tanto de cambios regulatorios, nuevas tecnologías y tendencias emergentes para incorporarlas estratégicamente.




¿Qué buscamos en ti?




 * Más de 4 años de experiencia demostrable en desarrollo de producto en empresas FMCG o startups.
 * Experiencia previa liderando equipos pequeños o medianos.
 * Fuerte capacidad analítica y orientación a datos en la toma de decisiones.
 * Capacidad para liderar en entornos dinámicos, con múltiples proyectos en paralelo y foco en resultados.
 * Experiencia en creación de marca propia y gestión de producto desde su concepción hasta la puesta en el mercado.
 * Habilidades organizativas excelentes, atención al detalle y capacidad para coordinar equipos multifuncionales.
 * Buenas habilidades de comunicación y liderazgo, con visión estratégica y capacidad de ejecución.
 * Conocimientos de estudios de mercado, investigación de consumidor, forecast y estrategias go-to-market.
 * Titulación en Marketing, ADE, Economia o campo relacionado.
 * Manejo avanzado de Excel y herramientas de gestión de proyectos (ej. Trello, Asana).




Indispensable:

 * Track record sólido en desarrollo y lanzamiento de productos.
 * Visión analítica y orientación a negocio.
 * Liderazgo y capacidad de influir en equipos y stakeholders.
 * Organización y enfoque en eficiencia operativa.
 * Sentido de cliente y empatía con el consumidor final.




¿Qué podemos ofrecerte?




✨ Impacto con propósito

Formarás parte de la comunidad más grande en bienestar íntimo, con más de 5 millones de seguidores. No solo serás parte de un referente a nivel nacional e internacional, sino que también nos ayudarás a romper tabúes y generar un impacto real en la sociedad.

Trabajamos cada día para que nuestro equipo tenga un ambiente sano, dinámico y divertido, donde te sentirás valorado/a y parte de algo grande.




🏡 Modelo híbrido y materiales a tu medida

De lunes a jueves nos encontramos en nuestras oficinas, y los viernes y miércoles alternos disfrutamos de home office. Además, te facilitamos todo lo que necesites para trabajar cómodamente desde donde estés.




💰 Beneficios que suman valor

title: Head of Customer Support (Fintech)
location: Santa Cruz de Tenerife, Canary Islands, Spain
description:
About MAKE




At MAKE, we believe creators deserve a banking solution that gets them. One that’s fast, intuitive, and built to help them grow. We’re building the world’s first financial platform tailored to content creators — making it easier to earn, spend, and save money across borders.

MAKE was founded with a specific purpose in mind to address the underserved needs of the Creator Economy often overlooked by traditional banking while bringing forward technology, banking, and global solutions for our Creator Economy Clients.




About the role:




We are seeking a highly motivated and organised individual to join our team as a Head of Customer Support within our FinTech Financial Institution. In this role, you will be responsible for establishing and overseeing the day-to-day operations of our Banking Customer Service team. This includes resolving non-technical customer inquiries via email (Zendesk) as well as technical questions regarding the use of and troubleshooting of the MAKE platform also with understanding and solving Level 1 and Level 2 banking related enquiries.




The ideal candidate will possess strong customer service skills, attention to detail, and a commitment to continuous improvement initiatives with some type of a background in the Financial Technologies or Banking Industry. This role reports directly to the Chief Executive Officer.




Responsibilities:




Team Leadership

 * Establish, lead, and supervise the European support team, providing guidance, training, and coaching to team members.
 * Foster a positive and collaborative team culture that values open communication and continuous learning.
 * Continually educating yourself and the customer service team in financial technology and banking products, markets and general knowledge.
 * Team Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energies and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others.




Customer Service

 * Ensure timely and accurate resolution of customer chats related to the our platform.
 * Uphold a high standard of customer service, addressing user inquiries with professionalism and efficiency.
 * Champion, implement, and manage corporate policies to ensure full compliance with industry regulations and internal standards.
 * Collaborate with other departments to resolve complex technical issues and enhance overall user satisfaction.
 * Through multiple digital channels create an SOP and infrastructure to personalize each interaction with a customer
 * Support teams within the business operations function as needed, including risk management, compliance and onboarding.
 * Comply with all regulatory requirements and internal policies related to customer service.




Process Improvement

 * Identify opportunities for process, tooling, and workflow improvements within the MAKE Support function.
 * Collaborate with cross-functional teams to implement continuous improvement initiatives aimed at enhancing service delivery and efficiency.
 * Check work of colleagues within team to meet internal and stakeholder requirements.
 * Assess the validity and applicability of previous or similar experiences and evaluate options under circumstances that are not covered by procedures.




Reporting and Analysis

 * Generate regular reports on the performance of the European Support team.
 * Analyze data to identify trends, address issues, and make data-driven recommendations for improvement.
 * To meet the needs of stakeholders/ customers through specialist advice and support




Qualifications:




Education and Experience

 * Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Finance, or a related field.
 * Minimum 8-10 years of leadership experience in support, success, or related roles.
 * Previous banking or financial technologies experience required.
 * Extensive knowledge of Zendesk or similar tools (e.g., Intercom).




Personal Attributes

 * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
 * Fluency in English is a must.
 * Detail-oriented and highly organised.
 * Strong analytical skills to track team effectiveness and make data-driven improvements.
 * Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team.




What’s in it for you

 * Competitive Salary: Attractive compensation package commensurate with experience and skills.
 * Professional Growth: Opportunities for career advancement and personal development in a fast-growing company.
 * Inclusive Culture: Be part of a diverse and collaborative team committed to innovation and excellence.
 * Collaborative and Supportive Team: Working with team members that understands the importance of the work you’ll be doing




If you are passionate about leading a team and dedicated to providing exceptional customer support, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity!

title: Director, Talent Science
description:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.

The company is founder led, profitable and growing.

Our Talent Science team has a mission to ensure that we follow objective, scientifically rigorous and efficient processes to select the very best new colleagues regardless of location, culture or background. We work closely with our key stakeholders to ensure that decisions are being made in the right way and that our hiring model supports the selection of world class talent. We lead an effective hiring ecosystem to deliver talent acquisition excellence. We leverage data to enable evidence-based decisions, weaving behavioural science throughout our selection process, working closely with our Hiring Leadership team who drive hiring at Canonical.

Due to growth of our team we are now hiring into 4 new leadership roles in our Talent Science team, to lead on delivery excellence. You will work directly with the Global Head of Talent Science to plan and drive work within the team against our strategic objectives. With overall accountability for project delivery relating to hiring outcomes you will drive efficiencies and create new processes whilst coaching and developing team members to grow their careers.

You will bring an analytical and objective approach to work and be seasoned in making data driven decisions relating to talent outcomes. Your experience will include being able to drive complete project roadmaps, experience interacting with senior leadership on talent related topics leveraging your thought leadership experience and the ability to influence and steer talent agendas internally. You will be capable of stepping up to lead the function when required. Experience of working with career frameworks and hiring outcomes is an essential requirement of this role.

Location: This role will be based remotely in the EMEA region.

The role entails


 * Leading and delivering the strategic Talent Science project roadmap using exceptional project management capability and influencing skills to manage multiple projects at any given time.
 * Leveraging expertise from your career to date to design and implement solutions to complex problems that ultimately ensure we hire exceptional talent.
 * Ensuring all talent selection initiatives map to high performance outcomes and career frameworks.
 * Designing professional processes and frameworks, allowing efficient delivery of our agenda.
 * Coaching and mentoring team members to grow and develop skill sets specifically around analytical capabilities and thought leadership, developing training materials and documentation to support team members.
 * Driving and representing the team in the absence of the Head of, acting as a credible and trusted adviser and an inspiring leader.
 * Working closely with company leaders to understand, appreciate and communicate our vision for talent at Canonical.
   
   

What we are looking for in you


 * Exceptional academic track record from both high school and university, with an undergraduate degree in a psychology or people based subject.
 * A track record of team leadership within the technology or consulting industry, with exceptional project management, to lead roadmap execution and delivery.
 * Proven ability to coach and mentor a team to deliver exceptional results.
 * Deep understanding of selection, career frameworks and building internal products to ensure that talent selection criteria matches desired outcomes.
 * Advanced analytical ability to dissect and analyse data using either SQL, Python and/or statistical analysis.
 * Exceptional written and spoken English with strong presentation skills for a senior audience.
 * Ability to travel internationally 4 times a year for company events.
   
   

Nice-to-have skills


 * Advanced psychometric assessments with an excellent understanding of the marketplace and vendors
 * A deep understanding of human behaviour and what drives intrinsic motivation
 * Experience of using Superset and advanced dashboarding
   
   

What we offer colleagues

We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus or commission. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.


 * Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
 * Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
 * Annual compensation review
 * Recognition rewards
 * Annual holiday leave
 * Maternity and paternity leave
 * Employee Assistance Programme
 * Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
 * Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events
   
   

About Canonical

Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.

Canonical is an equal opportunity employer

We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: IT Service Management (ITSM) Director
description:
Role purpose:

The primary purpose of the ITSM Director is to lead and manage the ITSM Team and to have overall accountability for our ITSM framework and processes such as; Incident, Change, Problem, Knowledge, Configuration, Service Catalogue, Service Transition and Service Level Management. Setting the vision and strategy for these processes and ensuring alignment with business and organizational goals and best practices (e.g. ITIL).

The ITSM Director with on-site presence at the Centre of Excellence to enhance governance, operational efficiency, and engagement with teams as a liaison between the business, technical teams, IT Domain Leads, and external service providers, managing service performance and SLAs. Ensuring that ITSM support regular service reviews in alignment with agreed governance. The role is further responsible for the management of the ServiceNow platform and for driving continuous improvements and enhancements within the platform to optimise ITSM processes and user experience.

Additionally for our organisation, the ITSM Director is also accountable for the implementation of our Identity and Access Management (IAM) Security policies to ensure compliance and delivery of our IAM operations.

Key Responsibilities:

Govern IT Programs: Provide guidelines, reports, and budget supervision in line with ITIL best practices

Organize and Define Processes: Establish IT service management processes to manage, design, plan and deliver our IT services

Oversee ITSM Framework: Ensure the ITSM framework aligns with organizational goals and best practices, overseeing key processes such as Incident, Change, Problem, Knowledge, Configuration, Service Catalogue, Service Transition and Service Level Management. Accountable for managing high quality ITIL compliant IT services to our users. Drive proactive actions and automation, identify and remediate potential service risks, reduction of MTTR and increase MTBF.

Liaison and Stakeholder Collaboration: Facilitate collaboration between the executive committee and project/service management teams and provide regular updates to stakeholders.

Security & Compliance: Work closely with Information Security, IT Domain Leads and Application/Data Owners to ensure stated IAM policies and procedures are in place.

Identity & Access Management Audit and Compliance: Provide support to internal and external audits of our Information Security Policies and IT General Controls (ITGC) including but not limited to user provisioning and deprovisioning process, access control mechanisms, user access reviews and certification.

Innovation: Keep in touch with enterprise architect and technology experts to discuss impact of new technologies and architecture roadmap.

Budget Management: Works along with the Senior Director of PMO/RMO and ITSM & above on yearly financial planning, though budget forecast and control

Team and People Management: ensure that direct and indirect reports are developed to their full potential and lead the recruiting process when required, so that we have the right profiles with the right skill-set working for the organization.

Language skills:



 * English (fluent)
   
   
   

Location:



 * Madrid, Spain
   
   
   

Must Have Experience



 * The ITSM Director must have extensive experience in IT service management, with a strong background in implementing and overseeing ITIL processes. This includes Incident, Change, Problem, Knowledge, Configuration, Service Catalogue, Service Transition and Service Level Management. Additionally, they should have hands-on managerial skills, excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities, and expertise in ITIL/Service Management.
 * Being able to clearly articulate goals and objectives and motivating and leading others towards their achievement.
 * Making decisions at the appropriate time, considering the needs of the situation, priorities, constraints, known risks, and the availability of necessary information and resources.
 * Understanding commercial considerations and ensuring alignment with them when making decisions or recommending actions. Experience with tools like ServiceNow and Power BI for developing performance dashboards and reports is also essential.
 * The role requires a minimum of 5 years of experience international experience, preferably in a managerial position.
   
   
   

Highly Desirable Experience



 * Broad knowledge of SAP and Service Now
 * Background in international organizations
 * Expertise in Big Data, Analytics and Machine Learning
 * Hospitality IT experience
   
   
   

Travel needs



 * Less than 15%
   
   
   

Soft skills:



 * Excellent good interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills
 * Passion for customer service, efficiency, effectiveness and quality
 * Good team player, willingness to assist others and share expertise
 * Highly influential, leadership, communication, efficiency, sociability
   
   
   

Education:



 * University Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Administration, or related field.
 * A recognized ITIL certification at Practitioner level or above is highly desirable.
   
   
   

Skills

SAP

IT Service Management

Service Now
