title: Director, ELA OMS QEHS
location: San Sebastián de los Reyes, Community of Madrid, Spain
description:
Welcome to the future of nuclear energy, where Westinghouse Electric Company is leading the field with expertise and progress to shape the power of tomorrow.

At Westinghouse, innovation is in our DNA. We are creative. We think differently. We reimagine the possible across the nuclear industry every day.

As the ELA (Europe, Latin America and Asia) OMS QEHS Director you will have full responsibility and organizational leadership of the Environmental, Health and Safety (EHS) and Quality functions for the ELA OMS organization.

The QEHS Director will collaborate with the Americas OMS EHS director, the global OMS Quality team and Global Operations Services leading a multi-country team in the range of 50 employees.

The role will report to the Senior Vice President, OMS QEHS and can be based in any European location where we have operations (Spain, France, Belgium, UK, Germany and Sweden).

Hybrid work schedule.

Ehs

Main Responsibilities:

Implement EHS Programs for ELA OMS.


 * Establish a governance structure to ensure the inclusion of all important partners implementation of programs/changes considering product differences, geographical differences, and performance needs/expectations.
 * Oversee ELA OMS EHS systems, programs, and policies that are compliant with applicable local, regional, national EHS regulations and standards.
 * Align with Westinghouse's EHS Management Systems.
 * Promote excellence in the organizations' performance in Environmental, and Health and Safety.
 * Provide leadership for EHS change plans aimed at driving a safety culture which enables business growth.
 * Develop measurement and reporting of EHS performance metrics to deliver tangible results for ELA OMS.
 * Evaluate trends for continuous improvement and corrective measures and measuring process effectiveness with both leading and lagging indicators.
 * Ensure resources for Environmental, Health, and Safety compliance along with Sustainability expectations globally.
 * Develop and support OMS EHS strategic vision, partner with Corporate and Business Unit EHS leaders to develop Westinghouse's EHS strategic vision and perform OMS EHS management reviews.
 * Maintain overall budget responsibility for all responsibilities ensuring process effectiveness and adherence to all applicable laws and regulations.
 * Coordinate needs, such as customer requests, supplier EHS, contractor EHS, mergers and acquisitions, LOA's.
 * Lead development of EHS integration plans for BU acquisitions and monitor execution of the plan to ensure integration that minimizes risk to the BU and Westinghouse.
 * Communicate and facilitate finding ELA OMS support for Westinghouse EHS internal audits.
   
   

Quality


 * Manage implementation of Westinghouse quality policies in compliance with local customer requirements, codes and standards.
 * Manage team of QA staff to support Westinghouse's Quality Assurance Program, manage Qualified Supplier List (QSL), and lead continuous improvement plans.
 * Ensure understanding of all European, Latin America and Asia quality requirements, as applicable, and understanding of Westinghouse's procedures.
 * Monitor and implement changes to Westinghouse global QMS.
 * Promote a culture of compliance and customer satisfaction.
 * Ensure quality program changes are communicated and implemented throughout Business Unit and Global Operations Services.
 * Interface on quality matters with the customers and the regulatory authority having jurisdication.
 * Communicate with all disciplines on quality matters (Engineering, Procurement, Production, Sales, EHS/HU).
 * Oversee internal and external audits, project audits, management assessments.
 * Lead corrective action program for ELA OMS.
 * Resolve solutions to quality problems and implement quality assurance best practices through continuous improvement.
 * Develop quality indoctrination training and other education programs.
 * Develop department budget and control expenditures to remain within budget.
 * Ensure staff are trained and manage employee performance to achieve department goals.
 * Compile quality performance data for annual management review.
   
   

Qualifications


 * Minimum Bachelor's degree (technical discipline).
 * MBA or Master's degree desirable.
 * 15+ years experience , including EH&S programs and quality program administration.
 * 5 + years experience in nuclear quality management.
 * ISO 14001 and/or 45001 knowledge and working experience.
 * English and any other European language required.
 * Demonstrated strong communication skills.
 * Travel required: Minimum 25%.
 * Global Travel required.
   
   

Why Westinghouse?

We know that to put forth your best effort, you need to be challenged and enjoy what you do in a supportive and respectful environment. We aim to maintain this balance by offering our employees the amenities, benefits and training they need to reach personal and professional goals.

Below is an example of what employees in Spain can expect:

    * Peer-to-peer recognition program.

    * Employee Assistance Program: confidential counseling and resources for employees, eligible dependents, and household members

 * Learning and development opportunities.

Our client is a Marine Survey and Environmental Consultancy company with its own accredited laboratories.

The company specializes in the marine environment and offshore projects, delivering geophysical, geotechnical, and environmental survey services to industries including oil & gas, renewables, submarine cables, and ports.

Create, validate, and propose geophysics methodologies (choice of equipment, procedures, and software) in line with Marine Survey industry best practices.

Problem-solving attitude.

Influential skills.

title: Key Account Manager (KAM)
description:
En Nortex estamos creciendo mucho y queremos seguir haciéndolo rodeados de gente con energía, con actitud y con ganas de salir ahí fuera a hablar con otras personas.




Nuestro trabajo va de vender servicios, sí.

Pero, sobre todo, va de escuchar, entender y construir relaciones. De ir poco a poco, de generar confianza y de hacer las cosas bien.




¿A quién buscamos?

A personas que:

 * Disfruten del trato con la gente
 * Se sientan cómodas saliendo a mercado y generando contacto
 * Tengan iniciativa, curiosidad y ganas de aprender
 * Sean buenas personas (esto aquí importa de verdad)
 * Quieran formar parte de un proyecto que crece rápido
 * Valoren un entorno donde trabajar a gusto es algo serio




Buscamos gente normal, con hambre y con actitud.




¿Hace falta experiencia?

Si tienes experiencia en servicios, facility management o limpieza, genial.

Si no la tienes, pero te reconoces en todo lo anterior, también queremos conocerte.




El conocimiento se aprende.

Las ganas, no.




¿Qué ofrecemos?

 * Un proyecto en pleno crecimiento, con recorrido real
 * Autonomía y espacio para aportar
 * Un equipo cercano y exigente a la vez
 * Aprendizaje y desarrollo profesional
 * Buen ambiente (de verdad, no de cartel)




Si al leer esto has pensado “igual este sitio encaja conmigo”,

probablemente no vas mal encaminado.




📩 Envíanos tu candidatura y hablamos.

title: Head of Procurement
description:
Job Description

Adevinta Spain is a leading company in the digital classified ads sector. The company manages well-known platforms in Spain such as Milanuncios, InfoJobs, and coches.net.

Its mission is to connect millions of people every month to exchange goods, services, and job opportunities.

The company is going through a significant transformation in which the Procurement team plays a key role to generate savings.

The ideal candidate will have a background in multiple indirect categories with a focus on IT, is familiar with companies that go through M&As and has excellent communication skills to engage with senior management.

The mission will be focused on understanding the needs of the company and building and delivering a solid spend management program.

Responsibilities


 * Manage spend in multiple indirect categories (Marketing, Product & Technology, Professional Services, Transactions and Corporate Services) focusing on local market needs
 * Manage the team
 * Work closely with the Global Strategic Sourcing team to align strategies, priorities and action plans
 * Be the key point of contact for local stakeholders (go-to-person for any Procurement-related topics in the market)
 * Drive stakeholder engagement locally and build fruitful collaborations in order to be seen as the local Procurement trusted advisor
 * Manage local stakeholders’ demand
 * Provide guidance and support to relevant local stakeholders about the Procurement process (Procurement ambassador) to ensure successful execution of projects & process compliance
 * Lead any Procurement-related activities locally (RFI / RFQ, negotiation, contract management, market screening, benchmarking…)
 * Drive supplier relationship management along with key stakeholders
 * Identify cost improvement opportunities to drive efficiency and positive bottom line impact
 * Support on the adjustment of ways of working, processes and operating model between Global Procurement and Local Procurement (whenever needed)
 * Enforce Global Procurement policies and standards
 * Stay updated on trends for different categories
 * Interact with other relevant functions (Legal, Finance…)
 * Report progress / achievements to line management whenever required
   
   

Profile


 * Master’s degree in Business Administration or Engineering
 * +5yr working experience in Procurement
 * +3yr category management / strategic sourcing experience
 * Hands-on & proactive; willing to challenge status quo and to work in an ever changing environment as part of the overall corporate transformation
 * Continuous improvement mindset to identify opportunities to leverage existing processes & tools
 * Data Driven
 * Ability to work in fast-paced environment and to deal with ambiguity
 * Strong business acumen
 * Good analytical and communication skills
 * Willingness to own and to drive end-to-end initiatives
 * Team player and team leader
 * Proficiency in English & Spanish (both written and verbal)
 * Knowledge of of Procurement systems such as SAP Ariba, SAP S/4 Hana, Coupa, others (beneficial but not mandatory)
 * Ability to work in a hybrid set-up (to cope with the home office & office working mode)

title: EHS Associate Director
description:
Insud Pharma is a recognised and respected company in the pharmaceutical and healthcare sector with more than 40 years of history and more than 7,000 employees in 50 countries specialising in the research, development, manufacture and marketing of active pharmaceutical ingredients and medicines for human and veterinary use.




Chemo, industrial area of the Insud Pharma group, is a world leader in R&D and manufacturing of more than 100 value-added active pharmaceutical ingredients and more than 122 medicines with more than 200 different product presentations. Chemo produces its own active pharmaceutical ingredients (API) as well as finished pharmaceutical products (FDF) covering the main therapeutic groups (Cardiology, Gastroenterology, Central Nervous System, Anti-infectives, Respiratory, Women's Health, Endocrinology, Dermatology and Urology).







General Responsibility

Lead and oversee the Environmental, Health, Safety (EHS&S) strategy and operations across INSUD PHARMA’s Pharma Fineshed Dosage Form (FDF) manufacturing sites (12 sites), ensuring compliance with legal requirements, corporate standards, and continuous improvement initiatives aligned with the company’s strategic objectives.




Specific Responsibilities

 * Drive the reduction of safety incidents and accidents with focus on key risks (inflammables, electrical, etc.)
 * Develop the new corporate EHS&S Master Plan 2024-2026.
 * Define, standardize and ensure the development of corporate EHS&S policies, standards, and guidelines.
 * Specify, harmonize and oversee the implementation of EHS&S technologies across pharmaceutical and chemical manufacturing sites.
 * Conduct internal audits, manage corrective action plans and ensure ongoing compliance and continuous improvement.
 * Identify, benchmark and promote best practices in safety, health, and environmental management.
 * Lead Operational Excellence and Double Impact projects, integrating EHS&S improvements with operational and cost efficiencies.
 * Foster a strong EHS&S culture, positioning EHS as a strategic business pillar.
 * Manage relationships and proceedings with governmental authorities and trade union committees.
 * Develop, monitor and report on key performance indicators (KPIs) and corporate dashboards for EHS&S performance.
 * Oversee audits and certifications related to continuous improvement and sustainability standards (ISO14001, ISO45001, Ecovadis, PSCI).
 * Ensure legal compliance in EHS&S and provide expert support to manufacturing locations.




Requirements and personal skills

 * Education: Degree in Engineering, Environmental Sciences, Industrial Safety or related fields. MBA will be an asset.
 * Languages: Fluent English and Spanish. Other languages will be an asset.
 * Experience (years/area): +10 years of proven experience in similar roles within the pharmaceutical or chemical industry. Have led HSE for a big Manufacturing site. Corporate roles and multi-site experience.
 * Specific Knowledge: Advanced knowledge of ISO14001, ISO45001, Ecovadis and PSCI standards and certifications. Experience managing internal and external audits, KPI development and corporate dashboard tools.
 * Travels: Available to travel once per week to the Sites in Spain (León) during the first year and then in addition once per quarter to international sites.
 * Personal skills:
 * Change Management
 * Proactivity and initiative
 * Ability to collaborate internally with production and maintenance and with with regulatory authorities and unions.
 * Excellence organizational skills.




COMMITMENT TO EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES




Insud Pharma is aware that business management has to be in line with the needs and demands of society, and therefore assumes the commitment to equal opportunities and treatment between men and women, as stated in the current legislation on the subject - Organic Law 3/2007, and we do not discriminate against any person on grounds of ethnicity, religion, age, sex, nationality, marital status, affective or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, or any other personal or social circumstance.

title: Marketing Director for Digital Segment
description:
Intellias’ Digital segment includes established verticals (for example Healthcare and Life Sciences) as well as emerging areas of expertise. To support strategic growth, we are hiring a Marketing Director with strong field marketing experience to develop and execute integrated marketing plans aligned to our business and vertical strategies.




What project we have for you

You will partner closely with sales, vertical leaders, technology and AI experts, and delivery teams to build brand awareness and generate demand. You will serve as the primary marketing partner for your assigned verticals, coordinating with central marketing teams (content, design, digital, PR) to bring campaigns to life across channels.

This role reports to the Director of Vertical Marketing and offers visibility with senior stakeholders, plus the chance to make a meaningful impact in a global company that moves quickly.




What you will do

Build the brand: Increase awareness and preference within your assigned markets by identifying high-impact opportunities, shaping messaging, and developing relationships with relevant influencers, communities, and media.

Plan and deliver campaigns: Design and run integrated, multi-channel campaigns, including messaging, channel plan, timelines, and budget ownership. Track performance and report results.

Own vertical channels: Manage and optimize vertical-specific marketing channels (for example LinkedIn presence, newsletters, and targeted nurture streams) in alignment with the campaign plan.

Partner with sales: Align on ICPs, target accounts, and priorities; support account-based and opportunity-based motions; develop field-ready assets; and drive measurable pipeline impact.

Support partnerships: Coordinate co-marketing with strategic partners in your verticals, including joint campaigns, events, content, and lead-sharing motions.

Events and field programs: Plan and execute webinars, executive roundtables, conferences, and sponsorships. Support on-site participation when needed.

Insights and optimization: Conduct market research, segmentation, and competitive analysis to inform plans; use data to improve conversion, cost per lead, and pipeline efficiency over time.




What you need for this

 * 8+ years of B2B marketing experience, including field marketing and events
 * Experience marketing to enterprise audiences, with a strong grasp of modern technology services and buyers
 * Industry familiarity in one or more of the following: Healthcare, iGaming, Travel, SaaS
 * Strong writing and content development skills, with the ability to produce high-quality communications for US, UK, and EU markets
 * Confidence working with senior stakeholders and presenting plans, performance, and recommendations clearly
 * Analytical, results-oriented mindset; comfortable operating in a lean environment with clear targets
 * Native-level English (written and spoken); experience working across time zones and multicultural teams
 * Ability to work independently, prioritize effectively, and adapt quickly in a fast-changing environment

Nice to have:

 * Experience in enterprise technology, consulting, or digital engineering services
 * Agency or client-facing account management experience
 * Additional languages
 * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field (including technology)




What it’s like to work at Intellias

At Intellias, where technology takes center stage, people always come before processes. By creating a comfortable atmosphere in our team, we empower individuals to unlock their true potential and achieve extraordinary results. That’s why we offer a range of benefits that support your well-being and charge your professional growth.

We are committed to fostering equity, diversity, and inclusion as an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without discrimination based on race, color, religion, age, gender, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

We welcome and celebrate the uniqueness of every individual. Join Intellias for a career where your perspectives and contributions are vital to our shared success.

title: Head of Legal
description:
Tu nuevo puesto




Buscamos un/a Head of Legal para liderar el área jurídica de un grupo empresarial consolidado. Tendrás un rol estratégico, acompañando al negocio en la toma de decisiones clave y garantizando un marco legal sólido que permita operar con seguridad, eficiencia y visión a largo plazo.

Serás la persona de referencia en materia legal dentro de la organización, con autonomía, visibilidad y capacidad real de impacto.




Entre tus principales responsabilidades estarán:




 * Asesorar al negocio en materia jurídico‑mercantil, asegurando la correcta aplicación de la normativa vigente.
 * Redactar, revisar, negociar y supervisar contratos mercantiles y documentación societaria.
 * Coordinar la relación con despachos externos y asegurar la coherencia jurídica en todas las áreas.
 * Impulsar y supervisar el cumplimiento normativo, identificando riesgos y proponiendo soluciones.
 * Acompañar a la dirección en decisiones estratégicas con impacto legal.

title: Senior Director, Product Design
description:
WHAT WE DO MATTERS:

At The Knot Worldwide, we champion celebration - and that starts with celebrating our people. Our employees are passionate dreamers, thoughtful doers, and lifelong learners who power meaningful moments for millions around the world. We’re united by authentic connection, shared purpose, and a deep commitment to the global community we serve. Here, flexibility and belonging go hand in hand with high performance. Driven by our core values, we believe the best ideas come from empowered teams: those who consistently collaborate with intention to design solutions, spark ideas, and drive impact. Our people are at the heart of our success.

WHAT WE DO MATTERS:

Here at The Knot Worldwide, we believe in doing work that matters. In 16 countries around the world, our leading family of brands (The Knot, WeddingWire, Bodas, Hitched, The Bash, The Bump, and more) inspire, inform, and celebrate our communities as they move through life’s milestones. 


ABOUT THE ROLE AND OUR TEAM:

The Knot Worldwide is seeking a Product Design leader to help drive the evolution of our planning experience, ensuring that we meet couples as soon as they get engaged and create value for them at every step of their wedding planning journey. You will be responsible for our acquisition and lifecycle strategy, optimizing our top of funnel experiences to delight couples from the start and help them meaningfully progress through planning. 

We seek a design leader who can solve challenging and complex user problems, create delightful and beautiful world-class products, hire and develop a strong team of product designers, and drive our organization’s design thinking and execution. The Knot Worldwide uses modern practices operating in agile, self-directed, performance-oriented squads who are driving outcomes through OKRs.

Product Design at The Knot Worldwide endeavors to solve important problems, develop excellent people, and operate as great partners with the rest of the company. If you’re attracted to this culture and a highly visible, impactful leadership role with a market leader that has its highest growth and impact ahead of it, we’d love to hear from you!

This role is located in BARCELONA with two days in office. 

RESPONSIBILITIES: 

 * Strategy
    * Partner with Product, Engineering, Data, and Marketing leadership on a product vision and strategy that aligns and inspires our organization to catapult us toward achieving aggressive business goals
    * Build and evangelize design thinking into how we work across product development, and represent design and user experience across the company’s leadership
    * Collaborate with front end engineering on a global pattern library approach that balances speed and creativity

 * Execution
    * Lead your team of full-stack product designers as they work within their squads to solve our users’ most important problems
    * Improve how design, product, engineering, and data work together with your fellow leaders across the three functions
    * Support and evolve a design culture within the product organization and across the established design organization

 * Hiring and team development
    * Continue growing and upgrading your design team
    * Improve our design hiring brand, e.g. through community representation or thought leadership  
    * Develop your design team into masters of their craft and thought leaders in the product community
      

SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATES HAVE:

 * 10+ years of digital product design and leadership including end-to-end execution, with proven track record running B2C experiences, shallow to deep, one time to repeated usage, innovation to iteration
 * Demonstrated experience across web and app experiences.
 * 2+ years of product design experience working on top of funnel and growth experiences
 * Demonstrated track record of creating and leading others to create beautiful industry-leading design solutions to solve important user and business problems
 * Strong business sense and desire to partner deeply with Sales to understand and support our core revenue stream
 * Facility with modern product development processes: agile, lean, iterative, outcome oriented, data driven (A/B testing), user research informed
 * Development orientation - you are eager to grow yourself and your team members
 * Strong presentation and written communication skills
 * Track record collaborating with product management, engineering, and marketing leaders

At The Knot Worldwide, we believe you are more than a resume and invite you to go for it, take the leap of faith, and apply for this job if it sparks your passion to join TKWW and make a difference!   


WORK MODEL:

This role is Together@TKWW-eligible and based near one of our office hubs. You’ll be expected to work in the office two days a week as part of our hybrid work model.

#management-track

 

At The Knot Worldwide, we believe you are more than a resume and invite you to go for it, take the leap of faith, and apply for this job. Together, we have an incredible opportunity to make it even easier for our customers to plan life’s most meaningful moments and for our small business owners to grow and scale. We would love to have you with us on our journey.   

WHAT WE LOVE ABOUT YOU:

 * Commit to our customers: You act as one team on behalf of our customers. You lead with head and heart, and build what matters for life's most meaningful moments.
 * Raise the bar: You define "great" and work backwards. You don't just accept how it's been done, but boldly define how it should be. You are unafraid to innovate, learn, and keep moving forward toward our shared vision.
 * Be all in: You believe in our mission and take ownership of your work. You debate openly to reach the best outcomes, speaking with clarity and care, embracing diverse perspectives, then commit fully.
 * Celebrate impact: You measure success by the outcomes you create. You hold yourself accountable to delivering value, while recognizing progress and the lessons learned along the way.  You love to win, together.

WHAT YOU LOVE ABOUT US:

We believe in a wide range of holistic offerings to support our employees so that they can live our values day in and day out. From mental wellbeing, physical health and financial planning, to engaging perks and discounts, we are in the business of celebrating and supporting the Moments that Matter, both in and out of the office.. We offer flexible vacation, generous parental leave, and prioritize initiatives that support the growth, development, and happiness of our people.

Together@TKWW is our approach to hybrid work. It’s designed to support how we work best: combining the flexibility we value with meaningful opportunities to connect in person. Whether a role is Together@TKWW-eligible or not, we believe in purposeful moments to come together, build strong relationships, and drive bold ideas forward.
 * For Together@TKWW-eligible roles, this includes regular in-office time to foster collaboration and connection.
 * For roles not eligible for in-office expectations, we support connection through virtual collaboration and intentional gatherings.

To facilitate in-person collaboration, we have office spaces in Barcelona, Spain; Delhi, India; Galway, Ireland; London, England; New York, NY; and Washington, D.C.

In addition to federal law requirements, The Knot Worldwide complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. The Knot Worldwide expressly prohibits any form of workplace harassment based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, or veteran status.

title: Head of Employment Law Spain (They/She/He)
description:
If you’re here, it’s because you’re looking for an exciting ride.

A ride that will fuel up your ambitions to take on a new challenge and stretch yourself beyond your comfort zone.

We’ll deliver a non-vanilla culture built on talent, where we work to amplify the impact on millions of people, paving the way forward together.

Not your usual app. We are the fastest-growing multi-category app connecting millions of users with businesses, and couriers, offering on-demand services from more than 170,000 local restaurants, grocers and supermarkets, and high street retail stores. We operate in more than 1500 cities across 23 countries.

Together we revolutionise the way people connect with their everyday needs, from delivering essentials to connecting our ecosystem of users through innovative solutions powered by technology. For us, every day is filled with purpose.

What makes our ride unique?

🤝 Our culture and strong values.

💪 Our career development philosophy.

🤝 Our commitment to being a force for good.

We have a vision: Building the largest marketplace in your city, to give access to anything in minutes. And this is where your ride starts.

Your Mission

Ready for a challenge? We’re looking for a forward-thinking Head of Employment Law to help us shape the future of Glovo. This is a pivotal role, where you’ll lead the advice to our core business, impacting how we engage, empower, and protect our Rider community. You’ll navigate some of the most complex litigations while forging a new operating model that sets new standards in the gig economy. This role is where deep legal expertise meets creative problem-solving to help redefine what fair, flexible, and sustainable work looks like in the gig economy.

In The Journey You Will

Lead the team of specialised employment lawyers who are redefining Glovo’s operating model in Spain, and oversee our HR operations in Yellow Park. Impact business operations and product with effective and out of the box advice.


 * Manage and coordinate litigation, investigations, and disputes, working with external counsel when needed.
 * Anticipate risks and recommend solutions to our management, including Public Affairs and settlement strategies.
 * Oversee strategic engagement with Works Councils and Unions to foster constructive social dialogue.
 * Builds strong relationships with executive and functional heads at Glovo, while working closely with and reporting to our parent entity.
   
   

What You Will Bring To The Ride

You are a Spanish qualified employment lawyer, with strong litigation experience, and 6 PQE, ideally combining experience in a reputable law firm, and advising tech companies.


 * You have led high performing teams, and are able to build strong relationships with your team, executives and functional leads through credibility and mutual respect.
 * You have excellent verbal and written communication in both English and Spanish; you have mastered executive communication and are able to simplify complex legal concepts for non-lawyers.
 * You have a hands-on approach to work and are always ready to roll up your sleeves to get things done.
 * Your advice is pragmatic and impactful, and you are able to balance legal risk with business opportunity.
   
   

Individuals representing diverse profiles, and abilities, encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Even if it seems you don't meet our musts don't let it stop you, we are all about finding the best talent out there! Skills can be learned, and embracing diversity is invaluable.

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves

🌟 An enticing equity plan that lets you own a piece of the action.

💪 Top-notch private health insurance to keep you at your peak.

🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!

💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.

🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.

🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!

👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.

🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.

Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds - all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

title: Director/a de Creatividad y Diseño
description:
Misión del Rol:

Impulsar la transformación del área de Branding y Creatividad mediante la definición de una visión estratégica clara, la organización eficiente del equipo y la coordinación fluida con las unidades de negocio y el resto de áreas de marketing. El rol es clave para garantizar que las soluciones creativas, las campañas y los proyectos de producción transmitan de forma sólida la personalidad de la marca, conecten con los clientes y generen impacto tangible en los objetivos del negocio.




Funciones y responsabilidades:

1. Liderazgo Estratégico de Marca

 * Definir la estrategia creativa, de diseño y de identidad visual del grupo, garantizando su alineación con los objetivos estratégicos de la organización.
 * Impulsar la evolución y maduración de las marcas del portfolio en un contexto de transformación.
 * Asegurar la coherencia visual y verbal en todos los canales, campañas y unidades de negocio.




2. Gestión, Organización y Desarrollo del Equipo

 * Liderar, estructurar y desarrollar un equipo creativo multidisciplinar (diseño gráfico, dirección de arte, producción, contenido gráfico, etc.).
 * Establecer modelos de trabajo eficientes y procesos claros que garanticen plazos, calidad y coordinación.
 * Promover una cultura creativa innovadora, colaborativa y orientada a resultados.




3. Coordinación con las distintas unidades de negocio y stakeholders internos

 * Trabajar de la mano de los responsables de marketing de cada línea de negocio para aterrizar la estrategia en campañas y proyectos concretos.
 * Participar en la definición de necesidades creativas para proyectos transversales de la compañía (digital, producto, comunicación corporativa, trade, eventos, etc.)
 * Alinear prioridades y cargas de trabajo entre áreas internas para garantizar eficiencia y consistencia.




4. Gestión de Producción y Relación con Partners Externos

 * Supervisar la producción de campañas, materiales gráficos, contenidos audiovisuales y piezas creativas en múltiples formatos y soportes.
 * Definir qué funciones deben ejecutarse internamente y cuáles externalizar, creando un modelo híbrido ágil y escalable.
 * Gestionar la relación con agencias creativas, estudios, productoras y otros proveedores, garantizando calidad y cumplimiento de expectativas.




5. Innovación y Mejora Continua

 * Impulsar nuevas líneas de trabajo creativo, explorando formatos emergentes, tendencias visuales y soluciones digitales.
 * Asegurar que los procesos del área evolucionen hacia un modelo más eficiente, flexible y orientado al impacto.
 * Estimular la creatividad del equipo y fomentar la búsqueda constante de nuevas ideas y enfoques.




Requisitos del Puesto:

 * Formación Académica: Estudios en Publicidad, Diseño Gráfico, Comunicación Audiovisual, Marketing, Bellas Artes o afines.
 * Formación complementaria en Branding, Dirección de Arte o Creatividad Estratégica es muy valorada.
 * Experiencia sólida (+6 años) en roles de dirección creativa, branding o diseño, preferiblemente en sectores con un alto componente experiencial como turismo, retail, ocio o digital.
 * Experiencia demostrada liderando equipos creativos.
 * Experiencia en coordinación con agencias externas, productoras y proveedores.
 * Profundo conocimiento de identidad de marca, diseño visual y campañas 360º.
 * Dominio de procesos creativos de principio a fin: conceptualización, diseño, producción y entrega.
 * Experiencia en gestión de proyectos y planificación de recursos en entornos dinámicos.
 * Conocimiento de las principales herramientas de diseño, creatividad y gestión de flujos de trabajo.




Qué ofrece la Organización:

Forma parte de una empresa líder en el sector turístico, inmersa en un momento clave de evolución y transformación, donde la creatividad y la identidad de marca son pilares estratégicos. Un entorno colaborativo, humano, inspirador y lleno de oportunidades para crecer, innovar y dejar huella.

title: Ramp Safety Manager España (sector aeroportuario)
description:
Newrest busca un /a RAMP SAFETY MANAGER ESPAÑA parasus actividades de catering aéreo en España para el centro de Madrid (San Fernandode Henares)

Funciones

Safety


 * Mantener actualizados procedimientos y los manuales de formación según normativa y/o requerimientos de las compañías aéreas.
 * Enlace para la implementación de procedimientos, autorizaciones y cambios de normativa emitidos por la sede central, las autoridades internacionales, nacionales, locales, así como por las aerolíneas.
 * Auditorías internas de seguridad operacional y vehículos.
 * Creación y divulgación de boletines de Seguridad Operacional en plataforma para todos los centros de España.
 * Concienciar y fomentar los valores de seguridad en plataforma, así como los procedimientos operacionales durante las formaciones y las operaciones.
 * Mantener actualizados los PVSO (Plan de Vigilancia de Seguridad Operacional) para cada aeropuerto.
 * Coordinar con AENA y otras entidades externas los aspectos de Seguridad Operacional pertinentes.
 * Apoyar a los centros en las auditorías externas, tanto de Autoridades, como de Clientes, cuyo alcance sea el área bajo su responsabilidad.
 * Apoyar a los centros en accidentes graves.
 * Análisis e Investigación de accidentes/ incidentes.
 * Análisis de los riesgos y peligros en plataforma.
 * Asesoramiento y ayuda al resto de las bases operativas de Newrest.
   
   

Security


 * Enlace con las autoridades nacionales y locales.
 * Asegurar la correcta ejecución y la actualización de los programas de seguridad.
 * Renovación de permisos y licencias requeridos por las Autoridades para el desarrollo de nuestras actividades en aeropuertos.
 * Asegurar el cumplimiento de la normativa vigente en Security contemplando todos los aspectos.
 * Asesoramiento y ayuda al resto de las bases operativas de Newrest.
 * Formación y concienciación en AVSEC de todo el personal involucrado en las operaciones.
   
   

Requisitos


 * Experiencia en puestos similares de al menos 5 años.
 * Imprescindible permiso de conducir B.
 * Formación Profesional Grado Superior / Bachillerato.
 * Usuario/a habitual de Microsoft Office.
 * Inglés avanzado hablado y escrito (se hablará a diario)
 * Disponibilidad para viajar a nivel nacional.
   
   

SOBRE Newrest

Los valores de Newrest son humildad, calidad, eficacia y sentido de laresponsabilidad.

Con más de 60.000 empleados en 53 países, Newrest es un actor mundialindependiente de la restauración aérea, y el único operador que Interviene enTodos los sectores: restauración aérea, restauración institucional, bases devida, restauración ferroviaria y comercio minorista.

Newrest es una empresa comprometida con la Igualdad de oportunidadesentre mujeres y hombres.

Para más información sobre Newrest: https://www.newrest.eu/

title: Sales Director
description:
Nuestro cliente es una compañía especializada en el desarrollo de soluciones industriales donde el diseño de procesos, la ingeniería aplicada y la fiabilidad operativa son factores críticos. La organización actúa en proyectos de alto componente técnico, integrando conocimiento ingenieril y capacidad productiva. Actualmente se encuentra en una fase de evolución de sus estructuras para dar respuesta a entornos industriales cada vez más complejos y regulados por lo que buscamos:




SALES DIRECTOR (provincia Castellón)




Puesto

En dependencia directa de la Dirección General, la persona seleccionada será responsable de liderar, dinamizar y desarrollar la estrategia comercial, garantizando el crecimiento sostenible del negocio, la rentabilidad de las cuentas y la consolidación de relaciones a largo plazo con clientes estratégicos.

Entre sus principales funciones se encuentran:

 * Dirección y desarrollo del equipo comercial
 * Definición y seguimiento de objetivos, KPIs y presupuestos.
 * Elaboración e implementación de la estrategia comercial y de desarrollo de negocio.
 * Participación en negociaciones de alto nivel con clientes estratégicos.
 * Supervisión del lanzamiento y seguimiento de proyectos.
 * Control de la rentabilidad de las cuentas.
 * Coordinación transversal con las áreas de ingeniería, producción, calidad y logística.
 * Gestión y optimización de herramientas CRM.
 * Representación de la empresa ante clientes, ferias y reuniones estratégicas.




Perfil

 * Formación universitaria en ADE, Economía o Ingeniería (valorable MBA o formación de postgrado en gestión comercial).
 * Experiencia mínima de 5 años en posiciones similares dentro de entornos industriales, preferiblemente en sectores técnicos.
 * Sólida experiencia en gestión de equipos comerciales y desarrollo de negocio.
 * Conocimientos avanzados en CRM, gestión de presupuestos y análisis de rentabilidad.
 * Nivel alto de inglés (C1).
 * Valorable experiencia en entornos internacionales.




A nivel competencial, se valorará especialmente:

 * Liderazgo y capacidad de influencia.
 * Orientación a resultados.
 * Habilidades de negociación y comunicación.
 * Capacidad analítica, planificación y organización.
 * Iniciativa y autonomía.

title: Head of content - EdTech (French fluent 🇫🇷)
description:
What if your next playground was transforming a massive Personal Brand ecosystem into a global, iconic Company Brand? What about challenging the status quo of an entire industry and building a brand you can be proud of, and that will help millions of people?

Then you’ll love this.

We’re not looking for a "Social Media Manager" to follow a calendar. We’re looking for a Head of Content (Brand & Content).

Someone who understands that to win today, you don't just need "content", you need a Movement. You will have the freedom to propose bold, unconventional concepts and the resources to bring them to life immediately.

We need a strategist like you who will bridge the gap between David Laroche’s personal influence and Paradox’s institutional power.

A Brand Architect, capable of orchestrating a complex dual-strategy: scaling a personal brand while simultaneously building a standalone Company Brand that outlasts its founder.

A Creative Disruptor, you hate "best practices" because they lead to average results. You constantly hunt for the "Purple Cow", the angle, the format, or the story that no one else is daring to tell.

A "Doer" with Taste, you don't just float ideas in a meeting room. You script them, you drive them, and you ship them.

This is a full-time position, carried out remotely or from our beautiful offices in Dubaï or Lisbon. French and English fluency is required.

About Paradox

WHAT WE DO

At Paradox, we are convinced that greatness is trainable.

That’s why we want to empower people at scale, by leveraging cutting edge technologies like AI, and crafting user experiences with outstanding aesthetics.

We do that by merging :


 * Human Intelligence (coaches, peers, and mentors)
 * Structured Intelligence (protocols, mental models, rituals),
 * Technological Intelligence (AI personalization, feedback loops, adaptive journeys)
   
   

To create one seamless ecosystem: Paradox OS.

OUR BELIEFS

We believe self-help is broken.

Most learning platforms are used the same way : consuming generic theories, getting quick highs, ending with no real change.

At Paradox, we believe people deserve more.

More than content. More than motivation.

They deserve a system that knows them, one that adapts, challenges, and grows with them.

We believe that every human learns differently, and that technology can do more than entertain or save time.

It can translate insight into transformation, through AI personalization, lean experimentation, and deep psychological understanding.

Because learning shouldn’t just make you feel inspired for a day, it should change how you live, think, and create.

OUR VISION

At Paradox, we want to re-invent how people grow.

We do not build another course or coaching app but the Operating System for Greatness.

Founded by David Laroche, a creator followed by over 2 million people and known for his interviews with icons like Tony Parker, Mike Horn, Richard Branson, and Gad Elmaleh, Paradox aims to build something that will live beyond any individual (including its founder), for decades to come.

Our ambition is to compete with players like Mindvalley (150M ARR), but with a sharper vision, grounded protocols for achievers, people who want more for their life, refusing mediocrity.

To do that, we bring together designers, tech, coaches, and filmmakers to create learning experiences that are cinematic, precise, and effective.

We move fast, aim high, and hold ourselves to the standards of Apple, Spotify, and MasterClass. No fluff, focused on meaningful impact.

While others hide their numbers, we want to let our results speak loudly for us.

Get obsessed with transforming our clients’ lives and let those transformations be our marketing.

And that’s where you come in.

YOUR MISSION 🚀

Transform a "Personal Brand" into a "Top-Tier Media".

You lead the Content Factory not by administrative management, but by creative authority. Your goal is to produce content that is so good, unique, and aligned with our values that it makes our competitors look outdated.

Your 4 Major Responsibilities


 * Architect the "Dual-Brand" Strategy
 * Build a symbiotic strategy where David Laroche’s personal brand and Paradox’s company brand reinforce each other without cannibalization.
 * Propose radical new formats (documentaries, reality shows, conceptual shorts) that shatter the boring codes of the "Self-Help" industry.
 * Define distinct, sharp editorial lines for each platform: Depth & Search for YouTube, Aesthetic & Vibe for Instagram, and Authority & Authenticity for LinkedIn.
 * Radical Concepting & End-to-End Delivery
 * Take responsibility for the entire production chain by writing the hooks, storyboarding the narrative, and ensuring the final cut matches the vision.
 * Deliver what you pitch: if your concept requires a specific motion designer, a drone operator, or a niche expert, you find them, hire them, and onboard them to bring the vision to life.
 * Treat David as the "Actor" on set: you ensure scripts, set, and direction are flawless so he can just show up, perform, and leave without any mental load, trusting you to handle every detail of the final output.
 * Command the Content Factory
 * Take full leadership of the internal content team (Factory Manager, Video Editors, Graphic Designers…) by setting the tempo, the culture, and the daily standards of excellence.
 * Provide high-level artistic direction and act as the final quality check; nothing gets published if it doesn't meet the "Paradox Standard" of aesthetics and storytelling.
 * Challenge the team to constantly upskill, pushing them for sharper cuts, better sound design, and stronger visual storytelling.
 * Fuel the Growth Engine
 * Treat content as our primary acquisition channel by collaborating with the Head of Growth to produce assets specifically designed to convert in Ads.
 * Analyze retention graphs, CTR, and CPL to iterate based on feedback, killing what fails and aggressively scaling what works.
   
   

DO YOU RECOGNIZE YOURSELF?

Experience


 * Proven Content Builder: You have successfully grown a media brand, a YouTube channel, or a personal brand to significant scale (100k+ followers or millions of views). You understand the mechanics of virality and retention.
 * Editorial Leadership: You have experience managing a creative team (editors, designers) and know how to give feedback that elevates a "good" video to a "masterpiece."
 * Elite Scripting Skills: You can show us scripts you wrote that performed. You know how to structure a narrative, write a hook that stops the scroll, and keep an audience engaged for 20+ minutes.
 * Performance Fluency: You have worked in environments where content had to drive business results (ads, launches, conversion), not just vanity metrics.
   
   

PERSONALITY


 * Intolerant to Mediocrity: You have "Taste." You are physically pained by a bad font, a clumsy cut, or a weak headline. You fight for the details that others ignore.
 * Psychology Obsessed: You don't just "make content"; you study human nature. You constantly ask why people click, why they share, and what emotion drives them.
 * A "Doer" with Authority: You command respect not by title, but by competence. You can jump into a project, write the script, direct the shoot, and fix the edit if needed.
 * No Ego, Just Impact: You don't fall in love with your ideas. You accept that even the best concepts can flop. When it happens, you don't take it personally; you analyze the data, pivot immediately, and ship the next concept with renewed energy.
   
   

Language

French: Native / Bilingual

English: Fluent English, you can hold a conversation on specific details with English-speaking colleagues.

Our chosen language for written communication is English.

IT MIGHT NOT BE FOR YOU

❌ If you need clarity before chaos — wrong place.

❌ If you prefer balance over intensity — wrong season.

❌ If you don't genuinely care about the topics we cover — wrong place.

❌ If you want to strategize without getting your hands dirty in scripting, directing, or the editing suite — wrong role.

WHY JOIN US ❤️

Help us market a product that fuses neuroscience, design, and AI to reinvent how humans grow.

Work with filmmakers, psychologists, engineers, and creatives obsessed with impact.

In the heart of our headquarters stands the 100 000 Dreams Wall,

Every photo on that wall is a life rewritten:

“I ran a marathon.”

“I started my company.”

“I found peace with my father.”

You will play a crucial role in the development of an Iconic Product.

Growth & Ambition


 * > 🏅Being surrounded by A-players who strive to perform
   
   

Impact & Meaning


 * > Playing a crucial role in the development of a company whose content and products will be used by millions of people
 * > Waking up in the morning feeling like you are making a difference is something!
   
   

We receive messages every day from clients who have successfully lost weight, quit smoking, reach a milestone in their business, built better relationships, and made positive changes in their lives.


 * > ❤️ Contributing to the Paradox Foundation (our non-profit), dedicated to empower teenagers
   
   

But that's not all, we believe that driven people deserve…

Freedom & Well Being 🗽


 * > 😎A company culture based on autonomy & accountability
 * > 🗽Flexible working hours
 * > 🌎A remote culture from day one with the freedom to work from wherever you want
 * > 🏤Magnificent office in Dubai, Paris, Lisbon - it feels like home :)
 * > 💻Work on Mac, for us it counts :)
 * > 🏀350€ per year paid by the company to take care of yourself ( for your gym, or dance class or whatever make you feel great)
 * > 📚150€ of books or education - At Paradox, we are learner lovers and we want to support you in that
 * > 🔥6 1:1coachings - A Paradox Coach supporting you on anything personal or professional, we want you to live your best life
   
   

How To Apply 📃

That job description resonated with you?

Click on "Apply for this position" and fill out a short questionnaire (7 to 8 minutes) to tell us more about yourself and send us your CV!

Hiring process

📃 A 6 to 8 minutes application survey.

💻 A 15-30 minutes video call with Timy, our HR, to assess the fit between the position, the company, and your own career path.

💻 A video-call focused on your career with Quentin, VP Marketing, to review your background & see how we can match your desires with our needs.

💻 A culture-fit video call to check in depth the alignment between you and our values and culture.

💼 Reference calls to get in-depth insights about how you work in different environments, make your onboarding easier, and give everybody on the team the insurance to be surrounded by A-Players.

🤗 Welcome aboard!

About Our Hiring Process

Our recruitment process is tailored to do our best to avoid that you or us realize that we’re not a perfect fit in two or six months. It might seem long, but every step serves a purpose and will allow us to get to know you better as much as it’ll help you understand more where you’ll be stepping in.

We also want you to have time to ask all of your questions regarding your job description, the long-term vision of your team and the company, our values, with the relevant interlocutor in front of you.

The steps mentioned above can be rearranged depending on yours and the interviewer’s availability.

If you’re not joining to tweak landing pages.

If you’re here to engineer behavioral change at scale, for millions.

Welcome to Paradox.

Let’s build the Operating System for Greatness.

title: Business Development Director (Real Estate)
description:
Business Development Director (Southern and Central Eastern Europe)




At LHH Executive Search we are currently supporting our client, an international leader in Real Estate services, that is looking for a Business Development Director Southern and Central Eastern Europe, based in Madrid.




Reporting to the Global Head of Business Development, your main responsibilities will include to:




 * Carry out competitive and sector monitoring, identify growth levers (prospect and customer segments and promising markets), support by the global "market intelligence" team within the Business Development structure.
 * Anticipate industry and market developments, propose commercial directions, improvements or modifications to the company products/services.
 * Ensure competitive intelligence on market developments, particularly in terms of new customer acquisition, changes in the offer (new services or new asset classes) and pricing standards.
 * Prepare, in collaboration with the Business Units, the company projected revenue budget, ensuring that the objectives are consistent with the market potential.
 * Define prospects, identify decision-makers, qualify the customer-prospect database.
 * Participate in the development of the commercial action plan for its scope, in line with the overall commercial strategy and, consequently, with the company global strategy.
 * Respond to request for proposals: compile and draft files, cost proposals in conjunction with the hierarchy and the dedicated business team (managed by him at the time of the call).
 * Prospect new lead accounts, high potentials, identify their needs and growth opportunities.
 * Define suitable commercial proposals, organize commercial meetings.
 * Evaluate the profitability of mandates for new clients and follow the defined approval process.
 * Draw up a business plan and a map of target customer accounts, define specific actions.
 * Activate and retain your active customer portfolio, negotiate with strategic or sensitive accounts, ensure visits, negotiate contracts, services, prices, discounts.
 * Negotiate and build projects, build and write tailor-made commercial offers, provide clients with methodological and organisational support in project management.
 * Target, organize and/or participate in external business events, which can be an opportunity to meet potential customers.
 * Work alongside each Business Unit and Brand Marketing on an external communication strategy that better communicates the company offer, its uniqueness and expertise, especially to the target customer market.
 * Maintain a business network through local contacts to strengthen the company's positioning and reputation and potentially generate business partnership agreements.
 * Direct management of the teams that carry out the sales action plan and customer strategy in the markets where the company is present.




We are looking for a senior real estate professional with a minimum of 5 years of experience in a similar role, bringing a deep knowledge of the sector (specialist in a profession and knowledge of all services throughout the life cycle of an asset and real estate value chain: development, capital markets, asset management, property management, leasing, etc). You will bring a master’s degree coupled with a business or management school diploma (a dual commercial and technical training is preferred). In addition, it is important that you combine a good knowledge of sales techniques and negotiation B2B or B2C, as well as tender management and project management (agile mode). You will manage a commercial team, so it is necessary that you bring leadership, dynamism, strategic and operational vision, ability to influence and persuade, and excellent communication and interpersonal skills. Fluency in English is mandatory, French is desired.




If you are looking for a strategic role that is key to ensure the growth and synergies across different markets in a European leader in real estate services, send your updated CV to this Business Development Director role and we will connect.

title: IT Project Manager (Madrid - Hybrid)
description:
The company

Our customer is a technology-based startup with solid funding that is currently undergoing expansion.




They will hire the selected candidate as an internal, permanent employee based in Madrid to provide services to their global organization.




Description of the position

We’re looking for a IT Project Manager for their global operations in Madrid. He/she will join the CIO Technical Project Office, acting as a strategic connector between IT, technical teams, and business stakeholders.




As a Project Manager, he/she will be responsible for coordinating strategic projects, ensuring alignment across technical domains, and establishing portfolio management standards.




He/she will work closely with senior leadership, engineering, IT, and PMO teams to provide visibility, consistency, and execution across initiatives. And also will be responsible for leading and coordinating some projects within the portfolio.




Key Responsibilities and Tasks

 * Translate strategic vision into actionable projects and oversee their execution.
 * Align project methodologies, reporting, and governance with global PMO standards.
 * Define and uphold project management standards across IT initiatives.
 * Manage the IT project portfolio, including planning, prioritization, and resource allocation.
 * Monitor progress, risks, and performance across projects.
 * Facilitate collaboration and communication among stakeholders and teams.
 * Deliver projects as part of the project delivery team.
 * Provide tools, guidance, and support to improve project outcomes.
 * Ensure compliance with internal policies and external regulations.
 * Promote continuous improvement and a culture of transparency and accountability.




Working Experience

More than 5 years of hands-on experience in Project Management or a similar role.




Tech skills (required)

 * Proven experience in project management within multinational environments.
 * Experience working closely with CIOs, PMOs, and cross-functional technical teams.
 * Knowledge of project governance frameworks and portfolio management tools.
 * Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and organizational skills.
 * Strong leadership and team collaboration skills.
 * Comfortable working in a fast-paced, agile startup environment.




Tech skills (preferred)

 * Experience in startups or scale-ups, especially in tech or SaaS.
 * Knowledge of MS Project, Planner, Dynamics 365, Jira, ServiceNow, and similar platforms.
 * Knowledge of PMP, Prince2, and Agile Scrum methodologies.
 * Understanding IT strategy, architecture, and application of lifecycle management.




Soft skills

 * Global Mindset: Experience working in a multi-national environment or on international teams.
 * Team Collaboration: Strong interpersonal skills and a team-oriented attitude.
 * Communication: Clear and concise communicator, both verbally and in writing.
 * Continuous Learning: Eagerness to stay updated on networking trends and emerging technologies.




Education and Training

 * Bachelor's Degree or vocational training qualification: In information technology, telecommunications, or a related field.
 * Certifications: Relevant certifications are a plus (PMP, Scrum Master…)




Languages

 * Spanish: At least a medium level is required. B2 level or higher.
 * English: Excellent Business English required (it is their working language). B2/C1 level.




Job conditions

 * Job location: Tres Cantos (Madrid).
 * Work permit: European Union nationality. Otherwise, an EU/Spain work permit is required.
 * Employment type: Permanent Full Time, as an internal employee.
 * Salary: Depending on qualifications and experience.
 * Work from home: Hybrid working model, including the possibility of working from home (70%), but according to the specific needs that may arise from the perspective of project development, department, clients, and/or partners.




If you are interested, please apply here or send an email to pm@montarelo.com, including in the subject: ‘Technical Project Manager’, along with your English CV.

title: Director, Talent Science
description:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.

The company is founder led, profitable and growing.

Our Talent Science team has a mission to ensure that we follow objective, scientifically rigorous and efficient processes to select the very best new colleagues regardless of location, culture or background. We work closely with our key stakeholders to ensure that decisions are being made in the right way and that our hiring model supports the selection of world class talent. We lead an effective hiring ecosystem to deliver talent acquisition excellence. We leverage data to enable evidence-based decisions, weaving behavioural science throughout our selection process, working closely with our Hiring Leadership team who drive hiring at Canonical.

Due to growth of our team we are now hiring into 4 new leadership roles in our Talent Science team, to lead on delivery excellence. You will work directly with the Global Head of Talent Science to plan and drive work within the team against our strategic objectives. With overall accountability for project delivery relating to hiring outcomes you will drive efficiencies and create new processes whilst coaching and developing team members to grow their careers.

You will bring an analytical and objective approach to work and be seasoned in making data driven decisions relating to talent outcomes. Your experience will include being able to drive complete project roadmaps, experience interacting with senior leadership on talent related topics leveraging your thought leadership experience and the ability to influence and steer talent agendas internally. You will be capable of stepping up to lead the function when required. Experience of working with career frameworks and hiring outcomes is an essential requirement of this role.

Location: This role will be based remotely in the EMEA region.

The role entails


 * Leading and delivering the strategic Talent Science project roadmap using exceptional project management capability and influencing skills to manage multiple projects at any given time.
 * Leveraging expertise from your career to date to design and implement solutions to complex problems that ultimately ensure we hire exceptional talent.
 * Ensuring all talent selection initiatives map to high performance outcomes and career frameworks.
 * Designing professional processes and frameworks, allowing efficient delivery of our agenda.
 * Coaching and mentoring team members to grow and develop skill sets specifically around analytical capabilities and thought leadership, developing training materials and documentation to support team members.
 * Driving and representing the team in the absence of the Head of, acting as a credible and trusted adviser and an inspiring leader.
 * Working closely with company leaders to understand, appreciate and communicate our vision for talent at Canonical.
   
   

What we are looking for in you


 * Exceptional academic track record from both high school and university, with an undergraduate degree in a psychology or people based subject.
 * A track record of team leadership within the technology or consulting industry, with exceptional project management, to lead roadmap execution and delivery.
 * Proven ability to coach and mentor a team to deliver exceptional results.
 * Deep understanding of selection, career frameworks and building internal products to ensure that talent selection criteria matches desired outcomes.
 * Advanced analytical ability to dissect and analyse data using either SQL, Python and/or statistical analysis.
 * Exceptional written and spoken English with strong presentation skills for a senior audience.
 * Ability to travel internationally 4 times a year for company events.
   
   

Nice-to-have skills


 * Advanced psychometric assessments with an excellent understanding of the marketplace and vendors
 * A deep understanding of human behaviour and what drives intrinsic motivation
 * Experience of using Superset and advanced dashboarding
   
   

What we offer colleagues

We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus or commission. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.


 * Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
 * Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
 * Annual compensation review
 * Recognition rewards
 * Annual holiday leave
 * Maternity and paternity leave
 * Employee Assistance Programme
 * Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
 * Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events
   
   

About Canonical

Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.

Canonical is an equal opportunity employer

We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: Sales & Events Development Manager California, USA
description:
Sales Development Manager




We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Sales Development Manager for a leading

restaurant group in the United States. This role is designed for an ambitious, "hunter"

professional who thrives on driving business growth, building a robust client portfolio, and

contributing to the success of our catering and events operations. The ideal candidate will be

focused on securing new business, expanding relationships with corporate clients, event

agencies, and key industry players, while promoting both in-restaurant and off-site catering

services.

Key Responsibilities:

● Sales & Business Development: Drive business growth by identifying and securing

new business opportunities with corporate clients, event agencies, and potential

partners. This includes selling group events, buyouts, and bespoke catering solutions for

external venues.

● Lead Generation & CRM Strategy: Implement CRM strategies to track, manage, and

optimize lead generation and conversion, ensuring that business development efforts are

focused and results-driven.

● Collaborate with Marketing: Work closely with the marketing team to design targeted

campaigns aimed at corporate clients. Use analytics to track and optimize outreach

efforts for maximum lead generation.

● Strategic Planning: Develop and execute business development strategies that align

with the company’s long-term goals and market trends, ensuring sustained growth in the

events and catering sector.

● Client Relationship Management: Build and nurture dynamic relationships with existing

clients and prospects, ensuring exceptional communication and service delivery tailored

to the American market's expectations.

● Event Solutions Sales: Promote bespoke catering and event solutions, including

in-restaurant group bookings, buyouts, and high-end off-site catering experiences,

working with clients to customize events to meet their needs.

● Market Presence & Networking: Represent the company at key industry events,

conferences, and networking opportunities to increase brand visibility, build relationships,

and generate sales leads.

● Performance Monitoring & Reporting: Set measurable sales goals, track progress,

and regularly report on key performance indicators (KPIs), including sales targets, lead

conversion rates, and market penetration.




Required Skills and Qualifications:

● Proven Hunter Mentality: A demonstrated track record of consistently exceeding sales

targets, with a strong focus on new business acquisition in the catering and events

sector.

● Experience: Minimum of 5 years in sales or business development, preferably within the

catering, events, or hospitality industry. Experience with corporate clients and high-end

event sales is a plus.

● CRM Expertise: Strong ability to implement and optimize CRM strategies to enhance

lead management, track sales performance, and convert leads into successful business

opportunities.

● Strategic Vision: Proven ability to create and execute business development plans that

align with company goals and drive substantial growth.

● Leadership & Collaboration: While this is primarily a sales-focused role, the ability to

work collaboratively with cross-functional teams, particularly marketing and event

operations, is essential to achieving sales objectives.

● PR and Networking Skills: Excellent communication and public relations skills, with the

ability to represent the company in high-stakes situations and at industry events.

● Adaptability: Willingness to travel as required for client meetings, events, and

conferences. Flexibility to relocate to the United States is a plus.

● Cultural Competence: Familiarity with the American market and its corporate culture is

a plus, though not required.

● Fluent in English: Strong verbal and written communication skills in English are a must.




The ideal candidate will be an experienced sales professional who is passionate about the

events and catering industry and thrives in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. If you are driven

by results and looking to take your sales career to the next level with an innovative, high-end

Spanish restaurant group, we want to hear from you.
