title: Project Director - Architect/Engineer
description:
HESCO, an engineering company specialized in providing comprehensive structural engineering services and execution projects; in addition to, Construction Management, Architecture, Interior Design, Civil Engineering, Infrastructure and Electromechanical designs; based in Spain with offices in Jeddah, Riyadh, Makkah, Syria, Turkey, Egypt, Lebanon and United Kingdom.




We are looking for a Project Director with over 20 years of experience in multinational companies within the Luxury Residential, Iconic Buildings, Sports, Hospitality, Infrastructure, Entertainment, and Landscaping sectors. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of delivering high-quality projects from conception to completion. This is a fantastic opportunity for an experienced professional with a global profile who is ready to take on new and exciting challenges in a dynamic and fast-paced environment.




*Availability for work in Arabia.




MAIN ACCOUNTABILITIES




 * Oversee and direct construction projects from inception to completion, ensuring that all deliverables are met on time, within budget, and with the highest quality standards.
 * Review the project in depth to establish a clear timeline of deliverables and accurately estimate costs at each stage of the project.
 * Supervise all construction activities on-site and off-site, ensuring compliance with construction standards, safety regulations, and required permits.
 * Coordinate and manage construction workers and subcontractors, ensuring that all teams work cohesively to meet project goals.
 * Select tools, materials, and equipment necessary for each phase of the project, and manage the inventory to ensure resources are available when needed.
 * Review the construction progress daily, ensuring the project is advancing as planned and making adjustments as needed to maintain momentum.
 * Negotiate contract terms, draft agreements, and ensure that all permits and licenses are obtained and maintained.
 * Ensure the quality of work by supervising the use of appropriate construction techniques and materials, maintaining the highest standards of quality and safety throughout the project lifecycle.
 * Maintain strong client-focused relationships, providing regular updates and exceeding client expectations at all stages of the project.




ACADEMIC BACKGROUND

Bachelor's and Master's degree in architecture, engineering or related field.




LANGUAGES

Proficiency in English.




EXPERIENCE

 * Over 20 years of experience managing projects in the Luxury Residential, Iconic Buildings, Sports, Hospitality, Infrastructure, Entertainment, and Landscaping sectors.
 * Proven track record in managing complex projects, from planning to completion, with a focus on delivering high-quality projects.
 * Ability to manage multidisciplinary teams, working closely with subcontractors, designers, and consultants.
 * Extensive knowledge knowledge of construction management processes means and methods.
 * Negotiation skills for managing contracts, agreements, and obtaining necessary permits.
 * Client-centric approach, with the ability to build long-term relationships and ensure client satisfaction.

title: Product Owner junior
description:
¿Quiénes somos?

ZITYCARD es una empresa pionera en desarrollos y soluciones de identidad digital, blockchain, acceso y pago en entornos cerrados mediante diversos soportes digitales (contactless, apps, biometría). ¡Queremos seguir sumando talento a nuestro equipo!

Trabajamos con una arquitectura cloud sobre AWS, equipos multidisciplinares y una cultura de aprendizaje continuo.

 

El rol

Buscamos un/una Junior Product Owner para unirse a nuestro equipo de producto. Es una posición ideal para alguien que quiera iniciar o consolidar su carrera en gestión de producto, aprendiendo de primera mano cómo se construye y evoluciona un producto CORE en un entorno SaaS y B2G.

Trabajarás de la mano con un Product Owner Senior, con acompañamiento y mentoring, participando activamente en la definición del producto y en la entrega de valor a clientes reales de la administración pública como Ayuntamientos y Diputaciones de toda la península.

 

¿Qué harás en tu día a día?
 * Colaborar en la definición y priorización del backlog de producto, alineándolo con los objetivos del negocio y las necesidades de los clientes.
 * Redactar user stories claras, con criterios de aceptación bien definidos, trabajando junto al equipo de desarrollo durante las sesiones de refinamiento.
 * Participar en el análisis de licitaciones públicas, ayudando a traducir requisitos funcionales en soluciones de producto viables.
 * Asistir y participar activamente en las ceremonias ágiles (dailies, refinamientos, retrospectivas…), asegurando que el equipo disponga de la información necesaria.
 * Colaborar con UX y equipos técnicos en sesiones de diseño para entender y proponer soluciones a requisitos funcionales, no funcionales y evolutivos.
 * Realizar pruebas de aceptación de nuevas funcionalidades antes de su lanzamiento.
 * Mantener actualizada la documentación de producto (funcional y de usuario).
 * Interactuar progresivamente con clientes de la administración pública (ayuntamientos y diputaciones), inicialmente con supervisión, para entender casos de uso y recoger feedback.
 * Aprender sobre arquitectura de producto y decisiones técnicas, trabajando codo a codo con el equipo de desarrollo.

 

¿Qué buscamos?
 * Formación en Ingeniería Informática, Telecomunicaciones o experiencia demostrable en el mundo del software.
 * 1–3 años de experiencia en roles relacionados con producto, desarrollo de software, consultoría tecnológica o similares.
 * Conocimientos de metodologías ágiles (Scrum, Kanban).
 * Interés genuino por producto y tecnología, con curiosidad por entender cómo funcionan las cosas y cómo aportar el máximo valor.
 * Capacidad analítica para descomponer problemas complejos en partes más manejables para los equipos y los diferentes stakeholders.
 * Capacidad para entender conversaciones técnicas (APIs REST, JSON, HTML/CSS), sin necesidad de programar.
 * Familiaridad con herramientas de gestión de producto y colaboración como Jira, Redmine, Figma u otras similares.
 * Experiencia en elaboración de documentación funcional o técnica.
 * Buenas habilidades de comunicación escrita y verbal.
 * Mentalidad de aprendizaje continuo, proactividad y ganas de crecer profesionalmente
 * Capacidad para gestionar varias tareas, priorizar y manejar adecuadamente expectativas.
 * Actitud colaborativa y facilidad para trabajar en equipos multidisciplinares y distribuidos.
 * Se valorará positivamente estar localizado en Zaragoza, aunque este no es un requisito imprescindible.

 

¿Qué ofrecemos?
 * Incorporarte a un producto CORE con impacto real en la ciudadanía y los comercios en un entorno B2G en una empresa estable y en continuo crecimiento
 * Acompañamiento y mentoring por parte de perfiles senior de producto y tecnología.

·       Contrato indefinido, full remoto, formación continua, buen ambiente de trabajo y diversas opciones de flexibilidad para conciliar vida personal y laboral.

 * Entorno de trabajo colaborativo, joven y dinámico, con equipos distribuidos por toda la península ibérica.
 * Oportunidades reales de crecimiento profesional dentro del área de producto.
 * Trabajo con tecnología moderna en un entorno SaaS sobre AWS.
 * Condiciones competitivas acordes a la experiencia y responsabilidad del rol.

title: Key Account Manager
description:
The Company

Our customer is a European leader in digital transformation and customer experience, helping major international brands drive growth through technology. From strategy to implementation, they design and deliver high-performance architectures and engaging digital experiences by combining advanced technologies, proven methodologies, and deep technical expertise.




With 2,200 people across 12 countries, they are looking for passionate individuals who share their values: Creative Spirit, Commitment, and Forward Thinking.




Description of the position

As a Key Account Manager, you'll be the driving force behind identifying and developing new key local clients (new names) while creating extraordinary experiences for them.




In this sense, you must ensure their needs are met by combining technology, data, insights, local specificities, and creativity to build digital assets that drive engagement, enhance efficiency, and accelerate sustainable growth.




We are a CX specialist with end-to-end capabilities serving large and mid-size enterprises, including deep expertise within our local teams across Experience Platforms, Digital Continuity (QA & DevOps), and Data & AI.




What is this all about?

• Develop and maintain strong, long-lasting relationships with key clients.

• Deliver presentations, product demos, and conduct client visits to showcase the value of our solutions.

• Collaborate closely with internal teams to ensure seamless delivery and client satisfaction.

• Located in Barcelona, but covering all of Spain and Portugal from a business standpoint.




Who are you?

• Economics, Business, Software Engineer, Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, or a related field. An MBA is highly valued.

• Passionate about Pre-Sales, Sales, and Hunting / Prospection.

• Experienced in selling Digital solutions (Project fixed time/fixed price, T&M...), particularly in eCommerce Brands.

• Expert in managing consultative sales processes within the tech space.

• Possesses strong negotiation skills with the ability to close deals.

• Enthusiastic about working within a close local team in Spain, while also joining a dynamic and stimulating global matrix organization.




Skills & Behaviours




Technical skills / Know-how

• Knowledge of customers' business, operational, and technological challenges.

• Writing content for sales proposals.

• Proven experience in business development.

• Negotiation techniques and contract management.

• Master the sales cycle and associated planning.

• Ability to listen and summarize customer needs.




Behavioral skills / Required qualities

• Results orientation & Sense of execution.

• Respect, Integrity & Commitment.

• Ability to work in matrix or cross-functional organizations.

• Excellent communication and presentation skills.

• Competence to understand and communicate complex technological solutions.

• Strong results orientation and ability to work autonomously.




Languages

• English & Spanish: a fluent level is mandatory.

• French & Catalan are highly valued.




Salary

• Permanent contract with a very competitive salary package and added benefits.

• Onsite/Hybrid/Remote policy work.




Job location

Barcelona. We need you to be a citizen of the European Union. If you are not, you must have a work permit. Availability to travel in monthly basis (locally and abroad).




If you consider this opportunity aligns with your profile and you are interested, apply here or send us an email to account_manager@montarelo.com, with “Key Account Manager” in the subject line, and your CV in English.

title: Director de Operaciones
description:
Buscamos un Director de Operaciones para empresa de Medical Device. Serás responsable de gestionar y optimizar las operaciones de logística y cadena de suministro a nivel internacional, con un enfoque particular en las actividades de importación, exportación y cumplimiento de regulaciones aduaneras con especial focus en mercado de Asia (China) donde tienen los centros de producción hasta destino en UE.

Detalles del cliente

Empresa de Medical Device con un gran crecimiento consolidado en los últimos años y a las puertas de dar el salto expansivo a EEUU y China con presencial local en ambos países.

Descripción de la oferta

Responsabilidades Principales:

 1. Gestión de la Cadena de Suministro:
     * Planificar, coordinar y supervisar todas las actividades de logística y aprovisionamiento internacional.
     * Desarrollar y mantener relaciones con proveedores, transportistas y socios logísticos a nivel global y desde China hacia el resto del mundo.
     * Optimizar los procesos de transporte y almacenamiento para reducir costos y mejorar la eficiencia.
     * Liderar el departamento de Compras

 2. Importación y Exportación:
     * Gestionar todas las operaciones de importación y exportación, asegurando el cumplimiento de las regulaciones internacionales y locales tanto a nivel import como export con base en China.
     * Preparar y revisar la documentación necesaria para el despacho de aduanas, incluyendo facturas comerciales, listas de empaque, y certificados de origen.
     * Coordinar con agentes aduaneros para facilitar el despacho eficiente y resolver cualquier problema relacionado con el comercio internacional.
     * Supervisión de la operativa de Almacenamiento junto a control de calidad.

 3. Regulaciones Aduaneras:
     * Mantenerse actualizado sobre las leyes y regulaciones aduaneras de los países relevantes.
     * Garantizar que todos los envíos cumplan con los requisitos aduaneros, incluyendo el pago de aranceles y la presentación de documentos precisos y completos.
     * Gestionar auditorías aduaneras y resolver cualquier disputa relacionada con aduanas.

 4. Planificación y Estrategia:
     * Analizar datos y tendencias de la cadena de suministro para identificar oportunidades de mejora.
     * Desarrollar estrategias de aprovisionamiento y logística que apoyen los objetivos de negocio y expansión internacional.
     * Implementar soluciones tecnológicas para mejorar la visibilidad y trazabilidad en la cadena de suministro.

 5. Coordinación Interdepartamental:
     * Colaborar con los departamentos de ventas, producción y finanzas para alinear las operaciones logísticas con las necesidades del negocio.
     * Facilitar la comunicación entre las oficinas internacionales y locales para asegurar la coherencia en las operaciones de la cadena de suministro.

Perfil del candidato

 * Formación Superior (Ingeniería, Industriales, ADE, Finanzas o similar)
 * Muy valorable Máster en Cadena de Suministro / Supply Chain
 * Inglés C1 (indispensable)
 * Epxeriencia en: Compras / Seguimiento de importaciones / Trazabilidad, costes y tiempos / Almacenja y Calidad
 * Perfil analítico, con mentalidad operativa y de costes

Oferta de empleo

Excelente oportunidad para dar el salto a nivel internacional como Director de Operaciones en una Biotech en pleno crecimiento y expansión


Desired Skills and Experience

Responsabilidades Principales:

Gestión de la Cadena de Suministro:
Planificar, coordinar y supervisar todas las actividades de logística y aprovisionamiento internacional.
Desarrollar y mantener relaciones con proveedores, transportistas y socios logísticos a nivel global y desde China hacia el resto del mundo.
Optimizar los procesos de transporte y almacenamiento para reducir costos y mejorar la eficiencia.
Liderar el departamento de Compras
Importación y Exportación:
Gestionar todas las operaciones de importación y exportación, asegurando el cumplimiento de las regulaciones internacionales y locales tanto a nivel import como export con base en China.

Supervisión de la operativa de Almacenamiento junto a control de calidad.

Garantizar que todos los envíos cumplan con los requisitos aduaneros, incluyendo el pago de aranceles y la presentación de documentos precisos y completos.

Analizar datos y tendencias de la cadena de suministro para identificar oportunidades de mejora.

title: Responsable Investment Banking / Oficina Panamá
description:
Resumen del Rol.




Buscamos un VP Investment Banking para una de las instituciones más prestigiosas y robustas del sector de Servicios de Inversión y Wealth Management a nivel internacional. Con sede en el hub financiero estratégico de Panamá, operan como un puente crítico entre los mercados de capitales globales y las oportunidades estratégicas en la región.

Cuentan con más de 100 años de trayectoria, manteniendo una solvencia que ha atravesado ciclos económicos globales con éxito rotundo y han sido reconocidos internacionalmente como Mejor Banco de Inversión y Mejor Banco en Panamá.




El VP de Investment Banking será responsable de liderar la originación, estructuración y ejecución de transacciones financieras complejas, incluyendo M&A, levantamiento de capital, estructuración de deuda y asesoría estratégica. Este rol exige una combinación de liderazgo, visión analítica, habilidades comerciales y profundo entendimiento del mercado financiero regional e internacional. Esta posición representa una oportunidad excepcional de expatriación estratégica hacia la Ciudad de Panamá, el hub financiero más dinámico de la región.




Responsabilidades Clave.

 * Estrategia de Inversión: liderar mandatos de Investment Banking, incluyendo M&A (buy-side y sell-side), emisiones de deuda y capital, liderar la gestión de activos y el diseño de carteras personalizadas, asegurando la optimización de rendimientos bajo estrictos parámetros de control de riesgo, reestructuraciones financieras y valoraciones financieras (DCF, comparables, precedentes).
 * Mercados de Capitales: Dirigir la estructuración de emisiones de deuda, capital y productos derivados, conectando las necesidades de financiamiento corporativo con oportunidades de inversión global.
 * Desarrollo de Negocio Institucional: Expandir la huella de la organización en Panamá mediante la captación y fidelización de cuentas clave, actuando como asesor senior para juntas directivas y oficinas de familia (Family Offices). Coordinar procesos de due diligence, modelización financiera y documentación de transacciones
 * Análisis y Prospectiva: Supervisar la inteligencia de mercados internacionales para identificar tendencias en renta fija, renta variable y activos alternativos.
 * Gestión de Equipos de Alto Rendimiento: Coordinar a los equipos de analistas y asociados, promoviendo una cultura de rigor técnico y ética profesional impecable.




Perfil requerido.

1. Formación académica:

 * Licenciatura en Finanzas, Economía, Administración de Empresas, Ingeniería con especialización financiera
 * MBA, Máster en Finanzas o CFA / CAIA muy valorado.




2. Experiencia y Conocimientos:

 * Mínimo 10 años en finanzas, banca de inversión o roles relacionados, corporate Finance, Boutique financiera o banca internacional. Experiencia demostrable liderando transacciones complejas
 * Sólido conocimiento de mercados de capitales, estructuración financiera y valuation.
 * Experiencia trabajando con clientes corporativos e institucionales
 * Gestión de Activos. Investment Banking.
 * Dominio experto de estrategias de inversión globales y operativa en mercados internacionales (EE.UU., Europa y Latam).
 * Profundo conocimiento técnico en análisis cuantitativo, valoración de empresas y normativa de mercados de valores.
 * Experiencia previa en instituciones financieras de alcance multinacional.




3. Idiomas

 * Español nativo
 * Inglés avanzado
 * Otro idoma es un plus




4. Disponibilidad:

Disposición para residencia permanente en Panamá. Nuestro cliente ofrece un sólido paquete de apoyo para la transición de residencia en Panamá, permitiendo al profesional desarrollar su carrera en un entorno multicultural y de alta competitividad global. Panamá no es solo un destino; es la plataforma de lanzamiento para los banqueros de inversión más influyentes de la década. Al unirse a nuestro cliente, el candidato seleccionado se sumergirá en un mercado con beneficios fiscales competitivos, una calidad de vida de alto estándar y, sobre todo, la oportunidad de liderar desde la Mejor Banca de Inversión del país.

title: Engineering Manager
description:
Position Summary:

Crystal Intelligence is seeking an experienced Engineering Manager to lead our Platform Squads, which form the backbone of our engineering efforts. These squads oversee the Core Platform, Core Data (including blockchain integrations), Developer Experience (DevEx), and DevOps. This is an 80% management role and 20% hands-on, focusing on driving technical excellence, fostering a high-performing team culture, and ensuring efficient processes to deliver scalable and reliable platform solutions that power our products and services.

At Crystal Expert, Engineering Managers are accountable for the process, ensuring that workflows run smoothly, priorities are aligned with business goals, and teams adhere to best practices in agile development. The successful candidate will work directly with a team of approximately 20 engineers while being part of a broader engineering organization of 50+ engineers, managing cross-squad dependencies and scaling engineering processes to meet organisational needs.

This is a fully remote role, open to candidates across Europe.

More details: crystalintelligence.com

Duties and responsibilities:

Leadership & Team Development:


 * Manage and mentor a team of engineers and engineering leads across multiple squads, fostering growth and professional development.
 * Build a collaborative and inclusive team culture that promotes innovation, quality, and accountability.
 * Set clear goals and expectations, provide regular feedback, and ensure team alignment with company objectives.
   
   

Process Management & Accountability:


 * Own and refine team processes, ensuring adherence to agile methodologies (e.g., Scrum) and facilitating key ceremonies (standups, sprint planning, retrospectives).
 * Oversee task management in JIRA, ensuring proper prioritization, assignment, and execution of work.
 * Collaborate with stakeholders to define and manage roadmaps, timelines, and deliverables.
 * Drive a culture of accountability for respecting and optimizing team processes.
   
   

Technical Strategy & Execution:


 * Drive the technical vision and roadmap for the Core Platform, Data Pipelines, DevEx, and DevOps squads.
 * Ensure the delivery of robust, high-performance data pipelines, including blockchain integrations, with a focus on scalability and reliability.
 * Advocate for best practices in software development, DevOps, and developer productivity across all squads.
 * Occasionally contribute hands-on to solve critical challenges or guide technical direction.
   
   

Cross-Functional Collaboration:


 * Partner with product managers, engineering managers, and other stakeholders to align platform goals with business priorities.
 * Work closely with DevOps and Developer Experience teams to enhance developer productivity and streamline workflows.
 * Collaborate with Core Data Pipeline squads to enable seamless blockchain integrations and data flow.
   
   

Process & Operational Excellence:


 * Define and implement processes that ensure high-quality, on-time delivery of platform initiatives.
 * Monitor and improve key performance metrics, such as lead time to changes, system uptime, pipeline latency, team velocity, and sprint success rate.
 * Ensure platform components comply with security, compliance, and scalability requirements.
   
   

Required:

Experience:


 * 10+ years of experience in software engineering, with at least 5 years working as a backend engineer and 3+ years in a senior leadership role managing multiple teams or squads.
 * Proven experience managing a team of approximately 15 engineers while being part of a larger 50+ engineer organization with cross-squad dependencies.
 * Strong technical background in system architecture, data engineering, and infrastructure management.
   
   

Process & Management Skills:


 * Proven ability to manage tasks using JIRA, ensuring prioritization, tracking, and alignment with roadmaps.
 * Experience in defining and managing roadmaps and aligning them with company objectives.
 * Deep understanding and application of agile methodologies, including Scrum, and a strong track record of facilitating ceremonies and ensuring process adherence.
 * Performance Monitoring: Ability to monitor and improve team performance using key metrics/KPIs such as velocity, throughput, and cycle time, ensuring continuous improvement.
   
   

Technical Skills:


 * Familiarity with scalable architecture patterns, microservices, and distributed systems.
 * Hands-on experience with data pipeline technologies, APIs, and DevOps tools (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform).
 * Solid understanding of CI/CD processes, cloud-native tools, and developer productivity enhancements.
   
   

Leadership Skills:


 * Proven ability to build, manage, and mentor high-performing engineering teams.
 * Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to engage effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
 * Experience driving cultural change and promoting a growth mindset within engineering organisations.
   
   

Problem-Solving:


 * Exceptional analytical skills with a track record of solving complex engineering challenges.
 * Strong decision-making abilities with a focus on balancing business and technical priorities.
   
   

Would be a plus:


 * Experience with blockchain technologies and integrations.
 * Familiarity with developer tooling, developer experience optimization, or DevOps practices.
 * Passion for software engineering, demonstrated through an active GitHub profile, technical blog, conference speaking engagements, or contributions to open-source projects.
 * Experience scaling engineering teams and processes in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

title: Head of Engineering
description:
This is a senior engineering leadership role responsible for connecting business strategy with engineering execution and playing a critical part in driving growth, operational excellence, and customer satisfaction. Our client is looking for someone who can strengthen delivery, modernise ways of working, introduce more structure and performance discipline, and help shape the future team model. You will operate at a senior leadership level, collaborating closely with product, commercial, finance, and executive stakeholders.



Your responsibilities and impact working as Head of Engineering:

 * Lead and develop multiple engineering teams, building a high-performing, accountable organisation
 * Set clear direction and improve delivery discipline, execution standards, and team maturity
 * Translate business goals into engineering priorities and measurable outcomes
 * Conduct gap analysis across capability, structure, and performance, and implement improvements
 * Modernise ways of working, including stronger product-engineering collaboration
 * Support and evolve team structures as AI and cloud transformation reshape delivery models
 * Oversee SaaS and on-premise solutions with responsibility for scalability, security, reliability, and cost awareness
 * Guide cloud strategy
 * Maintain technical credibility with engineers without needing to code
 * Manage senior stakeholders effectively
 * Balance strategic thinking with operational execution
 * Drive change constructively, improving performance without destabilising teams




Skills, Qualifications, and Interests You Need to Succeed in This Role:

 * Experience leading multiple engineering teams
 * Background at Senior Engineering Manager or early Head-of level
 * Experience in SaaS and cloud-based environments
 * Strong understanding of Azure and/or AWS and distributed systems
 * Comfortable overseeing infrastructure, architecture, and cost management discussions
 * Understanding of modern software development practices
 * Experience working with product managers or product owners
 * Commercially aware and able to link engineering to business outcomes
 * Experience in regulated environments is a plus, but not essential
 * Confident and pragmatic leadership style
 * Comfortable challenging ideas constructively
 * Curious, improvement-focused, and resilient in evolving environments




What’s in it for you?

 * Opportunity for career progression
 * High visibility with senior leadership
 * Hybrid flexibilty
 * Performance based bonus

description:

title: Wellness Director (European work permit required)
description:
Additional Information

Job Number 26017114

Job Category Spa

Location The St. Regis Mardavall Mallorca Resort, Carretera Palma-Andratx 19, Palma de Mallorca, Baleares, Spain, 7181VIEW ON MAP

Schedule Full Time

Job Summary

Responsible for managing and supervising all areas of the spa, pool and kids club, including its programs, services, hours of operation, facilities and staff. Coordinates the delivery of spa services, including salon, skin care, fitness and wellness, massage, program coordinating, reservations, reception desk and locker room areas. As a department head, directs and works with the management team and hourly employees to successfully execute all operations. Strives to continually improve guest and employee satisfaction and maximize the financial performance of the department.

CANDIDATE PROFILE

Education And Experience


 * 2-year degree from an accredited university in Business Administration, Hotel and Restaurant Management, or related major; 4 years experience in the spa, guest services, front desk, sales and marketing, or related professional area.
   
   

OR


 * 4-year bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Hotel and Restaurant Management, or related major; 2 years experience in the spa, guest services, front desk, sales and marketing, or related professional area.
   
   

CORE WORK ACTIVITIES

Managing Spa Operations and Budgets


 * Selects vendors for spa retail operations and managing contract agreements.
 * Oversees retail product research, product selection and purchasing, product display.
 * Manages supply inventories and purchasing control, including uniforms.
 * Monitors the spa's actual and projected sales to ensure revenue goals are met or exceeded.
 * Maintains cleanliness of spa and related areas and equipment.
   
   

Managing Spa Sales and Marketing Strategy


 * Creates and coordinates special services for groups including group gifting programs, group amenities, group turndown gifts, letters and invitations, creating special spa services for specific groups and spa contract addendum negotiation.
 * Develops and Manages spa promotions including gifting programs, gift with purchase, co-op marketing efforts and holiday events.
 * Ensures spa services are included in all property-related marketing and advertising.
 * Identifies and recommending new products and product enhancements to remain competitive in the market.
   
   

Managing Spa Revenue Management Strategy


 * Monitors and Manages the payroll function.
 * Manages areas of operation to budget by reviewing operating statements, budget worksheets and payroll progress reports.
 * Manages Spa controllable expenses such guest amenities, linen expense, professional salon products, plants, decorations and paper supplies to achieve or exceed budgeted goals.
   
   

Ensuring and Delivering Exceptional Customer Service


 * Displays leadership in guest hospitality, exemplifying excellent customer service, and creating a positive atmosphere for guest relations.
 * Empowers employees to provide excellent customer service.
 * Strives to improve service performance.
   
   

Conducting Human Resources Activities


 * Reviews comment cards, guest satisfaction results and other data to identify areas of improvement.
 * Reviews findings with employees to develop appropriate corrective action, sharing plans with property leadership and ensuring corrective action is taken to continuously improve results.
 * Interviews and hires management and hourly employees with the appropriate skills to meet the business needs of the operation.
 * Develops, implements and maintains a Spa orientation program for employees to receive the appropriate new hire training to successfully perform their job.
 * Administers the performance appraisal process for direct report managers.
 * Develops business goals and creates appropriate development plans.
 * Establishes and maintains open, collaborative relationships with employees and ensuring employees do the same within the team.
 * Solicits employee feedback, utilizes an “open door” policy and reviewing employee satisfaction results to identify and address employee problems or concerns.
 * Ensures that regular on-going communication is happening in all areas of responsibility to create awareness of business objectives and communicates expectations, recognizing performance, and producing desired business results.
 * Celebrates successes and publicly recognizes the contributions of team members.
   
   

At Marriott International, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, welcoming all and providing access to opportunity. Combining timeless glamour with a vanguard spirit, St. Regis Hotels & Resorts is committed to delivering exquisite experiences at more than 50 luxury hotels and resorts in the best addresses around the world. Beginning with the debut of The St. Regis hotel in New York by John Jacob Astor IV at the dawn of the twentieth century, the brand has remained committed to an uncompromising level of bespoke and anticipatory service for all of its guests, delivered flawlessly by a team of gracious hosts that combine classic sophistication and modern sensibility, as well as our signature Butler Service. We invite you to explore careers at St. Regis. In joining St. Regis, you join a portfolio of brands with Marriott International. Be where you can do your best work, begin your purpose, belong to an amazing global team, and become the best version of you.

title: Clinical Development Medical Director (CDMD) Neuroscience
description:
Summary

LOCATION: London or Homeworker, Dublin, Barcelona

ROLE TYPE: Hybrid Working,

The Clinical Development Medical Director (CDMD) is the Global clinical leader of defined and assigned program level activities and deliverables (e.g. submission activities, briefing books etc.), or clinical trial(s), under the leadership of the GPCH or Sr CDMD.

About The Role

Major accountabilities:


 * Provides clinical leadership, scientific and medical strategic input for all clinical deliverables in the assigned or defined program activities as applicable. Clinical deliverables may include (sections of) individual protocols consistent with the Integrated Development Plans (IDP) and CDP, clinical data review, program specific standards, clinical components of regulatory documents/registration dossiers, and publications
 * Leads development of clinical sections of trial and program level regulatory documents (e.g., Investigator’s Brochures, briefing books, safety updates, submission dossiers, and responses to Health Authorities)
 * Drives execution of the section of the clinical program in partnership with global line functions, assigned Clinical Trial Heads (CTHs), and regional/country medical associates if applicable
 * Provides medical oversight and leadership of trials and may act as medical monitor. Provides input into final analyses and interpretation including the development of the Clinical Study Report(s) (CSRs), publications and internal/external presentations"
 * Supports GPCH or Sr CDMD in ensuring overall benefit/risk assessment and monitor safety of the molecule for the assigned section on an ongoing basis and may be a core member of the Safety Management Team (SMT), and supports overall program safety reporting (e.g., Periodic Safety Update Reports (PSURs), Drug Safety Update Reports (DSURs), and other safety related documents) in collaboration with Patient Safety
 * May be assigned to provide medical input into IDP/CDP and CTP reviews and contributing/driving development of disease clinical standards for new disease areas.
 * As a medical expert, supports the GPCH or CDH/TAH in interactions with external stakeholders (e.g., regulatory authorities, key opinion leaders, data monitoring boards, advisory boards, patient advocacy groups), internal stakeholders (e.g., CTT, Research, Translational Medicine, Global Medical Affairs, Marketing, HE&OR), and internal decision boards. As the medical/clinical lead interacts with and represents Novartis to global key opinion leaders and experts and may lead or co-chair steering committees for defined clinical trials or section of a clinical development program
 * May work with Biomedical Research/Translational Medical Sciences to drive transition of pre-PoC (Proof of Concept) projects to DDP (Development Decision Point) and with BD&L (Business Development & Licensing) including target identification and due diligences together with other medical matters, as assigned by the CDH
 * Ensures career development of Program reports and other clinical colleagues through active participation in the performance management and talent planning processes. Provides on-boarding, training, & mentoring support
 * Contributes to medical/scientific training of relevant Novartis stakeholders on the disease area and compound/molecule. May serve as speaker for franchise medical/scientific training
 * May serve on or lead global initiatives (e.g., process improvement, training, SOP development, other Clinical Development line function initiatives)
 * May be assigned to lead clinical trial(s) as Clinical Scientific Lead and provide leadership and guidance for all clinical aspects of a clinical trial.
   
   

Education


 * MD or equivalent medical degree required. Advanced knowledge and clinical training in a medical/scientific area (e.g., internal medicine or sub-specialty) required, with Medical Board certification preferred; Clinical practice experience ≥ 4 years (including residency) preferred
   
   

Experience


 * ≥ 5 years of involvement in clinical research or global drug development in an academic or industry environment spanning clinical activities in Phases I through IV. ≥ 3 years of contribution to and accomplishment in all aspects of conducting clinical trials (e.g., planning, executing, reporting and publishing) in a global/matrix environment in pharmaceutical industry.
 * Experience in late phase clinical development preferred
 * Solid and advanced scientific acumen and ability to analyze and interpret scientific literature and data
 * Advanced knowledge of assigned therapeutic area
 * Demonstrated ability to establish strong scientific partnership with key stakeholders
 * Thorough knowledge of ICH, GCP, clinical trial design and methodology, statistical analysis methodology, and regulatory/ clinical development process
 * 1 year or more of people management experience required, this may include management in a matrix environment. Global people management experience desirable
 * Experience with operating and delivering in a complex global matrix environment. and excellent team player
 * Excellent communication skills, written and oral
 * Excellent interpersonal skills
 * Excellent negotiation and conflict resolution skills
   
   

Commitment To Diversity And Inclusion

Novartis is committed to building an outstanding, inclusive work environment and diverse teams representative of the patients and communities we serve.

Why Novartis: Helping people with disease and their families takes more than innovative science. It takes a community of smart, passionate people like you. Collaborating, supporting and inspiring each other. Combining to achieve breakthroughs that change patients’ lives. Ready to create a brighter future together? https://www.novartis.com/about/strategy/people-and-culture

Benefits and Rewards: Learn about all the ways we’ll help you thrive personally and professionally.

Read our handbook (PDF 30 MB)

title: CTO (EMPRESA SERVICIOS)
description:
Buscamos un/a CTO qué será responsable de coordinar y liderar el área de desarrollo de software, asegurando que las soluciones tecnológicas de la empresa estén alineadas con los objetivos estratégicos y comerciales. Buscamos también alguien que participe activamente en la implementación técnica. Este enfoque implica estar dispuesto/a a involucrarse en el código, resolver problemas técnicos directamente, y tomar decisiones técnicas basadas en su propia experiencia práctica, en lugar de delegar completamente las tareas a su equipo.







¿Qué harás en tu día a día?:




🚀Gestión del desarrollo de software: Liderar el diseño, la implementación y el mantenimiento de las soluciones de software, asegurando su escalabilidad y eficiencia.

🚀Colaboración con otros equipos: Trabajar en coordinación con el Administrador de Sistemas para garantizar que las plataformas tecnológicas sean integrales, seguras y optimizadas.

🚀Innovación tecnológica: Identificar nuevas tendencias y tecnologías para implementar soluciones que mantengan a la empresa competitiva y para maximizar el rendimiento. Actualmente el desarrollo de aplicaciones web utilizando Laravel y gestión de bases de datos con MySQL, diseños y funcionalidades con Bootstrap y Livewire.

🚀Desarrollo de aplicaciones móviles en iOS y Android que apoyen las necesidades operativas y de cliente.

🚀Optimización de procesos de desarrollo: Mejorar continuamente los procesos de desarrollo, incorporando metodologías ágiles y prácticas de DevOps.

🚀Integrar inteligencia artificial en nuestros sistemas para optimizar procesos y decisiones en la operativa interna.

🚀Colaboración interdepartamental: Cooperar con los equipos de marketing, operaciones y ventas para utilizar la tecnología como palanca de mejora en los procesos internos y externos.

title: Director of Quality Assurance
description:
In a nutshell 📌

Position: Director of Quality Assurance

Location: Paterna (Valencia) – On-site

Experience: 5+ years in quality leadership within pharmaceutical FDF manufacturing




Want to know more? 👇

BioNanoPharma is committed to excellence in the development, manufacturing, and distribution of innovative pharmaceutical products. Our mission is to ensure the highest standards of quality and safety at every stage, with a strong focus on continuous improvement and sustainability. Would you like to join a team that is leading the transformation of the healthcare sector?




Ready to be a #Challenger? 💪

What are we looking for? 🔎

We are seeking a Director of Quality Assurance to lead and oversee the implementation of quality programs across all BioNaNopharma operations, ensuring regulatory compliance and continuous improvement. You will be the key reference for quality, acting as Deputy QP and substituting for the Technical Director of Quality when required.




The challenge! 🚀

 * Develop and maintain the Quality Management System (QMS) in line with international standards.
 * Manage quality documentation: SOPs, work instructions, and records.
 * Plan and execute internal and external audits, ensuring readiness for regulatory and client inspections.
 * Ensure compliance with GMP, GDP, FDA, and industry standards.
 * Oversee the acquisition and maintenance of certifications and licenses.
 * Identify and mitigate compliance risks in the supply chain and storage.
 * Conduct regular warehouse inspections, monitoring temperature and storage conditions.
 * Implement quality controls in inventory and logistics processes.
 * Develop protocols for temperature-sensitive products.
 * Manage quality in freight forwarding operations and external providers.
 * Investigate incidents and lead corrective and preventive actions (CAPAs).
 * Drive continuous improvement using Lean/Six Sigma methodologies and KPIs.
 * Foster a culture of quality and design training programs for the team.
 * Collaborate with operations, logistics, and customer service to align quality objectives.
 * Manage customer complaints and lead root cause analysis.
 * Report to senior management on quality performance and areas for improvement.




What do you need? 🤓

 * University degree in Pharmacy, Medicine, Veterinary Medicine, Chemistry, Pharmaceutical Chemistry and Technology, or Biology.
 * At least 5 years of experience in a quality leadership role within a pharmaceutical FDF manufacturing site.
 * Experience with GMP, GDP, and FDA regulations (FDA experience is a plus).
 * Quality-related certifications (ISO 9001 Lead Auditor, Six Sigma Black Belt, GDP Certification) are highly valued.
 * Advanced knowledge of quality systems management and audits.
 * Fluent in Spanish and English.
 * Strong leadership, communication, analytical, and stakeholder management skills.




Our benefits! 👏

📜 Permanent contract

🎯 Professional development and continuous training

🏥 Private health insurance

🍽️ Subsidized canteen

🚗 Free parking

🎁 Additional social benefits




What is the selection process like? 🕵️

 * Initial contact by phone or email.
 * 1-2 on-site interviews with HR and the Technical Management team.
 * Ongoing communication about your application status.




🔷Think this role isn’t for you?🔷

Follow us on LinkedIn to stay updated on future opportunities and news at BioNanoPharma. We’d love to connect!

#BioNaNopharma #Challenger #QualityAssurance #InsudTalent




COMMITMENT TO EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES

At BioNanoPharma, we promote equal opportunities and non-discrimination based on gender, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or any other condition. We believe diversity drives innovation and growth.

title: Principal in Procurement / Supply Chain Management
description:
At Inverto, we specialize in strategic procurement and supply chain management. We work globally with market-leading companies, developing transformational strategies and implementing them to make their businesses stronger. And you can too – with our continued success, this is your chance to grow as we grow. Our teams want to succeed – and you’ll be in great company, working with people and in a business that supports you to thrive. How You'll Impact.

Far-reaching responsibility.

You will ensure the professional and personal development of your team. And as a member of the Inverto management team, you will help to shape management strategies and support the international growth of our company.

Strategic customer management.

You will work closely with our customers and be their sparring partner whilst in charge of their projects.

University degree with above-average grades and 7-12 years of relevant professional experience.

title: Partner Sales Director - IHV Alliances
description:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation, and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office-based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.

The company is founder-led, profitable, and growing.

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced IHV Alliance Sales Director to join our strategic partnerships team who will be responsible for leading IHV Alliances in the EMEA or APAC regions. Reporting to the Head of IHV Alliances, the IHV Alliance Sales Director will work closely with IHV and Canonical teams in those geographies to develop and maintain strong relationships with senior executives at strategic partner organizations, fostering collaboration and alignment on joint business objectives. The IHV Alliance Sales Director will manage and grow a team of Business Development Managers and help them identify and pursue new business opportunities with IHVs, driving revenue growth and market expansion, develop and execute go-to-market strategies for joint offerings, oversee the day-to-day management of alliances including contract negotiations and performance tracking, and issue resolution.

The Director is responsible for building trusted relationships with IHV partners, increasing Canonical market share and attach rate, evangelising the partnership and leading all business interactions from engineer to CxO. They will often run customer workshops focused on particular initiatives at that customer, attend sales events, give public presentations and lead executive engagements.

Location: This role will be based remotely in EMEA or APAC regions.

As an IHV Alliance Sales Director you will:


 * Build strategic relationships with IHV partners at all levels
 * Grow their awareness of open source capabilities on Canonical Ubuntu
 * Demonstrate a deep understanding of the Linux and cloud software ecosystem
 * Negotiate contracts and commercial business terms
 * Demonstrate a deep understanding of large and complex organizations
 * Lead partnerships in APAC with companies like Lenovo, Huawei, Quanta, Fujitsu, Inspur, H3C, NEC, Asus etc.
 * Lead partnerships in EMEA with companies like Ericsson, Nokia, Bull, Siemens
 * Lead partnerships in the Americas with companies like Dell, HPE, IBM, Supermicro
 * Work closely with marketing, sales engineering and product management
 * Deliver on targets, objectives and provide a voice of the partner
 * Lead executive interlocks between IHV and Canonical leadership
 * Travel regularly - sometimes internationally - to drive partnerships in person
   
   

Valued skills and experience


 * Experience in alliance or indirect sales management roles
 * Autonomous, disciplined, hands-on, get-it-done mentality
 * Ability to capture customer requirements, evaluate gaps, identify and create opportunities
 * Passionate about Ubuntu products and mission
 * Comfortable in fast-paced and high pressure environments with measurable goals
 * Experience with Linux, virtualization, containers, and other cloud technologies.
 * Excellent communication and presentation skills
   
   

What we offer colleagues

We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognize outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus or commission. We provide all team members with additional benefits which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.


 * Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
 * Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
 * Annual compensation review
 * Recognition rewards
 * Annual holiday leave
 * Maternity and paternity leave
 * Team Member Assistance Program & Wellness Platform
 * Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
 * Priority Pass and travel upgrades for long-haul company events
   
   

About Canonical

Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open-source projects and the platform for AI, IoT, and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence; in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since our inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.

Canonical is an equal opportunity employer

We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: Director of Engineering - Europe
description:
Thermal Works LLC is seeking a dynamic Director of Engineering to join our team as the primary engineering lead in Europe. In this strategic role, you will serve as an extension of our U.S.-based engineering organization, reporting directly to our Operations Director, Europe and driving design development, technical leadership, and customer-facing engineering support for data center applications.

We are looking for a leader who is highly organized, proactive, technically strong, and capable of working independently while coordinating seamlessly with cross-functional teams across continents. You will play a pivotal role in shaping data center cooling solutions for the EU market, ensuring our customers receive best-in-class support, engineering guidance, and technical delivery.

Thermal Works values efficiency and strives to maintain smooth operations. Our primary focus is on providing an integrated cooling system for data centers and commercial buildings that dramatically reduces the energy demand, water consumption and cost of cooling. It transforms the economics and eco-metrics of commercial cooling with zero water solutions that outperform air- and water-cooled systems in any climate.

Thermal Works solutions are sustainable and cutting-edge. For that reason, we are highly selective about the people we bring on board because our work depends on it. Change and progress happen quickly, and we must be able to trust one another to be honest, communicative, reliable, and self-motivated. We seek ultra-creatives and superstar performers with a sense of humility and a hunger to make a positive impact in the world. We offer flexibility and endless growth opportunities to those who can harness their skills and talents and identify how and where to use them to add value. Thermal Works is one of several “inspired infrastructure” companies that work collaboratively under our parent company, Endeavour Energy, LLC. At the heart of this ecosystem is a diverse group of bright, passionate, dedicated people, working together to make a real difference for people and the planet. Thermal Works is looking for someone to join our team to help us move the needle towards sustainable change. Are you ready to join the journey?

Key Responsibilities

Include But Are Not Limited To

Engineering Leadership & Coordination


 * Serve as the primary engineering lead for Thermal Works in Europe, functioning as an extension of the U.S. engineering and R&D team.
 * Report directly to the Director of Research & Development and collaborate closely on product design, testing, and performance validation.
 * Lead data center design development for EU clients, ensuring alignment with technical requirements, local regulations, and Thermal Works’ performance standards.
 * Provide application engineering support, including evaluating customer requirements, developing engineered solutions, and participating in technical presentations.
   
   

Customer & Field Technical Support


 * Act as a senior technical liaison for EU customers, providing engineering guidance, troubleshooting expertise, and system optimization recommendations.
 * Partner with the service technician network in Spain to strengthen technical capabilities, support recruitment efforts, and build local engineering talent to support operations.
 * Deliver technical coordination across stakeholders—including customers, contractors, and internal teams—to ensure seamless project execution.
   
   

Training, Validation & R&D Collaboration


 * Travel to the U.S. for hands-on technical training to gain deep familiarity with Thermal Works’ products, systems, and testing methodologies.
 * Support design validation efforts by applying real-world feedback and ensuring consistent performance across diverse European applications.
 * Contribute insights from EU market needs to influence future product development and enhancements.
   
   

Systems Design Oversight & Technical Excellence

Responsibilities

While not responsible for shipping, delivery, or commissioning (managed by the Program Operations Manager), you will work as a technical peer, assisting with:


 * Advanced HVAC and thermal management system review, including critical data center cooling applications.
 * Verification of system performance assumptions, load calculations, airflow management strategies, and integration requirements.
 * Collaborative problem-solving to address complex engineering challenges and ensure optimal design outcomes.
   
   

Skills & Requirements

Education / Experience

Required


 * Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, HVAC Engineering, or related field.
 * 7+ years of experience in HVAC system design, data center engineering, or mission-critical facilities.
 * Demonstrated experience leading engineering efforts and supporting customer-facing technical engagements.
 * Strong understanding of high-density cooling, thermal management, and critical system redundancy.
   
   

Preferred


 * Master’s degree in Mechanical or HVAC Engineering.
 * LEED, ASHRAE, or other industry certifications.
 * Experience with emerging cooling technologies (e.g., liquid cooling, modular cooling, advanced economization).
 * Prior leadership or team-building experience, especially across international regions.
   
   

Specific Skills / Abilities

HVAC & Data Center Cooling Expertise


 * Deep knowledge of HVAC and thermal systems including CRAC/CRAH units, air handling units, chillers, in-row cooling, air-side economization, and thermal containment strategies.
 * Proficient in evaluating heat loads, airflow modeling, and system performance for mission-critical environments.
   
   

Software Proficiency


 * Experience with HVAC design and simulation software (e.g., Trane Trace, Carrier HAP, EnergyPlus).
 * Familiarity with AutoCAD, Revit, or similar tools for coordination and design review.
   
   

Energy Efficiency & Sustainability


 * Strong understanding of energy-efficient cooling technologies, sustainable design strategies, and compliance with EU standards and directives.
 * Knowledge of ASHRAE guidelines, local building codes, and requirements for sustainable data center design.
   
   

Leadership & Communication


 * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage both technical and non-technical audiences.
 * Proven ability to collaborate across disciplines and cultures to deliver successful engineering outcomes.
 * Ability to mentor, develop, and support local technical talent.
   
   

Problem-Solving & Critical Thinking


 * Strong analytical and troubleshooting abilities, especially in high-density cooling environments.
 * Innovative mindset with the ability to identify efficiency improvements and enhance system reliability.
   
   

Great Place to Work

Enjoy…

A fast-paced, entrepreneurial culture focused on innovation.

A flexible, autonomous work environment.

A culture of respect, learning, and excellence.

Experienced, highly talented experts as team peers.

Growth and travel opportunities.

A team of change-makers having a significant impact on people and the planet.

About Our Parent Company, Endeavour Energy

Endeavour is the main hub—a self-sustaining incubation platform for innovation—within a larger ecosystem focused on sustainable infrastructure. It provides the space, time, and all necessary resources to take advanced technologies from ideation through development to launch. Every day we are helping innovators to develop and launch global solutions to tackle global challenges, which is no easy task. Our teams work extremely hard because we all believe in Endeavour’s mission: to guarantee clean water and renewable energy to everyone, everywhere. We are working hard to decentralize infrastructure and deploy innovative energy, water, and waste treatment solutions that are more sustainable and more profitable than those currently in existence. Our team of passionate change-makers want to leave the world better than the way we found it. Endeavour prides itself on hiring talented, highly motivated people because that’s what our mission requires. There is a strong sense of camaraderie between good-natured people who bring their A-game to work, every day, to make a difference. To work at Endeavour is to know that you are part of something special—something bigger than yourself.

Today, we’re deploying solutions that are immediately scalable and profitable, including EV charging, data center cooling, and waste to fuel systems. As we look to the future, Endeavour intends to remain at the forefront of innovation, taking on grander challenges based on the needs of our clients and of local economies. We hope that our Sustainable Habitat for Innovation towards a Purpose (SHIP) will be a model that inspires people and businesses to #jointhejourney so that together we can combine the best of what technology and the natural world can offer to ensure a regenerative/sustainable world for generations to come.

Position: Director of Engineering

Full-Time or Part-Time: Full-Time

Reports to: Operations Director, Europe

Location: Europe (Remote) Preferred in Spain. Travel required in Europe and the United States

Contact: recruiting@endeavourii.com

title: SENIOR DIRECTOR, PEOPLE & CULTURE INDUSTRIAL OPERATIONS
description:
Position Summary / Mission

Provide strategic and operational P&C support the stakeholders to drive delivery of the business objectives. Develop and deploy the P&C strategy and programs for the business enabling commercial excellence. Work in close partnership with local and global leaders, key client groups and Corporate P&C to support the organization and our employees’ current and future growth, ensuring best performance in order to achieve the business strategy.

Core Responsibilities

 * Support cultural change.

Preferred Skills & Competences

title: US Sales Director
description:
Build & Lead Fleet’s Largest Market

 * Location: Barcelona, Spain
 * Availability: ASAP
 * Languages: English & Spanish required
 * Relocation: Open to North or Central America within 12–18 months




Our Story

We are, above all, entrepreneurs — ambitious, pragmatic, and transparent.

Since launching in 2019, we made a bold choice: to bootstrap Fleet with a simple yet ambitious mission: Become the leading Device-as-a-Service provider in Europe.




Why We Exist

We make IT management — for startups and scale-ups — simple, fast, and frictionless.




Where we Come From

We are rooted in an ecosystem of founders, VCs, operators, and top-tier talent across Germany, France, Spain, and the UK. Our DNA is 100% entrepreneurial: ownership, high standards, and kindness.




Your Contribution: Own & Scale the U.S. Market

As U.S. Sales Director, you will play a strategic role in Fleet’s expansion in the United States.

You will work directly with Alex (VP Sales Europe), and the founders, fully owning the U.S. sales perimeter and preparing it for long-term scale.




The U.S. is set to become Fleet’s #1 market. You will be responsible for making that happen.




Your Mission

Lead & Elevate the U.S. Team

 * Manage a team composed of 1 SDR, 2–3 Account Executives, and 1 Account Manager
 * Coach junior profiles on mid-market deal execution
 * Improve pipeline visibility, forecasting accuracy, and CRM rigor
 * Participate in hiring, onboarding, and structuring the team




You are not a corporate manager — you are a builder who leads from the front.




Mid-Market Deal Mastery (Closing Support)

You will not carry a personal quota, but:

 * Join strategic mid-market deals (500–1,000 employees)
 * Support negotiations and unblock complex situations
 * Step in when enterprise-level accounts require senior involvement
 * Ensure retention and expansion of key U.S. accounts




Closing excellence is mandatory.




Protect & Expand Revenue

 * Ensure strong retention across key accounts
 * Support expansion strategies
 * Work closely with Customer Success and Operations to guarantee smooth onboarding
 * Strengthen long-term client relationships




The U.S. perimeter is too strategic to operate without senior ownership.




Cross-Functional & External Leadership

 * Work horizontally with Ops, Finance, and Product
 * Maintain relationships with financial institutions and key partners
 * Ensure scalability of processes for future geographic expansion




This is not only a sales leadership role — it’s business leadership.




Prepare Future Expansion

 * Structure the U.S. market independently from Europe
 * Replicate and scale Fleet’s European playbook
 * Be open to relocating to North or Central America
 * Build the foundation for a 20–30 person organization within 12–36 months




We are hiring for ceiling, not comfort.




Your Profile: Builder, Owner, High Ceiling

We are looking for someone with strong fundamentals and high growth potential.




You’re likely a great fit if:

 * You have 5–8+ years of B2B sales experience
 * You have closed mid-market deals (3–6 month cycles)
 * You’ve managed a small team (4–5 people minimum)
 * You have hiring and onboarding exposure
 * You are strong in deal management, negotiation, and expansion
 * You are structured: clean pipeline, accurate forecasting, CRM discipline
 * You are comfortable operating from Barcelona (1pm–9pm CET schedule)
 * You are proficient in English and Spanish
 * You are open to relocating if the company requires it




Who SHOULD Apply

This role is for you if:

 * You want to own a full strategic market
 * You are comfortable managing and still getting involved in deals
 * You thrive in fast-moving, entrepreneurial environments
 * You take ownership of both input and output
 * You are motivated by long-term leadership growth
 * You want to build something that can outgrow Europe




Your Chance

At Fleet, we build win-win environments. You bring ambition and discipline; we provide impact, autonomy, growth, and visibility.




What we offer

 * Full responsibility for Fleet’s most strategic market — rare, valuable, and highly formative.
 * A competitive package + variable compensation + benefits.
 * Access to a Tier 1 VC network: Sequoia, Accel, Index, Cocoa…
 * A team of 40 ambitious, demanding, and supportive talents.
 * A self-financed, profitable, fast-growing company.
 * Full autonomy, real ownership, and visible impact from day one.
 * Mentoring, introductions, and a genuine career accelerator.

title: Head of Finance
description:
¿Eres Head of Finance y buscas nuevos retos? ¡Sigue leyendo!




Buscamos CFO para unirse a un estudio de arquitectura y diseño de alto nivel que ofrece soluciones atemporales y personalizadas a través de un enfoque creativo refinado e innovador.




Operan bajo una estructura de Holding con distintas verticales y un fuerte componente de gestión de proyectos. Además, se encuentran en plena transición tecnológica: migrando de sistemas legados (A3) a un entorno de ERP avanzado + Data Lake + Inteligencia Artificial.




Tu misión será asegurar la salud financiera del hoy (rigor contable y tesorería) mientras construyes la infraestructura de datos del mañana. Buscamos un Head of Finance que actúe como arquitecto financiero del ecosistema.




Responsabilidades y Funciones

Garantizarás la integridad financiera de la organización. Tus responsabilidades se dividen en cuatro pilares estratégicos:

1. Planificación Financiera y Estrategia

- Visión a largo plazo

- Liderar la elaboración de presupuestos anuales por vertical y realizar previsiones (forecasts) trimestrales para anticipar necesidades de caja e inversión.

- Gestión de Inversiones (CAPEX)




2. Excelencia Contable y Reporting

- Rigor Técnico: Supervisar el ciclo contable completo.

- Consolidación: Gestionar la complejidad del Holding, asegurando una consolidación correcta de balances, cuentas de resultados y operaciones intercompany.

- Compliance & Governance: Garantizar el cumplimiento estricto de las normativas fiscales y contables, implementando controles internos que blinden a la empresa ante auditorías.

- Integridad del Dato: Asegurar que los estados financieros sean precisos no solo para el Registro Mercantil, sino para alimentar el Data Lake con información veraz y estructurada.




3. Transformación Tecnológica

- Liderazgo ERP: Dirigir la migración del software actual (A3) a un ERP de mercado (tipo SAP Business One, Navision o Dynamics). No solo implementarlo, sino diseñarlo bajo las mejores prácticas.

- Integración con IA: Colaborar con el equipo técnico para que el flujo de datos financieros se integre automáticamente en el ecosistema de Inteligencia Artificial.

- Automatización: Identificar procesos manuales repetitivos y aplicar tecnología para optimizar la eficiencia del departamento.




4. Socio de Operaciones y Análisis

- Nexo con Operaciones: Trabajar codo a codo con los Project Managers para traducir la ejecución de proyectos en métricas financieras (WIP, margen por proyecto, desviaciones).

- Financial Analysis: Ir más allá del dato histórico. Proporcionar insights sobre rentabilidad por vertical y eficiencia operativa para apoyar la toma de decisiones estratégicas.

- Risk Management: Identificar riesgos financieros (flujo de caja, clientes, costes) y proponer estrategias de mitigación proactivas.




Requisitos

Buscamos un perfil híbrido: la solidez técnica de un auditor con la agilidad de un controller de negocio.




- Formación y Base: Licenciatura en ADE, Economía o similar. Muy valorable paso previo por Big 4 o Auditoría

- Experiencia: Mínimo 5-7 años de experiencia, con un rol reciente como Responsable Financiero, Controller Senior o Finance Manager en empresa final. Muy valorable paso previo por empresas medianas similares

- Tech-Savvy (Imprescindible): Usuario avanzado de ERPs (SAP B1, Business Central, Netsuite). Debes sentirte cómodo hablando de integración de datos, BI y tecnología.

- Mentalidad Operativa: Experiencia en empresas de servicios o gestión de proyectos. Entiendes la diferencia entre facturación y producción, y sabes gestionar una tesorería dinámica.

- Soft Skills: Capacidad de liderazgo (gestión de equipo pequeño), comunicación clara con stakeholders y criterio estético en la presentación de informes.

- Inglés avanzado (hablado y escrito)




¿Qué pueden ofrecerte?

- Impacto Real: La oportunidad de diseñar un departamento financiero desde los cimientos y liderar una transformación digital puntera (Data Lake/IA).

- Posición Estratégica: Reporte directo a la propiedad y colaboración estrecha con todas las áreas de negocio.

- Un entorno de trabajo que valora la excelencia, el diseño y la calidad humana, lejos de las estructuras rígidas corporativas.




Condiciones:

- Contrato indefinido a jornada completa

- Horario 09:00h a 18:30h y viernes jornada intensiva hasta las 15:00h

- Salario competitivo acorde a tu expertise

title: Responsable de Operaciones (Fotovoltaica)
description:
Acerca del empleo

 * Rol de Responsable de Operaciones (Fotovoltaica), Valencia.
 * Desde We Hunt estamos colaborando con una empresa nacional referente en el desarrollo/EPC de proyecto de energías renovables.




Sobre nuestro cliente:

Empresa de ámbito nacional especializada en el desarrollo, promoción y construcción en proyectos de energía proveniente de fuentes renovables; el Responsable de Operaciones se responsabilizará de desarrollar / colaborar en la gestión de las operaciones de ejecución, compras y O&M de los servicios de EPC de autoconsumos y Utility Scale.




Educación/Formación

 * Titulación en Ingeniería Eléctrica, Ingeniería Industrial o similar.
 * Otras ingenierías afines con experiencia comprobada en energía solar FV.
 * Se valorará formación de posgrado (MBA o Máster en Energías Renovables, Sistemas Eléctricos de Potencia, etc.).
 * Cursos específicos en software de diseño fotovoltaico (PVsyst, AutoCAD, SketchUp)
 * Certificaciones en PRL, instalador eléctrico o gestor energético.




Experiencia (H/M/D)

 * Experiencia mínima de 5 años en operaciones EPC de la industria Fotovoltaica.
 * Experiencia en autoconsumo industrial y utility scale.
 * Dirección de equipos y selección de personal técnico.
 * Gestión integral de subcontratas.
 * Experiencia en compras técnicas.
 * Dominio de herramientas de planificación.
 * Dirección y gestión de equipos técnicos multidisciplinares
 * Conocimiento técnico sólido del mercado eléctrico, normativa aplicable y estándares del sector.
 * Inglés alto; italiano valorable.
 * Digitalización operativa (ERP, Power BI).




Funciones y tareas a desarrollar

A. Dirección operativa de proyectos

 * Planificación integral y supervisión de obras de autoconsumo industrial y utility scale.
 * Coordinación de ingeniería, compras, logística y construcción.
 * Asegurar plazos, costes, calidad y seguridad.
 * Validación de memorias de construcción, replanteos y commissioning.
 * Resolución de incidencias técnicas y operativas.

B. Gestión del equipo y crecimiento del área

 * Liderar y desarrollar al equipo de operaciones: PMs, jefes de obra, técnicos y personal de campo.
 * Responsable del proceso completo de contratación del personal operativo.
 * Responsable de la correcta integración de las nuevas incorporaciones.
 * Definición de roles, funciones y estándares de trabajo.

C. Gestión de subcontratas

 * Homologación, selección, negociación y coordinación de subcontratas.
 * Supervisión del cumplimiento de estándares de calidad, seguridad y rendimiento.
 * Control de certificaciones, mediciones y performance de contratistas.
 * Gestión de reclamaciones e incidencias.

D. Gestión del área de compras

 * Integrar y dirigir el proceso de compras técnicas.
 * Asegurar disponibilidad de materiales según planificación.
 * Negociación de precios, condiciones y acuerdos marco.
 * Control de stocks y logística de obra.

E. Mejora continua y procesos

 * Estandarización de procesos de ejecución, seguridad, control económico y commissioning.
 * Implantación de herramientas digitales.
 * Optimización continua del coste, tiempo y calidad.

F. Expansión internacional

 * Adaptación de procesos operativos a normativas en proyectos internacionales.
 * Gestión de proveedores y subcontratas locales.
 * Soporte al establecimiento operativo en nuevos mercados




Competencias:

 * Liderazgo y gestión de personas.
 * Negociación con proveedores y subcontratas.
 * Organización y capacidad analítica.
 * Orientación a resultados.




Qué ofrecemos:

 * Formar parte de una empresa con fuerte posicionamiento en el mercado nacional e internacional.
 * Proyecto profesional a largo plazo. Estabilidad profesional.
 * Dinamismo, participación en diferentes proyectos de la compañía.
 * Asumir un puesto de responsabilidad.




Siguientes pasos:

Si estás interesado en esta oferta, haz clic en "aplicar ahora" para enviar una copia actualizada de tu CV; garantizamos la confidencialidad.

Si eres un profesional con el compromiso de transformar las Administraciones Públicas para hacerlas más eficientes, modernas y transparentes, ¡te estamos esperando!




En nuestra unidad de Sector Público, trabajamos día a día para transformar la gestión pública, ayudando a las administraciones a mejorar su eficiencia operativa, optimizar el uso de los recursos y garantizar el cumplimiento de la normativa. Estamos buscando a un/a profesional senior con un enfoque estratégico para liderar proyectos que mejoren los procesos de gestión de recursos humanos en Administraciones Públicas.




¿Cómo puedes contribuir en nuestros equipos?

 * Planificando, organizando y llevando el control de cronogramas y recursos de los proyectos asignados, siendo el punto de contacto con el cliente, y garantizando la calidad de los entregables.

 * Enfoque en asegurar el cumplimiento normativo y la eficacia en la gestión pública: analizando la normativa vigente y proponiendo soluciones que permitan una mejora en la gestión de los recursos públicos.
 * Teniendo en el horizonte la transformación digital del sector público: Identificar oportunidades para incorporar soluciones tecnológicas que faciliten la gestión de recursos humanos dentro de las administraciones.
 * Todo lo anterior bajo el prisma del acompañamiento estratégico y colaborativo en la toma de decisiones a nivel ejecutivo, ofreciendo propuestas que impulsen la eficiencia y sostenibilidad de los servicios públicos.




Requisitos

#Together, Grow as One #One heart, one NTT DATA. Future at Heart
